```text
345	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
346	# Then steps — role-complete restructure: identity / posture / CLI ordering
347	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
348	#
349	# Scenarios lead-kq0 (f1ac9534c1d58318, e522f7393dfcd1c1, a481db51463526d2,
350	# a6b3e510821aba52) pin the structural ordering of the lead-po and
351	# lead-architect templates: role identity (depth-1 #) and default-posture
352	# header (depth-2 ##) must lead the document, and procedural shop-msg CLI
353	# content must come after both the posture header AND after every §3.2
354	# activity has been named at least once. The §3.2 activity lists for each
355	# role are hard-coded below because the scenarios reference them by index
356	# ("scenario 10" / "scenario 14") within the dispatched feature file.
357	
358	_LEAD_PO_ACTIVITY_NAMES_FROM_SCENARIO_10 = (
359	    "Interview stakeholder",
360	    "Maintain product brief",
361	    "Write PDR for new functionality",
362	    "Write Gherkin scenarios",
363	    "Respond to BC `clarify`",
364	)
365	
366	_LEAD_ARCHITECT_ACTIVITY_NAMES_FROM_SCENARIO_14 = (
367	    "Write ADRs",
368	    "Maintain structurizr workspace",
369	    "Collaborate with PO on BC decomposition",
370	    "Assign scenarios to BCs",
371	    "Reconcile scenario registers",
372	    # The naming scenario phrases this one as an alternation:
373	    # either "Send `request_bugfix`" appears OR both message-type names
374	    # appear. Treat the canonical token as `request_bugfix` for ordering
375	    # purposes — that is the substring all renderings share.
376	    "request_bugfix",
377	    "Read a BC-shop's card via `request_shop_card`",
378	    "Respond to BC `clarify`",
379	)
380	
381	
382	def _first_index(content: str, needle: str) -> int:
383	    idx = content.find(needle)
384	    assert idx >= 0, f"expected substring {needle!r} to appear in content"
385	    return idx
386	
387	
388	@then(parsers.parse('a "{header}" identity header appears in the output'))
389	def then_identity_header_appears(header: str, context: dict) -> None:
390	    # The identity header is the depth-1 (#) heading that opens the role
391	    # prompt — the bare-presence check; ordering is asserted by separate
392	    # byte-offset steps below.
393	    content = context["template_content"]
394	    assert header in content, (
395	        f"template {context.get('shown_template_name')!r} is missing identity "
396	        f"header {header!r}"
397	    )
398	    # Stash the offset for the byte-offset Then steps that follow.
399	    context["identity_header_offset"] = content.index(header)
400	
401	
402	@then(parsers.parse('a "{header}" posture header appears in the output'))
403	def then_posture_header_appears(header: str, context: dict) -> None:
404	    content = context["template_content"]
405	    assert header in content, (
406	        f"template {context.get('shown_template_name')!r} is missing posture "
407	        f"header {header!r}"
408	    )
409	    context["posture_header_offset"] = content.index(header)
410	    context["posture_header_text"] = header
411	
412	
413	@then(
414	    parsers.parse(
415	        'the byte offset of the identity header is less than the byte offset '
416	        'of the first occurrence of the string "{needle}"'
417	    )
418	)
419	def then_identity_before_needle(needle: str, context: dict) -> None:
420	    content = context["template_content"]
421	    identity_offset = context["identity_header_offset"]
422	    needle_offset = _first_index(content, needle)
423	    assert identity_offset < needle_offset, (
424	        f"identity header at byte {identity_offset} must precede first "
425	        f"{needle!r} at byte {needle_offset}; template "
426	        f"{context.get('shown_template_name')!r}"
427	    )
428	
429	
430	@then(
431	    parsers.parse(
432	        'the byte offset of the posture header is less than the byte offset '
433	        'of the first occurrence of the string "{needle}"'
434	    )
435	)
436	def then_posture_before_needle(needle: str, context: dict) -> None:
437	    content = context["template_content"]
438	    posture_offset = context["posture_header_offset"]
439	    needle_offset = _first_index(content, needle)
440	    assert posture_offset < needle_offset, (
441	        f"posture header at byte {posture_offset} must precede first "
442	        f"{needle!r} at byte {needle_offset}; template "
443	        f"{context.get('shown_template_name')!r}"
444	    )
445	
446	
447	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
448	# Then steps — role-complete restructure: §3.2 activity coverage
449	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
450	#
451	# Scenarios dddd6c3b2eed7e45 (PO) and 0aea22a97e63d4f8 (Architect) pin
452	# that each §3.2 activity must be NAMED in the template. The qualifier
453	# variant additionally asserts that a qualifier word appears in proximity
454	# to the activity name. The alternation variant accepts either the
455	# "Send `request_bugfix`" phrasing or both bare message-type names.
456	
457	
458	@then(
459	    parsers.re(
460	        r'^the content names the activity "(?P<activity>[^"]+)"$'
461	    )
462	)
463	def then_content_names_activity(activity: str, context: dict) -> None:
464	    content = context["template_content"]
465	    assert activity in content, (
466	        f"template {context.get('shown_template_name')!r} does not name §3.2 "
467	        f"activity {activity!r}"
468	    )
469	
470	
471	@then(
472	    parsers.re(
473	        r'^the content names the activity "(?P<activity>[^"]+)" with the '
474	        r'qualifier "(?P<qualifier>[^"]+)"$'
475	    )
476	)
477	def then_content_names_activity_with_qualifier(
478	    activity: str, qualifier: str, context: dict
479	) -> None:
480	    content = context["template_content"]
481	    assert activity in content, (
482	        f"template {context.get('shown_template_name')!r} does not name §3.2 "
483	        f"activity {activity!r}"
484	    )
485	    # The qualifier must appear in the same paragraph / contiguous block as
486	    # the activity name. We approximate "same block" as: somewhere within
487	    # 200 bytes after the activity name OR on the same line. That's broad
488	    # enough to allow a parenthetical, a sentence-following clause, or the
489	    # next sentence, but tight enough that the qualifier is contextually
490	    # bound to the activity rather than an unrelated mention elsewhere.
491	    idx = content.index(activity)
492	    window = content[idx : idx + 200 + len(activity)]
493	    assert qualifier in window, (
494	        f"qualifier {qualifier!r} must appear near activity {activity!r} in "
495	        f"template {context.get('shown_template_name')!r}; window after "
496	        f"activity:\n{window!r}"
497	    )
498	
499	
500	@then(
501	    parsers.re(
502	        r'^the content names the activity "(?P<activity>[^"]+)" with the '
503	        r'qualifier "(?P<q1>[^"]+)" or "(?P<q2>[^"]+)"$'
504	    )
505	)
506	def then_content_names_activity_with_either_qualifier(
507	    activity: str, q1: str, q2: str, context: dict
508	) -> None:
509	    content = context["template_content"]
510	    assert activity in content, (
511	        f"template {context.get('shown_template_name')!r} does not name §3.2 "
512	        f"activity {activity!r}"
513	    )
514	    idx = content.index(activity)
515	    window = content[idx : idx + 200 + len(activity)]
516	    assert q1 in window or q2 in window, (
517	        f"at least one of qualifiers {q1!r} / {q2!r} must appear near "
518	        f"activity {activity!r} in template "
519	        f"{context.get('shown_template_name')!r}; window after activity:\n"
520	        f"{window!r}"
521	    )
522	
523	
524	@then(
525	    parsers.re(
526	        r'^the content names the activity "(?P<activity>[^"]+)" or '
527	        r'equivalently mentions both "(?P<token_a>[^"]+)" and '
528	        r'"(?P<token_b>[^"]+)" as dispatch vehicles$'
529	    )
530	)
531	def then_content_names_activity_or_equivalent_pair(
532	    activity: str, token_a: str, token_b: str, context: dict
533	) -> None:
534	    content = context["template_content"]
535	    # Either: the explicit "Send `request_bugfix`" phrasing appears,
536	    # OR both message-type names appear somewhere in the content. The
537	    # latter satisfies the §3.2 activity entry "Send `request_bugfix` /
538	    # `request_maintenance`" without forcing the template to repeat the
539	    # "Send " prefix.
540	    if activity in content:
541	        return
542	    assert token_a in content and token_b in content, (
543	        f"template {context.get('shown_template_name')!r} must either name "
544	        f"activity {activity!r} OR mention both {token_a!r} and {token_b!r} "
545	        f"as dispatch vehicles; neither condition held"
546	    )
547	
548	
549	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
550	# Given + Then steps — §3.2 activity guidance coverage (scenarios
551	# 9a9421ad59ee5d67 and 5ccb3fb1122f9341)
552	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
553	#
554	# The Given step stashes the activity list (literal phrasing as it
555	# appears in the dispatched scenario) on context. The Then step iterates
556	# that list and, for each activity, verifies either:
557	#   (a) the activity name appears on a line that also contains a colon
558	#       followed by non-whitespace prose (one-line guidance), OR
559	#   (b) the activity name appears as a subsection heading whose body
560	#       carries at least one sentence of prose, OR
561	#   (c) within a 240-byte window after the activity name the literal
562	#       phrase "guidance pending" appears (case-insensitive).
563	# The "no bare list item" assertion checks that no line of the form
564	# "- <activity>" or "* <activity>" exists without trailing colon/prose.
565	
566	
567	def _activity_block_satisfies_guidance(content: str, activity: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:
568	    """Return (ok, reason) for the activity-guidance check.
569	
570	    The check accepts any of three shapes:
571	      1. "guidance pending" within a 240-byte window after the activity
572	         name (case-insensitive).
573	      2. A line that names the activity AND contains a colon followed by
574	         non-whitespace prose on the same line — one-line guidance.
575	      3. A subsection heading (## or ### or ####) whose text names the
576	         activity, with at least one non-blank, non-heading line after
577	         it in the same subsection — multi-line guidance.
578	    """
579	    lower_content = content.lower()
580	    lower_activity = activity.lower()
581	
582	    # Shape 1: explicit "guidance pending" marker near the activity.
583	    idx = lower_content.find(lower_activity)
584	    while idx != -1:
585	        window = lower_content[idx : idx + 240 + len(lower_activity)]
586	        if "guidance pending" in window:
587	            return True, "guidance-pending marker present"
588	        idx = lower_content.find(lower_activity, idx + 1)
589	
590	    # Shapes 2 and 3: walk line by line.
591	    lines = content.splitlines()
592	    for i, line in enumerate(lines):
593	        if activity not in line:
594	            continue
595	        stripped = line.lstrip()
596	        # Shape 3: heading naming the activity.
597	        if stripped.startswith("#"):
598	            # Look for at least one non-blank, non-heading line after.
599	            for j in range(i + 1, len(lines)):
600	                follow = lines[j].strip()
601	                if follow.startswith("#"):
602	                    break  # next heading reached; no body in this subsection
603	                if follow:
604	                    return True, "subsection-with-body"
605	            # Heading with no body — fall through to bare-list check.
606	            continue
607	        # Shape 2: one-line guidance. Activity name followed by a colon
608	        # and then non-whitespace on the same line.
609	        after = line.split(activity, 1)[1]
610	        if ":" in after:
611	            tail = after.split(":", 1)[1]
612	            if tail.strip():
613	                return True, "one-line guidance"
614	        # Bullet line with just the activity name and nothing else after.
615	        # Continue scanning — another occurrence might satisfy.
616	    return False, "no satisfying block found"
617	
618	
619	@given(
620	    parsers.re(
621	        r'^the §3\.2 PO activities (?P<activity_list>".+")$'
622	    )
623	)
624	def given_po_activity_list(activity_list: str, context: dict) -> None:
625	    # `activity_list` is the literal comma- and "and"-separated tail
626	    # after "the §3.2 PO activities ". Extract each quoted activity name
627	    # in order; ", and " is just punctuation between items.
628	    import re
629	
630	    items = re.findall(r'"([^"]+)"', activity_list)
631	    assert items, (
632	        f"Given step parsed no activity names out of {activity_list!r}"
633	    )
634	    context["activity_list"] = items
635	
636	
637	@given(
638	    parsers.re(
639	        r'^the §3\.2 Architect activities (?P<activity_list>".+")$'
640	    )
641	)
642	def given_architect_activity_list(activity_list: str, context: dict) -> None:
643	    import re
644	
645	    items = re.findall(r'"([^"]+)"', activity_list)
646	    assert items, (
647	        f"Given step parsed no activity names out of {activity_list!r}"
648	    )
649	    context["activity_list"] = items
650	
651	
652	@then(
653	    'for each activity in that list, the content has a contiguous block — '
654	    'either a subsection that names the activity or a line that names the '
655	    'activity — that contains at minimum one sentence of guidance OR an '
656	    'explicit marker of the form "guidance pending" (case-insensitive)'
657	)
658	def then_each_activity_has_guidance(context: dict) -> None:
659	    content = context["template_content"]
660	    activities = context["activity_list"]
661	    failures: list[str] = []
662	    for activity in activities:
663	        ok, reason = _activity_block_satisfies_guidance(content, activity)
664	        if not ok:
665	            failures.append(f"  - {activity!r}: {reason}")
666	    assert not failures, (
667	        f"template {context.get('shown_template_name')!r} has activities "
668	        f"without guidance or a 'guidance pending' marker:\n"
669	        + "\n".join(failures)
670	    )
671	
672	
673	@then(
674	    parsers.re(
675	        r'^no §3\.2 (PO|Architect) activity appears as a bare list item with '
676	        r'neither guidance nor a "guidance pending" marker$'
677	    )
678	)
679	def then_no_bare_list_item(context: dict) -> None:
680	    content = context["template_content"]
681	    activities = context["activity_list"]
682	    lower = content.lower()
683	    failures: list[str] = []
684	    for activity in activities:
685	        # A bare list item is a line that starts with a bullet marker
686	        # (- or *) followed by only the activity name and optional
687	        # whitespace, with no trailing prose on the same line AND no
688	        # "guidance pending" marker in the immediate vicinity.
689	        for line in content.splitlines():
690	            stripped = line.strip()
691	            if not (stripped.startswith("- ") or stripped.startswith("* ")):
692	                continue
693	            payload = stripped[2:].strip()
694	            if payload != activity:
695	                continue
696	            # Bare list item suspected. Check for nearby guidance-pending.
697	            idx = lower.find(activity.lower())
698	            window = lower[idx : idx + 240 + len(activity)]
699	            if "guidance pending" not in window:
700	                failures.append(
701	                    f"  - {activity!r}: bare list item without guidance "
702	                    f"or 'guidance pending' marker"
703	                )
704	    assert not failures, (
705	        f"template {context.get('shown_template_name')!r} has bare §3.2 "
706	        f"activity list items:\n" + "\n".join(failures)
707	    )
708	
709	
710	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
711	# Then steps — role-complete restructure: CLI subordination ordering
712	# (scenarios e522f7393dfcd1c1 and a6b3e510821aba52)
713	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
714	
715	
716	@then(
717	    'every heading whose text mentions "shop-msg" appears at heading depth '
718	    'three (###) or deeper, never at depth two (##) or depth one (#)'
719	)
720	def then_shop_msg_headings_depth_three_or_deeper(context: dict) -> None:
721	    content = context["template_content"]
722	    offenders: list[tuple[int, str]] = []
723	    for lineno, line in enumerate(content.splitlines(), start=1):
724	        stripped = line.lstrip()
725	        if not stripped.startswith("#"):
726	            continue
727	        if "shop-msg" not in stripped:
728	            continue
729	        # Count the leading # characters to determine depth.
730	        depth = 0
731	        for ch in stripped:
732	            if ch == "#":
733	                depth += 1
734	            else:
735	                break
736	        if depth < 3:
737	            offenders.append((lineno, line.rstrip()))
738	    assert not offenders, (
739	        f"template {context.get('shown_template_name')!r} has shop-msg "
740	        f"headings at depth < 3:\n"
741	        + "\n".join(f"  line {n}: {l!r}" for n, l in offenders)
742	    )
743	
744	
745	@then(
746	    parsers.parse(
747	        'the first occurrence of the substring "shop-msg" in the content '
748	        'appears after the "{header}" header'
749	    )
750	)
751	def then_first_shop_msg_after_header(header: str, context: dict) -> None:
752	    content = context["template_content"]
753	    header_idx = content.find(header)
754	    assert header_idx >= 0, (
755	        f"premise of Then violated: template "
756	        f"{context.get('shown_template_name')!r} is missing header {header!r}"
757	    )
758	    shop_msg_idx = content.find("shop-msg")
759	    assert shop_msg_idx >= 0, (
760	        f"premise of Then violated: template "
761	        f"{context.get('shown_template_name')!r} has no 'shop-msg' substring"
762	    )
763	    assert header_idx < shop_msg_idx, (
764	        f"header {header!r} at byte {header_idx} must precede first "
765	        f"'shop-msg' at byte {shop_msg_idx} in template "
766	        f"{context.get('shown_template_name')!r}"
767	    )
768	
769	
770	@then(
771	    parsers.re(
772	        r'^the first occurrence of the substring "shop-msg" in the content '
773	        r'appears after every §3\.2 (?P<role>PO|Architect) activity name '
774	        r'from scenario (?P<scen_idx>\d+) has appeared at least once$'
775	    )
776	)
777	def then_first_shop_msg_after_all_activities(
778	    role: str, scen_idx: str, context: dict
779	) -> None:
780	    content = context["template_content"]
781	    if role == "PO":
782	        activities = _LEAD_PO_ACTIVITY_NAMES_FROM_SCENARIO_10
783	    elif role == "Architect":
784	        activities = _LEAD_ARCHITECT_ACTIVITY_NAMES_FROM_SCENARIO_14
785	    else:
786	        raise AssertionError(f"unrecognized role {role!r}")
787	    shop_msg_idx = content.find("shop-msg")
788	    assert shop_msg_idx >= 0, (
789	        f"premise of Then violated: template "
790	        f"{context.get('shown_template_name')!r} has no 'shop-msg' substring"
791	    )
792	    missing: list[str] = []
793	    too_late: list[tuple[str, int, int]] = []
794	    for activity in activities:
795	        idx = content.find(activity)
796	        if idx < 0:
797	            missing.append(activity)
798	            continue
799	        if idx >= shop_msg_idx:
800	            too_late.append((activity, idx, shop_msg_idx))
801	    assert not missing, (
802	        f"premise of Then violated: template "
803	        f"{context.get('shown_template_name')!r} is missing §3.2 {role} "
804	        f"activity name(s): {missing!r}"
805	    )
806	    assert not too_late, (
807	        f"first 'shop-msg' at byte {shop_msg_idx} in template "
808	        f"{context.get('shown_template_name')!r} precedes §3.2 {role} "
809	        f"activity name(s) (each must appear at least once before):\n"
810	        + "\n".join(
811	            f"  - {a!r} first appears at byte {ai} (>= {smi})"
812	            for a, ai, smi in too_late
813	        )
814	    )
815	
816	
817	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
818	# Step definitions — inter-shop messaging encapsulation (lead-bwu)
819	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
820	#
821	# Scenarios 17-25 of features/inter_shop_messaging_encapsulation.feature
822	# pin two invariants across the four canonical templates and the two
823	# CLAUDE.md files of the shopsystem product:
824	#
825	#   1. CLI-only mailbox access: every prescribed inbox/outbox operation
826	#      names a `shop-msg` subcommand. No filesystem-direct inspection,
827	#      no filename-convention reasoning, no set-difference-on-filenames
828	#      characterization of "unprocessed".
829	#
830	#   2. bd-decoupling: bd participation is the local work-tracker
831	#      concern of each shop; no template or CLAUDE.md makes a bd
832	#      command a precondition for a shop-msg invocation, and no
833	#      template names `--bd-ref` (the flag has been replaced with the
834	#      optional, tracker-neutral `--provenance-ref`).
835	#
836	# Scenarios 21, 22, 25 reach OUTSIDE the BC root to read the lead-shop
837	# CLAUDE.md at <product>/CLAUDE.md and the BC's own CLAUDE.md at
838	# <product>/repos/shopsystem-templates/CLAUDE.md. The lead's dispatch
839	# (lead-bwu) explicitly names both files as in-scope surfaces — this
840	# read is sanctioned by the message.
841	
842	
843	def _product_root() -> Path:
844	    """Return the lead-shop (product) root directory.
845	
846	    The BC root for shopsystem-templates is two levels below the product
847	    root: <product>/repos/shopsystem-templates. Resolve by walking up
848	    from this file (tests/conftest.py inside the BC) two more levels.
849	    """
850	    return Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent.parent.parent
851	
852	
853	def _read_file_at_product_path(relative_path: str) -> str:
854	    """Read a file path that is given relative to the product root.
855	
856	    The scenarios spell paths as "CLAUDE.md" (lead shop) and
857	    "repos/shopsystem-templates/CLAUDE.md" (BC). Both anchor at the
858	    product root.
859	    """
860	    target = _product_root() / relative_path
861	    return target.read_text()
862	
863	
864	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
865	# Given steps — file-at-path premise (scenarios 21, 22, 25)
866	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
867	
868	
869	@given(
870	    parsers.parse(
871	        'the file at "{relative_path}" exists and is the canonical CLAUDE.md '
872	        'for the shopsystem-templates BC shop'
873	    )
874	)
875	def given_file_at_path_is_bc_claude_md(relative_path: str, context: dict) -> None:
876	    target = _product_root() / relative_path
877	    assert target.exists(), (
878	        f"premise of Given violated: file {target!s} does not exist"
879	    )
880	    context["file_path"] = str(target)
881	    context["file_relative_path"] = relative_path
882	
883	
884	@given(
885	    parsers.parse(
886	        'the file at "{relative_path}" exists at the lead shop\'s repository '
887	        'root and describes the lead shop\'s router behavior and '
888	        'feature-request handling'
889	    )
890	)
891	def given_file_at_path_is_lead_claude_md(relative_path: str, context: dict) -> None:
892	    target = _product_root() / relative_path
893	    assert target.exists(), (
894	        f"premise of Given violated: file {target!s} does not exist"
895	    )
896	    context["file_path"] = str(target)
897	    context["file_relative_path"] = relative_path
898	
899	
900	@given(
901	    parsers.parse(
902	        'the file at {relative_path} exists and is the canonical CLAUDE.md '
903	        'for the named shop'
904	    )
905	)
906	def given_file_at_path_is_named_shop_claude_md(
907	    relative_path: str, context: dict
908	) -> None:
909	    # Scenario Outline (scenario 25) substitutes the path from the
910	    # Examples table. The path arrives without surrounding quotes from
911	    # pytest-bdd's outline substitution, so strip optional whitespace.
912	    relative_path = relative_path.strip()
913	    target = _product_root() / relative_path
914	    assert target.exists(), (
915	        f"premise of Given violated: file {target!s} does not exist"
916	    )
917	    context["file_path"] = str(target)
918	    context["file_relative_path"] = relative_path
919	
920	
921	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
922	# When step — read the file the Given just established
923	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
924	
925	
926	@when("I read that file")
927	def when_read_that_file(context: dict) -> None:
928	    path = context["file_path"]
929	    context["file_content"] = Path(path).read_text()
930	
931	
932	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
933	# Then steps — generic substring contains / does-not-contain
934	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
935	#
936	# "the content" assertions read from context["template_content"] (set
937	# by the existing "I read the X template via shop-templates show X"
938	# step). "the file" assertions read from context["file_content"] (set
939	# by the "I read that file" step above).
940	
941	
942	@then(parsers.parse('the content contains the literal substring "{needle}"'))
943	def then_content_contains_literal_substring(needle: str, context: dict) -> None:
944	    content = context["template_content"]
945	    assert needle in content, (
946	        f"template {context.get('shown_template_name')!r} is missing "
947	        f"required literal substring {needle!r}"
948	    )
949	
950	
951	@then(parsers.parse('the content does not contain the literal substring "{needle}"'))
952	def then_content_does_not_contain_literal_substring(
953	    needle: str, context: dict
954	) -> None:
955	    content = context["template_content"]
956	    assert needle not in content, (
957	        f"template {context.get('shown_template_name')!r} contains "
958	        f"forbidden literal substring {needle!r}"
959	    )
960	
961	
962	@then(parsers.parse('the file does not contain the literal substring "{needle}"'))
963	def then_file_does_not_contain_literal_substring(
964	    needle: str, context: dict
965	) -> None:
966	    content = context["file_content"]
967	    assert needle not in content, (
968	        f"file {context.get('file_relative_path')!r} contains forbidden "
969	        f"literal substring {needle!r}"
970	    )
971	
972	
973	@then(
974	    parsers.parse(
975	        'the file contains the literal substring "{needle}" as the named '
976	        'operation for identifying unprocessed work'
977	    )
978	)
979	def then_file_contains_substring_as_unprocessed_op(
980	    needle: str, context: dict
981	) -> None:
982	    content = context["file_content"]
983	    assert needle in content, (
984	        f"file {context.get('file_relative_path')!r} is missing required "
985	        f"literal substring {needle!r} (named operation for identifying "
986	        f"unprocessed work)"
987	    )
988	
989	
990	@then(
991	    parsers.parse(
992	        'the file contains the literal substring "{needle}" as the named '
993	        'operation for reading a specific inbox message'
994	    )
995	)
996	def then_file_contains_substring_as_read_inbox_op(
997	    needle: str, context: dict
998	) -> None:
999	    content = context["file_content"]
1000	    assert needle in content, (
1001	        f"file {context.get('file_relative_path')!r} is missing required "
1002	        f"literal substring {needle!r} (named operation for reading a "
1003	        f"specific inbox message)"
1004	    )
1005	
1006	
1007	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
1008	# Then steps — instructional negations (no "read the file directly"-style
1009	# guidance with respect to inbox/outbox)
1010	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
1011	#
1012	# These steps assert that the template/file does not instruct the
1013	# reader to perform a non-shop-msg filesystem operation on the
1014	# inbox/outbox. The check is conservative: forbid the literal
1015	# substrings that would betray such instruction (the same shapes the
1016	# scenario text enumerates), and additionally forbid common bypass
1017	# phrasings ("cat <BC root>/inbox", "open inbox/", etc.) when they
1018	# co-occur with the mailbox words.
1019	
1020	
1021	_INBOX_OUTBOX_TOKENS = ("inbox", "outbox")
1022	
1023	
1024	def _content_for_target(context: dict, target_label: str) -> tuple[str, str]:
1025	    """Return (content, identifier) for assertion error messages.
1026	
1027	    `target_label` is 'content' (template) or 'file' (CLAUDE.md).
1028	    """
1029	    if target_label == "content":
1030	        return (
1031	            context["template_content"],
1032	            f"template {context.get('shown_template_name')!r}",
1033	        )
1034	    return (
1035	        context["file_content"],
1036	        f"file {context.get('file_relative_path')!r}",
1037	    )
1038	
1039	
1040	def _assert_no_direct_inbox_outbox_instruction(
1041	    content: str, identifier: str
1042	) -> None:
1043	    """Common check for 'does not instruct the reader to ... inbox/outbox'.
1044	
1045	    The scenarios call out the same shapes from different angles. The
1046	    check below covers the union conservatively:
1047	
1048	    - Literal phrases like "read the file directly", "read the YAML
1049	      directly", "open the file" combined with inbox/outbox proximity.
1050	    - Direct cat/ls/grep/open verbs with inbox/outbox as direct object.
1051	
1052	    A line is an offense only if it gives an action on a mailbox path
1053	    without naming a shop-msg subcommand on the same line. That keeps
1054	    sentences like "shop-msg read inbox reads the file" from
1055	    incidentally triggering: the shop-msg subcommand name is present.
1056	    """
1057	    offenders: list[tuple[int, str]] = []
1058	    for lineno, line in enumerate(content.splitlines(), start=1):
1059	        lower = line.lower()
1060	        # Quick reject: no inbox/outbox mention → no offense possible.
1061	        if not any(tok in lower for tok in _INBOX_OUTBOX_TOKENS):
1062	            continue
1063	        # If the line already names a shop-msg subcommand, the inbox/
1064	        # outbox reference is within a sanctioned context. Skip.
1065	        if "shop-msg" in lower:
1066	            continue
1067	        # Offending phrasings: direct filesystem verbs / "read directly"
1068	        # variants targeting the mailbox.
1069	        triggers = (
1070	            "read the file directly",
1071	            "read the yaml directly",
1072	            "read the file",
1073	            "read the yaml",
1074	            "open the file",
1075	            "open the yaml",
1076	            "cat inbox",
1077	            "cat outbox",
1078	            "cat <bc root>/inbox",
1079	            "cat <bc root>/outbox",
1080	            "ls inbox",
1081	            "ls outbox",
1082	            "grep inbox",
1083	            "grep outbox",
1084	            "inspect the file",
1085	            "inspect the yaml",
1086	        )
1087	        if any(t in lower for t in triggers):
1088	            offenders.append((lineno, line.rstrip()))
1089	            continue
1090	        # Path-shape offenses: a sentence that names an inbox/outbox
1091	        # path token (`<BC root>/inbox/`, `inbox/`, `outbox/`) as the
1092	        # target of an inspection verb without a shop-msg cite. Look
1093	        # for action verbs in the same line.
1094	        action_verbs = (
1095	            "open ", "cat ", "ls ", "grep ", "inspect ", "view ",
1096	            "edit ", "tail ", "head ",
1097	        )
1098	        path_tokens = (
1099	            "<bc root>/inbox", "<bc root>/outbox",
1100	            "repos/<bc>/inbox", "repos/<bc>/outbox",
1101	        )
1102	        if any(v in lower for v in action_verbs) and any(
1103	            p in lower for p in path_tokens
1104	        ):
1105	            offenders.append((lineno, line.rstrip()))
1106	    assert not offenders, (
1107	        f"{identifier} contains instruction(s) to operate on inbox/outbox "
1108	        f"directly (without shop-msg) — forbidden:\n"
1109	        + "\n".join(f"  line {n}: {l!r}" for n, l in offenders)
1110	    )
1111	
1112	
1113	@then(
1114	    'the content does not instruct the reader to "read the file directly" '
1115	    'or "read the YAML directly" with respect to the inbox or outbox'
1116	)
1117	def then_content_no_read_directly_instruction(context: dict) -> None:
1118	    content, ident = _content_for_target(context, "content")
1119	    _assert_no_direct_inbox_outbox_instruction(content, ident)
1120	
1121	
1122	@then(
1123	    "the content does not instruct the reader to read the inbox YAML file "
1124	    "directly or by any non-shop-msg means"
1125	)
1126	def then_content_no_read_inbox_yaml_non_shop_msg(context: dict) -> None:
1127	    content, ident = _content_for_target(context, "content")
1128	    _assert_no_direct_inbox_outbox_instruction(content, ident)
1129	
1130	
1131	@then(
1132	    'the content does not instruct the PO to open, cat, or otherwise '
1133	    'inspect any path under "inbox/" or "outbox/" by any non-shop-msg means'
1134	)
1135	def then_content_no_po_inspect_inbox_outbox(context: dict) -> None:
1136	    content, ident = _content_for_target(context, "content")
1137	    _assert_no_direct_inbox_outbox_instruction(content, ident)
1138

1169	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
1170	# Then steps — set-difference-on-filenames negation (scenario 21)
1171	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
1172	
1173	
1174	@then(
1175	    'the file does not contain any sentence asserting that "a message is '
1176	    'unprocessed when there is no outbox file" or any other '
1177	    'set-difference-on-filenames characterization of unprocessed state'
1178	)
1179	def then_file_no_set_difference_unprocessed(context: dict) -> None:
1180	    content = context["file_content"]
1181	    lower = content.lower()
1182	    # The exact forbidden assertion (case-insensitive, allowing for
1183	    # minor punctuation around it).
1184	    forbidden_phrases = (
1185	        "a message is unprocessed when there is no outbox file",
1186	        "a message is considered unprocessed when there is no outbox file",
1187	        "message is unprocessed when there is no outbox",
1188	        "unprocessed when there is no outbox",
1189	        "no matching outbox file for its",  # set-difference shape
1190	        "no outbox file for its",            # set-difference shape
1191	        "no outbox file matching",
1192	        "no matching outbox",
1193	    )
1194	    offenders = [p for p in forbidden_phrases if p in lower]
1195	    assert not offenders, (
1196	        f"file {context.get('file_relative_path')!r} contains set-difference-"
1197	        f"on-filenames characterization(s) of 'unprocessed' state: "
1198	        f"{offenders!r}"
1199	    )
1200	
1201	
1202	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
1203	# Then steps — dispatch / inspection naming (scenario 22)
1204	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
1205	
1206	
1207	@then(
1208	    'every step or sentence that describes dispatching work to a BC names '
1209	    '"shop-msg send" as the dispatch operation'
1210	)
1211	def then_file_every_dispatch_sentence_names_shop_msg_send(
1212	    context: dict,
1213	) -> None:
1214	    content = context["file_content"]
1215	    # The scenario targets sentences that describe the *operation* of
1216	    # dispatching work to a BC (the inter-shop dispatch operation), not
1217	    # sentences that merely use "dispatched" as a past participle in a
1218	    # noun-phrase label, and not sentences about dispatching subagents
1219	    # within the lead shop. Discriminator: the line is procedural shape
1220	    # AND its subject is the Architect/lead acting toward a BC.
1221	    #
1222	    # Concrete signals of procedural shape: numbered list item
1223	    # (re. r"^\d+\."), imperative verb form ("dispatch the", "send"),
1224	    # OR a co-occurrence of "dispatch" with a message-type token
1225	    # (`assign_scenarios`/`request_bugfix`/`request_maintenance`).
1226	    #
1227	    # Concrete signals of "to a BC, not to a subagent": the line does
1228	    # NOT contain "subagent" / "lead-po" / "lead-architect" /
1229	    # "bc-implementer" / "bc-reviewer" (those are subagent dispatch).
1230	    offenders: list[tuple[int, str]] = []
1231	    lines = content.splitlines()
1232	    for i, line in enumerate(lines):
1233	        lower = line.lower()
1234	        # Quick reject 1: no dispatch terminology at all → skip.
1235	        if "dispatch" not in lower:
1236	            continue
1237	        # Quick reject 2: subagent-dispatch context → skip.
1238	        subagent_tokens = (
1239	            "subagent", "lead-po", "lead-architect", "bc-implementer",
1240	            "bc-reviewer",
1241	        )
1242	        if any(t in lower for t in subagent_tokens):
1243	            continue
1244	        # Procedural-shape filter. Match one of:
1245	        #   (a) Numbered list item (^\s*\d+\.) on the same line.
1246	        #   (b) Bulleted item beginning with an imperative voice that
1247	        #       describes an action ("- Architect dispatches", etc.) —
1248	        #       conservative: require the line to also name a
1249	        #       message-type token, "send", or "shop-msg send".
1250	        #   (c) Co-occurrence of "dispatch" with a message-type token
1251	        #       (those tokens are only used in procedural contexts).
1252	        stripped = line.lstrip()
1253	        is_numbered = bool(re.match(r"^\d+\.\s", stripped))
1254	        has_msg_type = any(
1255	            t in lower for t in (
1256	                "assign_scenarios", "request_bugfix",
1257	                "request_maintenance",
1258	            )
1259	        )
1260	        if not (is_numbered or has_msg_type):
1261	            continue
1262	        # The line is a procedural dispatch sentence. Verify
1263	        # "shop-msg send" appears within a local window.
1264	        window_start = max(0, i - 2)
1265	        window_end = min(len(lines), i + 5)
1266	        window = "\n".join(lines[window_start:window_end]).lower()
1267	        if "shop-msg send" in window:
1268	            continue
1269	        offenders.append((i + 1, line.rstrip()))
1270	    assert not offenders, (
1271	        f"file {context.get('file_relative_path')!r} has dispatch-describing "
1272	        f"sentences that do not name 'shop-msg send' in their local context:\n"
1273	        + "\n".join(f"  line {n}: {l!r}" for n, l in offenders)
1274	    )
1275	
1276	
1277	@then(
1278	    'every step or sentence that describes inspecting a BC\'s outbox state '
1279	    'names a "shop-msg" subcommand (for example "shop-msg pending outbox" '
1280	    'or "shop-msg read outbox") as the inspection operation'
1281	)
1282	def then_file_every_outbox_inspection_names_shop_msg(context: dict) -> None:
1283	    content = context["file_content"]
1284	    # We are looking for sentences that describe an inspection of a BC's
1285	    # outbox. The discriminating signal: a mention of "outbox" alongside
1286	    # an inspection verb ("inspect", "check", "list", "see", "read",
1287	    # "review", "examine"). For each, verify that a shop-msg subcommand
1288	    # appears in the local window.
1289	    inspection_verbs = (
1290	        "inspect", "check", "list", "see ", "read ", "review",
1291	        "examine", "view ", "look at", "look up",
1292	    )
1293	    offenders: list[tuple[int, str]] = []
1294	    lines = content.splitlines()
1295	    for i, line in enumerate(lines):
1296	        lower = line.lower()
1297	        if "outbox" not in lower:
1298	            continue
1299	        if not any(v in lower for v in inspection_verbs):
1300	            continue
1301	        # Local window for shop-msg presence.
1302	        window_start = max(0, i - 2)
1303	        window_end = min(len(lines), i + 5)
1304	        window = "\n".join(lines[window_start:window_end]).lower()
1305	        if "shop-msg pending outbox" in window or "shop-msg read outbox" in window or "shop-msg read" in window or "shop-msg pending" in window:
1306	            continue
1307	        offenders.append((i + 1, line.rstrip()))
1308	    assert not offenders, (
1309	        f"file {context.get('file_relative_path')!r} has outbox-inspection "
1310	        f"sentences that do not name a 'shop-msg' subcommand:\n"
1311	        + "\n".join(f"  line {n}: {l!r}" for n, l in offenders)
1312	    )
1313	
1314	
1315	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
1316	# Then steps — scenario 23: bc-implementer's mechanism-observation
1317	# section discipline
1318	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
1319	#
1320	# The "section" referred to in the scenario is the
1321	# "## Surfacing mechanism observations" subtree of the bc-implementer
1322	# template — from the H2 header until the next H2 header.
1323	
1324	
1325	def _extract_mechanism_observation_section(content: str) -> str:
1326	    marker = "## Surfacing mechanism observations"
1327	    start = content.find(marker)
1328	    assert start >= 0, (
1329	        "bc-implementer template missing 'Surfacing mechanism observations' "
1330	        "H2 section"
1331	    )
1332	    # Find the next H2 (## ) header after start.
1333	    rest = content[start + len(marker):]
1334	    # Scan line by line for the next "## " heading.
1335	    section_lines = [marker]
1336	    for line in rest.splitlines():
1337	        # An H2 starts at column 0 with "## " but NOT "### "; the H3
1338	        # subsections inside the section ("### Carve-outs", "### When
1339	        # to NOT emit a mechanism observation") are PART of the section.
1340	        if line.startswith("## ") and not line.startswith("### "):
1341	            break
1342	        section_lines.append(line)
1343	    return "\n".join(section_lines)
1344	
1345	
1346	@then(
1347	    parsers.parse(
1348	        'the "{section_header}" section does not contain the literal '
1349	        'substring "{needle}"'
1350	    )
1351	)
1352	def then_section_does_not_contain_substring(
1353	    section_header: str, needle: str, context: dict
1354	) -> None:
1355	    content = context["template_content"]
1356	    # Use the helper for the known section. Generalize if other sections
1357	    # need this step.
1358	    assert section_header == "Surfacing mechanism observations", (
1359	        f"step only knows the 'Surfacing mechanism observations' section; "
1360	        f"got {section_header!r}"
1361	    )
1362	    section = _extract_mechanism_observation_section(content)
1363	    assert needle not in section, (
1364	        f"section {section_header!r} of template "
1365	        f"{context.get('shown_template_name')!r} contains forbidden "
1366	        f"literal substring {needle!r}"
1367	    )
1368	
1369	
1370	@then(parsers.parse('the section does not contain the literal substring "{needle}"'))
1371	def then_section_does_not_contain_substring_short(
1372	    needle: str, context: dict
1373	) -> None:
1374	    # The shorter "And the section does not contain ..." form refers
1375	    # back to the section established by the preceding "the
1376	    # 'Surfacing mechanism observations' section does not contain ..."
1377	    # step. We re-extract by header name (the section is unique in the
1378	    # bc-implementer template).
1379	    content = context["template_content"]
1380	    section = _extract_mechanism_observation_section(content)
1381	    assert needle not in section, (
1382	        f"'Surfacing mechanism observations' section of template "
1383	        f"{context.get('shown_template_name')!r} contains forbidden "
1384	        f"literal substring {needle!r}"
1385	    )
1386	
1387	
1388	@then(
1389	    'the section does not contain language of the form "Create a beads '
1390	    'issue" or "create a beads issue" or "create a bd issue" as a numbered '
1391	    'or otherwise-ordered step that precedes the shop-msg respond '
1392	    'mechanism_observation step'
1393	)
1394	def then_section_no_create_beads_precondition(context: dict) -> None:
1395	    content = context["template_content"]
1396	    section = _extract_mechanism_observation_section(content)
1397	    # Find the first shop-msg respond mechanism_observation reference.
1398	    smr_idx = section.find("shop-msg respond mechanism_observation")
1399	    assert smr_idx >= 0, (
1400	        "'Surfacing mechanism observations' section must reference "
1401	        "'shop-msg respond mechanism_observation' (otherwise the section "
1402	        "has lost its load-bearing CLI guidance)"
1403	    )
1404	    pre = section[:smr_idx].lower()
1405	    forbidden_phrases = (
1406	        "create a beads issue",
1407	        "create a bd issue",
1408	        # Numbered/ordered step language with beads precondition shape.
1409	        "1. create a bead",
1410	        "1. create a beads",
1411	        "step 1: create a bead",
1412	        "step 1: create a beads",
1413	    )
1414	    hits = [p for p in forbidden_phrases if p in pre]
1415	    assert not hits, (
1416	        f"'Surfacing mechanism observations' section names a beads-issue-"
1417	        f"creation step that precedes the shop-msg respond "
1418	        f"mechanism_observation step: {hits!r}"
1419	    )
1420	
1421	
1422	@then(
1423	    'every shop-msg respond mechanism_observation invocation example in '
1424	    'the section is composable without referring to any flag whose name '
1425	    'contains the substring "bd"'
1426	)
1427	def then_section_no_bd_flag_in_invocation(context: dict) -> None:
1428	    content = context["template_content"]
1429	    section = _extract_mechanism_observation_section(content)
1430	    # Find every shop-msg respond mechanism_observation block and walk
1431	    # its argument lines forward until the block ends (a blank line or
1432	    # a non-continuation line). Reject any flag --<name>... where name
1433	    # contains "bd".
1434	    offenders: list[str] = []
1435	    lines = section.splitlines()
1436	    i = 0
1437	    while i < len(lines):
1438	        if "shop-msg respond mechanism_observation" in lines[i]:
1439	            # Collect this line and subsequent continuation lines (those
1440	            # indented or starting with whitespace + flag-shape).
1441	            block_lines = [lines[i]]
1442	            j = i + 1
1443	            while j < len(lines):
1444	                follow = lines[j]
1445	                stripped = follow.strip()
1446	                if not stripped:
1447	                    break
1448	                # Continuation: line ends with backslash, or starts with
1449	                # whitespace + "--" / "[--".
1450	                prev = block_lines[-1].rstrip()
1451	                if prev.endswith("\\") or stripped.startswith("--") or stripped.startswith("[--"):
1452	                    block_lines.append(follow)
1453	                    j += 1
1454	                    continue
1455	                break
1456	            block = "\n".join(block_lines)
1457	            # Find every flag --<name> in the block. A flag name
1458	            # containing "bd" is offensive.
1459	            for match in re.finditer(r"--([a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9-]*)", block):
1460	                flag_name = match.group(1)
1461	                if "bd" in flag_name.lower():
1462	                    offenders.append(f"flag --{flag_name} in block: {block!r}")
1463	            i = j
1464	            continue
1465	        i += 1
1466	    assert not offenders, (
1467	        "shop-msg respond mechanism_observation example(s) in "
1468	        "'Surfacing mechanism observations' section contain a flag whose "
1469	        "name carries 'bd':\n  " + "\n  ".join(offenders)
1470	    )
1471	
1472	
1473	@then(
1474	    'if the section mentions a provenance pointer at all it names it via '
1475	    'the optional flag "--provenance-ref"'
1476	)
1477	def then_section_provenance_pointer_via_provenance_ref(context: dict) -> None:
1478	    content = context["template_content"]
1479	    section = _extract_mechanism_observation_section(content)
1480	    lower = section.lower()
1481	    if "provenance" not in lower:
1482	        return  # vacuously satisfied
1483	    # If "provenance" appears, "--provenance-ref" must also appear.
1484	    assert "--provenance-ref" in section, (
1485	        "'Surfacing mechanism observations' section mentions 'provenance' "
1486	        "but does not name the optional flag '--provenance-ref'"
1487	    )
1488	
1489	
1490	@then(
1491	    'the section contains an explicit statement that emitting the '
1492	    'mechanism_observation does not require the BC to use bd or to create '
1493	    'a bd issue'
1494	)
1495	def then_section_explicit_no_bd_requirement(context: dict) -> None:
1496	    content = context["template_content"]
1497	    section = _extract_mechanism_observation_section(content)
1498	    lower = section.lower()
1499	    # Acceptable phrasings that satisfy the explicit-statement check.
1500	    # The check is a substring OR — at least one of these phrasings
1501	    # must appear.
1502	    candidate_phrasings = (
1503	        "does not require bd",
1504	        "does not require the bc to use bd",
1505	        "does not require a bd issue",
1506	        "does not require creating a bd issue",
1507	        "does not require beads",
1508	        "no bd issue is required",
1509	        "no bd issue required",
1510	        "bd is not required",
1511	        "bd participation is not required",
1512	        "no beads issue is required",
1513	        "without bd",
1514	        "without a bd issue",
1515	        "without creating a bd issue",
1516	    )
1517	    if not any(p in lower for p in candidate_phrasings):
1518	        raise AssertionError(
1519	            "'Surfacing mechanism observations' section must contain an "
1520	            "explicit statement that emitting the mechanism_observation "
1521	            "does not require bd participation. None of the acceptable "
1522	            f"phrasings were found:\n  {candidate_phrasings!r}"
1523	        )
1524	
1525	
1526	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
1527	# Then steps — Scenario Outlines (scenarios 24 and 25): bd-decoupling
1528	# across all four templates and both CLAUDE.md files
1529	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
1530	
1531	
1532	def _assert_no_bd_precondition_in_content(content: str, identifier: str) -> None:
1533	    """Assert that no sentence, list item, or numbered step instructs the
1534	    reader to create/claim/update a bd issue as a precondition for a
1535	    shop-msg invocation."""
1536	    # Strategy: find every shop-msg invocation (line containing
1537	    # "shop-msg send" or "shop-msg respond"). For each, walk backward
1538	    # within the same enclosing block (run of non-blank lines, or
1539	    # numbered/list block) and assert that the preceding lines do not
1540	    # contain a bd-action verb (create/claim/update) targeting a bd
1541	    # issue.
1542	    bd_action_patterns = (
1543	        r"\bbd\s+(?:create|update|claim|update.*--claim)\b",
1544	        r"\bcreate\s+a\s+bd\s+issue\b",
1545	        r"\bcreate\s+a\s+beads\s+issue\b",
1546	        r"\bcreate\s+a\s+bead\b",
1547	        r"\bclaim\s+the\s+bd\s+issue\b",
1548	        r"\bclaim\s+a\s+bd\s+issue\b",
1549	        r"\bupdate\s+the\s+bd\s+issue\b",
1550	        r"\bfile\s+a\s+bd\s+issue\b",
1551	        r"\bfile\s+a\s+beads\s+issue\b",
1552	    )
1553	    offenders: list[str] = []
1554	    lines = content.splitlines()
1555	    for i, line in enumerate(lines):
1556	        lower = line.lower()
1557	        if "shop-msg send" not in lower and "shop-msg respond" not in lower:
1558	            continue
1559	        # Walk backward to find the enclosing block boundary. A block
1560	        # boundary is a blank line or a heading line.
1561	        block_start = i
1562	        while block_start > 0:
1563	            prev = lines[block_start - 1]
1564	            if not prev.strip():
1565	                break
1566	            if prev.lstrip().startswith("#"):
1567	                break
1568	            block_start -= 1
1569	        preceding = "\n".join(lines[block_start:i]).lower()
1570	        # Check for any bd-action pattern in the preceding block region.
1571	        for pat in bd_action_patterns:
1572	            m = re.search(pat, preceding)
1573	            if m:
1574	                # Distinguish "instructs as precondition" from "describes
1575	                # observationally". A line that is part of a numbered or
1576	                # bulleted procedural list and precedes a shop-msg step
1577	                # is procedural-precondition shape. Lines that are part
1578	                # of prose explanations (e.g., "bd holds the work
1579	                # registry") are observational. The discriminator:
1580	                # the bd action appears in a numbered/bulleted step.
1581	                for j in range(block_start, i):
1582	                    line_j = lines[j]
1583	                    stripped_j = line_j.lstrip()
1584	                    if re.search(pat, line_j.lower()):
1585	                        if (
1586	                            stripped_j.startswith("- ")
1587	                            or stripped_j.startswith("* ")
1588	                            or re.match(r"^\d+\.\s", stripped_j)
1589	                            or re.match(r"^step\s+\d+", stripped_j.lower())
1590	                        ):
1591	                            offenders.append(
1592	                                f"line {j + 1}: {line_j.rstrip()!r} "
1593	                                f"(precedes shop-msg invocation at line {i + 1})"
1594	                            )
1595	                            break
1596	                break  # one offense per shop-msg invocation is enough
1597	    assert not offenders, (
1598	        f"{identifier} instructs creating/claiming/updating a bd issue as "
1599	        f"a precondition for a shop-msg invocation:\n  "
1600	        + "\n  ".join(offenders)
1601	    )
1602	
1603	
1604	def _assert_shop_msg_examples_composable_without_bd(
1605	    content: str, subcommand: str, identifier: str
1606	) -> None:
1607	    """For every example of `subcommand` (e.g., 'shop-msg send' or
1608	    'shop-msg respond'), check the invocation block does not require a
1609	    bd subcommand to run first. The check: no `bd` invocation appears
1610	    in the example's own block as a prerequisite step.
1611	    """
1612	    # An "invocation example" is a line containing the subcommand. The
1613	    # block is what surrounds it as one contiguous procedural unit
1614	    # (numbered list item, or code block). If any earlier step in the
1615	    # same numbered procedural list says "bd X", that example is not
1616	    # composable without bd.
1617	    offenders: list[str] = []
1618	    lines = content.splitlines()
1619	    for i, line in enumerate(lines):
1620	        lower = line.lower()
1621	        if subcommand not in lower:
1622	            continue
1623	        # Identify the enclosing numbered-list step. Walk backward to
1624	        # find the start of the numbered-list run.
1625	        # A "numbered procedural unit" is a contiguous run of
1626	        # lines that includes at least one numbered step (\d+\.\s) and
1627	        # ends at a blank line or heading.
1628	        # Find run boundaries.
1629	        run_start = i
1630	        while run_start > 0:
1631	            prev = lines[run_start - 1]
1632	            if not prev.strip() or prev.lstrip().startswith("#"):
1633	                break
1634	            run_start -= 1
1635	        run_end = i
1636	        while run_end + 1 < len(lines):
1637	            nxt = lines[run_end + 1]
1638	            if not nxt.strip() or nxt.lstrip().startswith("#"):
1639	                break
1640	            run_end += 1
1641	        run = "\n".join(lines[run_start : run_end + 1])
1642	        # Look only at lines BEFORE the subcommand line in this run.
1643	        before = "\n".join(lines[run_start:i]).lower()
1644	        # A bd-precondition in the procedural unit is a numbered or
1645	        # bulleted step containing a `bd ` command verb.
1646	        # We require the bd command to be at the start of a step line
1647	        # (after the bullet/number).
1648	        bd_step_re = re.compile(
1649	            r"(?:^|\n)\s*(?:\d+\.\s+|[-*]\s+|step\s+\d+[:.]\s+)[^\n]*\bbd\s+(?:create|update|claim|push|prime|ready|show|close)\b",
1650	            re.IGNORECASE,
1651	        )
1652	        if bd_step_re.search(before):
1653	            offenders.append(
1654	                f"line {i + 1}: {line.rstrip()!r} is preceded in its "
1655	                f"procedural run by a 'bd ...' step"
1656	            )
1657	    assert not offenders, (
1658	        f"{identifier} has {subcommand!r} invocation example(s) that are "
1659	        f"not composable without first running a 'bd' subcommand:\n  "
1660	        + "\n  ".join(offenders)
1661	    )
1662	
1663	
1664	@then(
1665	    'the content does not contain any sentence, list item, or numbered '
1666	    'step that instructs the reader to create, claim, or update a bd '
1667	    'issue as a precondition for invoking any "shop-msg send" or '
1668	    '"shop-msg respond" subcommand'
1669	)
1670	def then_content_no_bd_precondition(context: dict) -> None:
1671	    content, ident = _content_for_target(context, "content")
1672	    _assert_no_bd_precondition_in_content(content, ident)
1673	
1674	
1675	@then(
1676	    parsers.parse(
1677	        'every "{subcommand}" invocation example in the content is '
1678	        'composable without the reader first running a "bd" subcommand'
1679	    )
1680	)
1681	def then_content_subcommand_composable_without_bd(
1682	    subcommand: str, context: dict
1683	) -> None:
1684	    content, ident = _content_for_target(context, "content")
1685	    _assert_shop_msg_examples_composable_without_bd(content, subcommand, ident)
1686	
1687	
1688	@then(
1689	    'the file does not contain any sentence, list item, or numbered '
1690	    'step that instructs the reader to create, claim, or update a bd '
1691	    'issue as a precondition for invoking any "shop-msg send" or '
1692	    '"shop-msg respond" subcommand'
1693	)
1694	def then_file_no_bd_precondition(context: dict) -> None:
1695	    content, ident = _content_for_target(context, "file")
1696	    _assert_no_bd_precondition_in_content(content, ident)
1697	
1698	
1699	@then(
1700	    parsers.parse(
1701	        'every "{subcommand}" invocation example in the file is composable '
1702	        'without the reader first running a "bd" subcommand'
1703	    )
1704	)
1705	def then_file_subcommand_composable_without_bd(
1706	    subcommand: str, context: dict
1707	) -> None:
1708	    content, ident = _content_for_target(context, "file")
1709	    _assert_shop_msg_examples_composable_without_bd(content, subcommand, ident)
1710	
1711
```

```text
1	"""Discovers Gherkin features under shopsystem-templates/features/ and
2	registers them as pytest-bdd scenarios. No-op when no .feature files are
3	present, so the suite stays green during a future drop-everything reset.
4	"""
5	from pathlib import Path
6	
7	from pytest_bdd import scenarios
8	
9	_features_dir = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "features"
10	if any(_features_dir.glob("*.feature")):
11	    scenarios(str(_features_dir))
12
```
</details></tool-use>

```feature
Feature: lead-architect 'Responding to a BC clarify' subsection names shop-msg read outbox per step

  @scenario_hash:e6bdf2f33bfae0d1 @bc:shopsystem-templates
  Scenario: lead-architect template's "Responding to a BC clarify" subsection names "shop-msg read outbox" on every step whose action is reading the BC's outbox, never relying on a surrounding clause
  When I read the lead-architect template via "shop-templates show lead-architect"
  And I locate the subsection that begins with the heading "### Responding to a BC clarify via shop-msg respond" and ends at the next heading of depth two (##) or depth three (###), whichever comes first
  Then within that subsection, every numbered step whose action is reading the clarify from the BC's outbox names the literal substring "shop-msg read outbox" on the same step
  And within that subsection, no numbered step describes reading the BC's outbox using a bare action verb — "open", "cat", "ls", "grep", "inspect", "view", "edit", "tail", "head", or a bare "Read" — without naming the literal substring "shop-msg read outbox" on the same step
  And within that subsection, no numbered step refers to the BC's outbox by a path-shaped reference such as "outbox/", "<BC root>/outbox/", or "the outbox file" without naming the literal substring "shop-msg read outbox" on the same step

```

```text
File created successfully at: /workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-templates/features/lead_architect_responding_to_clarify_cli_naming.feature (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
  \ scenarios dispatched under lead-bwu. The BC should NOT emit a separate work_done\
  \ on lead-bwu retroactively. lead-bwu remains in_progress on the lead side until\
  \ lead-e4g closes; closure of lead-e4g closes lead-bwu as well.\n\nPROCEDURAL REMINDERS:\n\
  - Hash discipline (per lead-5cv): scenario hashing is canonicalized by the messaging\
  \ BC's 'scenarios hash' rule; the @scenario_hash tag on each carried ScenarioPayload\
  \ must match 'scenarios hash' of its body. The shop-msg send CLI handles this automatically;\
  \ the Implementer's sufficiency check should verify the dispatched hashes match\
  \ the bodies as received.\n- Encapsulation: all message inspection routes through\
  \ 'shop-msg pending inbox' / 'shop-msg read inbox' on this BC's side; no direct\
  \ mailbox storage reads."
scenarios:
- hash: e6bdf2f33bfae0d1
  tags:
  - '@scenario_hash:e6bdf2f33bfae0d1'
  - '@bc:shopsystem-templates'
  gherkin: "Feature: lead-architect 'Responding to a BC clarify' subsection names\
    \ shop-msg read outbox per step\n\n  @scenario_hash:e6bdf2f33bfae0d1 @bc:shopsystem-templates\n\
    \  Scenario: lead-architect template's \"Responding to a BC clarify\" subsection\
    \ names \"shop-msg read outbox\" on every step whose action is reading the BC's\
    \ outbox, never relying on a surrounding clause\n  When I read the lead-architect\
    \ template via \"shop-templates show lead-architect\"\n  And I locate the subsection\
    \ that begins with the heading \"### Responding to a BC clarify via shop-msg respond\"\
    \ and ends at the next heading of depth two (##) or depth three (###), whichever\
    \ comes first\n  Then within that subsection, every numbered step whose action\
    \ is reading the clarify from the BC's outbox names the literal substring \"shop-msg\
    \ read outbox\" on the same step\n  And within that subsection, no numbered step\
    \ describes reading the BC's outbox using a bare action verb \u2014 \"open\",\
    \ \"cat\", \"ls\", \"grep\", \"inspect\", \"view\", \"edit\", \"tail\", \"head\"\
    , or a bare \"Read\" \u2014 without naming the literal substring \"shop-msg read\
    \ outbox\" on the same step\n  And within that subsection, no numbered step refers\
    \ to the BC's outbox by a path-shaped reference such as \"outbox/\", \"<BC root>/outbox/\"\
    , or \"the outbox file\" without naming the literal substring \"shop-msg read\
    \ outbox\" on the same step\n\n"
```

```text
100	If any scenario fails (1)–(3), emit `clarify` naming the specific scenario
101	and the specific gap. If (4) fires, emit `clarify` naming the existing
102	coverage and the three-way question above.
103	
104	## Doing the work — `assign_scenarios`
105	
106	When sufficient, the assigned scenario is a specification of new behavior.
107	Default expectation: the BC does not yet support what the scenario describes,
108	and `tests/conftest.py` does not yet have step definitions for the scenario's
109	phrasings. Both are *your job* to add.
110	
111	1. **Write each scenario** to `<BC root>/features/<descriptive-name>.feature`,
112	   preserving its tags including the `@scenario_hash` tag exactly.
113	2. **Add step definitions** for any Given/When/Then phrasings the scenario
114	   uses that are not already covered in `tests/conftest.py`. The step
115	   definitions are Python; they exercise the BC's interfaces (importing from
116	   `src/`).
117	3. **Run the BDD suite** with `python3 -m pytest tests/`. Expect failure on
118	   the first run because the BC capability does not yet exist.
119	4. **Implement the capability** in `src/` so the scenario passes. Implement
120	   exactly what the scenario's Then steps assert — no more, no less. If you
121	   find yourself wanting to handle a case the scenario does not pin, that is
122	   a sign the scenario is incomplete; resist the urge and let the Reviewer
123	   surface it.
124	5. **Re-run the BDD suite** until it passes. Re-run unit tests too — do not
125	   regress existing capabilities.
126	6. **DO NOT run `shop-msg respond work_done` and DO NOT emit any outbox
127	   response by any other means.** You are not the gate. The Reviewer
128	   holds it.
129	
130	## Sufficiency check — `request_bugfix`
131	
132	`request_bugfix` may carry plain-language fix instructions, a tightened
133	Gherkin scenario, or both. The check has two parts:
134	
135	1. **Description is concrete enough to know what is being changed.** It
136	   names the behavior under change and (when applicable) references the
137	   prior scenario hash being tightened.
138	2. **If `scenarios` is non-empty:** each embedded scenario must pass the
139	   `assign_scenarios` sufficiency check (well-formed Gherkin, concrete
140	   steps, hash tag present).
141	
142	If either part fails, emit `clarify`.
143	
144	## Doing the work — `request_bugfix`
145	
146	When sufficient, treat embedded `scenarios` as additive to the BC's
147	existing scenario set (do NOT delete prior scenarios unless the
148	description explicitly says they are superseded). Otherwise the flow is
149	the same as `assign_scenarios`:
150	
151	1. Write each new scenario to `<BC root>/features/<descriptive-name>.feature`.
152	2. Add step definitions in `tests/conftest.py` for any new phrasings.
153	3. Modify `src/` to make the new scenario(s) pass.
154	4. Run BDD; **all** scenarios must pass — both the new ones and any
155	   pre-existing ones the description does not mark as superseded.
156	5. Do NOT run `shop-msg respond work_done` and do NOT write to the outbox
157	   by other means; the Reviewer holds the gate.
158	
159	## Hand-off to the Reviewer
160	
161	You are not the final authority on `work_done`. The Reviewer is dispatched
162	after you, gates the outbox, and is the only role that emits `work_done` for
163	`assign_scenarios` work. When you finish, **leave the BC in its post-work
164	state** (feature file written, step defs added, capability implemented, BDD
165	passing, unit tests passing) and report your work in your final response
166	message — do not run `shop-msg respond work_done` and do not write any
167	outbox file by other means.
168	
169	The one exception: if your sufficiency check on the assigned scenarios fails
170	(see above), emit `clarify` via `shop-msg respond clarify` directly. The
171	Reviewer is only dispatched when the Implementer has done work, not when
172	the Implementer has clarified back.
173	
174	## Surfacing mechanism observations
```

```text
1700	    parsers.parse(
1701	        'every "{subcommand}" invocation example in the file is composable '
1702	        'without the reader first running a "bd" subcommand'
1703	    )
1704	)
1705	def then_file_subcommand_composable_without_bd(
1706	    subcommand: str, context: dict
1707	) -> None:

1709	    _assert_shop_msg_examples_composable_without_bd(content, subcommand, ident)
1710	
1711	
1712	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
1713	# Step definitions — lead-architect "Responding to a BC clarify"
1714	# per-step CLI naming (scenario e6bdf2f33bfae0d1 / lead-e4g)
1715	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
1716	#
1717	# These steps localize to a named subsection of the lead-architect
1718	# template and enforce a per-step invariant: every numbered procedural
1719	# step whose action is reading the BC's outbox must name the literal
1720	# substring "shop-msg read outbox" on the same step. A surrounding
1721	# Constraints clause is too weak to prevent regression — the per-step
1722	# discipline is what closes the asymmetry surfaced by lead-bwu's
1723	# clarify against lead-architect.md line 272 vs lead-po.md lines 160-162.
1724	
1725	
1726	def _extract_subsection_between_headings(
1727	    content: str, start_heading: str
1728	) -> str:
1729	    """Return the body of the subsection that begins with `start_heading`
1730	    and runs until the next heading of depth two (## ) or depth three
1731	    (### ), whichever comes first. The returned text includes the
1732	    starting heading line and stops just before the terminating heading.
1733	    """
1734	    start_idx = content.find(start_heading)
1735	    assert start_idx >= 0, (
1736	        f"subsection start heading not found in content: {start_heading!r}"
1737	    )
1738	    rest = content[start_idx + len(start_heading):]
1739	    section_lines = [start_heading]
1740	    for line in rest.splitlines():
1741	        stripped = line.lstrip()
1742	        # Stop at the next H2 (## ) or H3 (### ). Note: the starting
1743	        # heading itself is H3; the *next* H3 or H2 terminates the
1744	        # subsection.
1745	        if stripped.startswith("## ") or stripped.startswith("### "):
1746	            break
1747	        section_lines.append(line)
1748	    return "\n".join(section_lines)
1749	
1750	
1751	def _numbered_steps_in_subsection(subsection: str) -> list[tuple[int, str]]:
1752	    """Return a list of (index, step_text) tuples for each numbered
1753	    step in `subsection`. A numbered step is a line whose left-stripped
1754	    form matches `\\d+\\.\\s`. Continuation lines (indented prose that
1755	    follows the numbered line until the next numbered line or blank
1756	    line) are joined into the same step_text — the per-step assertion
1757	    treats the step as a single unit.
1758	    """
1759	    lines = subsection.splitlines()
1760	    steps: list[tuple[int, str]] = []
1761	    i = 0
1762	    while i < len(lines):
1763	        stripped = lines[i].lstrip()
1764	        if re.match(r"^\d+\.\s", stripped):
1765	            step_lines = [lines[i]]
1766	            j = i + 1
1767	            while j < len(lines):
1768	                follow = lines[j]
1769	                follow_stripped = follow.lstrip()
1770	                # Stop at the next numbered step, a blank line, or a
1771	                # heading. Continuation lines are typically indented
1772	                # prose that belongs to the current step.
1773	                if re.match(r"^\d+\.\s", follow_stripped):
1774	                    break
1775	                if not follow.strip():
1776	                    break
1777	                if follow_stripped.startswith("#"):
1778	                    break
1779	                step_lines.append(follow)
1780	                j += 1
1781	            number_match = re.match(r"^(\d+)\.", stripped)
1782	            assert number_match is not None  # guarded by the outer match
1783	            steps.append((int(number_match.group(1)), "\n".join(step_lines)))
1784	            i = j
1785	            continue
1786	        i += 1
1787	    return steps
1788	
1789	
1790	_OUTBOX_READ_BARE_VERBS = (
1791	    "open", "cat", "ls", "grep", "inspect", "view", "edit", "tail", "head",
1792	)
1793	_OUTBOX_PATH_SHAPES = (
1794	    "outbox/", "<bc root>/outbox/", "the outbox file",
1795	)
1796	
1797	
1798	@when(
1799	    parsers.parse(
1800	        'I locate the subsection that begins with the heading '
1801	        '"{start_heading}" and ends at the next heading of depth two (##) '
1802	        'or depth three (###), whichever comes first'
1803	    )
1804	)
1805	def when_locate_subsection_between_headings(
1806	    start_heading: str, context: dict
1807	) -> None:
1808	    content = context["template_content"]
1809	    subsection = _extract_subsection_between_headings(content, start_heading)
1810	    context["located_subsection"] = subsection
1811	    context["located_subsection_heading"] = start_heading
1812	
1813	
1814	def _step_describes_reading_outbox(step_text: str) -> bool:
1815	    """Heuristic discriminator: does the step describe reading the BC's
1816	    outbox? The step text is one numbered list item plus its
1817	    continuation lines (per `_numbered_steps_in_subsection`).
1818	
1819	    The discriminator fires when the step contains BOTH:
1820	      (a) the word "outbox" (the target), AND
1821	      (b) a reading-shape signal: a bare reading verb listed in
1822	          `_OUTBOX_READ_BARE_VERBS`, OR the verb "Read" / "read", OR a
1823	          path-shape reference from `_OUTBOX_PATH_SHAPES`.
1824	
1825	    A step that names "shop-msg read outbox" already satisfies the
1826	    invariant — the discriminator still fires for it (because it does
1827	    describe reading the outbox), and the per-step naming check then
1828	    passes trivially.
1829	    """
1830	    lower = step_text.lower()
1831	    if "outbox" not in lower:
1832	        return False
1833	    if "read" in lower:
1834	        return True
1835	    for verb in _OUTBOX_READ_BARE_VERBS:
1836	        # Word-boundary match so "viewed" doesn't trip "view" only if
1837	        # we want it to; but the verb list itself is what the scenario
1838	        # enumerates, so a substring match against the lowercase step
1839	        # text is what the scenario asks for.
1840	        if re.search(rf"\b{re.escape(verb)}\b", lower):
1841	            return True
1842	    for shape in _OUTBOX_PATH_SHAPES:
1843	        if shape in lower:
1844	            return True
1845	    return False
1846	
1847	
1848	@then(
1849	    'within that subsection, every numbered step whose action is reading '
1850	    'the clarify from the BC\'s outbox names the literal substring '
1851	    '"shop-msg read outbox" on the same step'
1852	)
1853	def then_subsection_outbox_read_steps_name_cli(context: dict) -> None:
1854	    subsection = context["located_subsection"]
1855	    heading = context["located_subsection_heading"]
1856	    offenders: list[str] = []
1857	    for number, step_text in _numbered_steps_in_subsection(subsection):
1858	        if not _step_describes_reading_outbox(step_text):
1859	            continue
1860	        if "shop-msg read outbox" in step_text:
1861	            continue
1862	        offenders.append(f"step {number}: {step_text!r}")
1863	    assert not offenders, (
1864	        f"subsection {heading!r} has step(s) whose action is reading the "
1865	        f"BC's outbox but do not name 'shop-msg read outbox' on the same "
1866	        f"step:\n  " + "\n  ".join(offenders)
1867	    )
1868	
1869	
1870	@then(
1871	    parsers.re(
1872	        r'^within that subsection, no numbered step describes reading the '
1873	        r"BC's outbox using a bare action verb . \"open\", \"cat\", \"ls\", "
1874	        r'\"grep\", \"inspect\", \"view\", \"edit\", \"tail\", \"head\", or '
1875	        r'a bare \"Read\" . without naming the literal substring '
1876	        r'"shop-msg read outbox" on the same step$'
1877	    )
1878	)
1879	def then_subsection_no_bare_verb_outbox_read(context: dict) -> None:
1880	    subsection = context["located_subsection"]
1881	    heading = context["located_subsection_heading"]
1882	    offenders: list[str] = []
1883	    for number, step_text in _numbered_steps_in_subsection(subsection):
1884	        lower = step_text.lower()
1885	        if "outbox" not in lower:
1886	            continue
1887	        if "shop-msg read outbox" in step_text:
1888	            continue
1889	        bare_verb_hits: list[str] = []
1890	        # Bare "Read" — distinct from "read outbox" in a shop-msg invocation
1891	        # (which is excluded by the "shop-msg read outbox" guard above).
1892	        if re.search(r"\bread\b", lower):
1893	            bare_verb_hits.append("Read")
1894	        for verb in _OUTBOX_READ_BARE_VERBS:
1895	            if re.search(rf"\b{re.escape(verb)}\b", lower):
1896	                bare_verb_hits.append(verb)
1897	        if bare_verb_hits:
1898	            offenders.append(
1899	                f"step {number}: bare verbs {bare_verb_hits!r} in "
1900	                f"step that references outbox without naming "
1901	                f"'shop-msg read outbox'; step text: {step_text!r}"
1902	            )
1903	    assert not offenders, (
1904	        f"subsection {heading!r} has bare-verb outbox-read step(s):\n  "
1905	        + "\n  ".join(offenders)
1906	    )
1907	
1908	
1909	@then(
1910	    parsers.re(
1911	        r'^within that subsection, no numbered step refers to the BC\'s '
1912	        r'outbox by a path-shaped reference such as "outbox/", '
1913	        r'"<BC root>/outbox/", or "the outbox file" without naming the '
1914	        r'literal substring "shop-msg read outbox" on the same step$'
1915	    )
1916	)
1917	def then_subsection_no_path_shape_outbox_ref(context: dict) -> None:
1918	    subsection = context["located_subsection"]
1919	    heading = context["located_subsection_heading"]
1920	    offenders: list[str] = []
1921	    for number, step_text in _numbered_steps_in_subsection(subsection):
1922	        if "shop-msg read outbox" in step_text:
1923	            continue
1924	        lower = step_text.lower()
1925	        path_hits: list[str] = []
1926	        for shape in _OUTBOX_PATH_SHAPES:
1927	            if shape in lower:
1928	                path_hits.append(shape)
1929	        if path_hits:
1930	            offenders.append(
1931	                f"step {number}: path-shape references {path_hits!r} "
1932	                f"without naming 'shop-msg read outbox'; step text: "
1933	                f"{step_text!r}"
1934	            )
1935	    assert not offenders, (
1936	        f"subsection {heading!r} has path-shape outbox reference(s) "
1937	        f"without CLI naming:\n  " + "\n  ".join(offenders)
1938	    )
1939
```
