100	
101	
102	@when(parsers.parse('I run "shop-templates show {name}"'))
103	def when_run_show(name: str, context: dict) -> None:
104	    result = _run_shop_templates("show", name)
105	    context["cli_returncode"] = result.returncode
106	    context["cli_stdout"] = result.stdout
107	    context["cli_stderr"] = result.stderr
108	    context["shown_template_name"] = name
109	
110	
111	@when(
112	    parsers.parse(
113	        'I read the {name} template via "shop-templates show {name_again}"'
114	    )
115	)
116	def when_read_template_via_show(name: str, name_again: str, context: dict) -> None:
117	    # Belt-and-braces: the two name slots must agree, otherwise the
118	    # scenario text is internally inconsistent and any Then assertion
119	    # would be assertion-by-coincidence.
120	    assert name == name_again, (
121	        f"scenario inconsistency: 'the {name} template' but "
122	        f"'shop-templates show {name_again}'"
123	    )
124	    result = _run_shop_templates("show", name)
125	    context["cli_returncode"] = result.returncode
126	    context["cli_stdout"] = result.stdout
127	    context["cli_stderr"] = result.stderr
128	    context["shown_template_name"] = name
129	    # The Then-steps for these scenarios refer to "the content" — keep an
130	    # explicit alias so the assertion language matches the feature text.
131	    context["template_content"] = result.stdout
132	
133	
134	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
135	# Then steps — generic CLI assertions
136	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
137	
138	
139	@then("the exit code is 0")
140	def then_exit_code_is_zero(context: dict) -> None:
141	    rc = context["cli_returncode"]
142	    assert rc == 0, (
143	        f"expected exit code 0; got {rc}; stderr:\n{context.get('cli_stderr', '')}"
144	    )
145	
146	
147	@then("the exit code is non-zero")
148	def then_exit_code_is_nonzero(context: dict) -> None:
149	    rc = context["cli_returncode"]
150	    assert rc != 0, (
151	        f"expected non-zero exit; got {rc}; stderr:\n{context.get('cli_stderr', '')}"
152	    )
153	
154	
155	@then("stderr is empty")
156	def then_stderr_is_empty(context: dict) -> None:
157	    stderr = context["cli_stderr"]
158	    assert stderr == "", f"expected empty stderr; got {stderr!r}"
159	
160	
161	@then("stdout is empty")
162	def then_stdout_is_empty(context: dict) -> None:
163	    stdout = context["cli_stdout"]
164	    assert stdout == "", f"expected empty stdout; got {stdout!r}"
165	
166	
167	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
168	# Then steps — shop-templates list shape
169	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
170	
171	
172	@then(
173	    parsers.re(
174	        r'stdout is exactly the four names '
175	        r'"(?P<n1>[^"]+)", "(?P<n2>[^"]+)", "(?P<n3>[^"]+)", "(?P<n4>[^"]+)", '
176	        r'one per line in that sorted order'
177	    )
178	)
179	def then_stdout_is_exactly_four_names_sorted(
180	    n1: str, n2: str, n3: str, n4: str, context: dict
181	) -> None:
182	    expected = [n1, n2, n3, n4]
183	    # Pin the sorted-order contract explicitly: if the scenario text
184	    # claims "sorted order" but the literal sequence supplied is not
185	    # actually sorted, the scenario is internally inconsistent and the
186	    # downstream assertion would be meaningless.
187	    assert expected == sorted(expected), (
188	        f"scenario premise violated: supplied names {expected!r} are not "
189	        f"in sorted order"
190	    )
191	    stdout = context["cli_stdout"]
192	    # The CLI uses print() per line, which emits a trailing newline after
193	    # the last line. Split on newlines and drop the final empty entry
194	    # that comes from that trailing newline.
195	    lines = stdout.split("\n")
196	    if lines and lines[-1] == "":
197	        lines = lines[:-1]
198	    assert lines == expected, (
199	        f"expected stdout lines {expected!r}; got {lines!r}"
200	    )
201	
202	
203	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------

213	    # Re-read the underlying template through the same package-data

217	    from shop_templates.cli import _read_template
218

223	    )
224	    actual = context["cli_stdout"]
225	    assert actual == expected, (
226	        f"shop-templates show {name} did not emit byte-identical content "

229	        f"expected_repr_tail={expected[-40:]!r}\n"
230	        f"actual_repr_tail={actual[-40:]!r}"
231	    )

241	    stderr = context["cli_stderr"]

247	@then(
248	    "stderr cites the list of available templates so the caller can recover"
249	)
250	def then_stderr_cites_available_templates(context: dict) -> None:
251	    stderr = context["cli_stderr"]
252	    # "cites the list" is satisfied when at least one real template name
253	    # appears in stderr — the caller can derive the available set from
254	    # there. We require every known template name to appear, which is
255	    # the strongest reading of "cites the list" and matches the
256	    # implementation's `Available: <comma-joined names>` shape.
257	    available = context.get("available_templates")
258	    if available is None:
259	        # Recover the list directly if a preceding Given did not stash it.
260	        result = _run_shop_templates("list")
261	        assert result.returncode == 0, (
262	            f"shop-templates list failed; stderr:\n{result.stderr}"
263	        )
264	        available = result.stdout.strip().splitlines()
265	    for name in available:
266	        assert name in stderr, (
267	            f"expected stderr to cite available template {name!r}; got:\n{stderr}"
268	        )

294	)
295	def then_content_names_empirical_pre_state_verification(context: dict) -> None:

298	    # surrounding message-type vocabulary must also appear so this isn't
299	    # satisfied by an unrelated use of "empirical" elsewhere in the
```

452	# that each §3.2 activity must be NAMED in the template. The qualifier

458	@then(

475	    )

477	def then_content_names_activity_with_qualifier(
478	    activity: str, qualifier: str, context: dict
479	) -> None:
480	    content = context["template_content"]
481	    assert activity in content, (
482	        f"template {context.get('shown_template_name')!r} does not name §3.2 "
483	        f"activity {activity!r}"
484	    )
485	    # The qualifier must appear in the same paragraph / contiguous block as
486	    # the activity name. We approximate "same block" as: somewhere within
487	    # 200 bytes after the activity name OR on the same line. That's broad
488	    # enough to allow a parenthetical, a sentence-following clause, or the
489	    # next sentence, but tight enough that the qualifier is contextually
490	    # bound to the activity rather than an unrelated mention elsewhere.
491	    idx = content.index(activity)
492	    window = content[idx : idx + 200 + len(activity)]
493	    assert qualifier in window, (
494	        f"qualifier {qualifier!r} must appear near activity {activity!r} in "

496	        f"activity:\n{window!r}"

532	    activity: str, token_a: str, token_b: str, context: dict
533	) -> None:
534	    content = context["template_content"]
535	    # Either: the explicit "Send `request_bugfix`" phrasing appears,
536	    # OR both message-type names appear somewhere in the content. The
537	    # latter satisfies the §3.2 activity entry "Send `request_bugfix` /
538	    # `request_maintenance`" without forcing the template to repeat the
539	    # "Send " prefix.
540	    if activity in content:
541	        return
542	    assert token_a in content and token_b in content, (
543	        f"template {context.get('shown_template_name')!r} must either name "
544	        f"activity {activity!r} OR mention both {token_a!r} and {token_b!r} "

549	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
550	# Given + Then steps — §3.2 activity guidance coverage (scenarios
551	# 9a9421ad59ee5d67 and 5ccb3fb1122f9341)
552	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
553	#
554	# The Given step stashes the activity list (literal phrasing as it
555	# appears in the dispatched scenario) on context. The Then step iterates
556	# that list and, for each activity, verifies either:
557	#   (a) the activity name appears on a line that also contains a colon
558	#       followed by non-whitespace prose (one-line guidance), OR
559	#   (b) the activity name appears as a subsection heading whose body
560	#       carries at least one sentence of prose, OR
561	#   (c) within a 240-byte window after the activity name the literal
562	#       phrase "guidance pending" appears (case-insensitive).
563	# The "no bare list item" assertion checks that no line of the form
564	# "- <activity>" or "* <activity>" exists without trailing colon/prose.
565	
566	
567	def _activity_block_satisfies_guidance(content: str, activity: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:
568	    """Return (ok, reason) for the activity-guidance check.
569	
570	    The check accepts any of three shapes:
571	      1. "guidance pending" within a 240-byte window after the activity
572	         name (case-insensitive).
573	      2. A line that names the activity AND contains a colon followed by
574	         non-whitespace prose on the same line — one-line guidance.
575	      3. A subsection heading (## or ### or ####) whose text names the
576	         activity, with at least one non-blank, non-heading line after
577	         it in the same subsection — multi-line guidance.
578	    """
579	    lower_content = content.lower()
580	    lower_activity = activity.lower()
581	
582	    # Shape 1: explicit "guidance pending" marker near the activity.
583	    idx = lower_content.find(lower_activity)
584	    while idx != -1:
585	        window = lower_content[idx : idx + 240 + len(lower_activity)]
586	        if "guidance pending" in window:
587	            return True, "guidance-pending marker present"
588	        idx = lower_content.find(lower_activity, idx + 1)
589	
590	    # Shapes 2 and 3: walk line by line.
591	    lines = content.splitlines()
592	    for i, line in enumerate(lines):
593	        if activity not in line:
594	            continue
595	        stripped = line.lstrip()
596	        # Shape 3: heading naming the activity.
597	        if stripped.startswith("#"):
598	            # Look for at least one non-blank, non-heading line after.
599	            for j in range(i + 1, len(lines)):
```

679	def then_no_bare_list_item(context: dict) -> None:

765	        f"'shop-msg' at byte {shop_msg_idx} in template "
766	        f"{context.get('shown_template_name')!r}"
767	    )
768	
769	
770	@then(
771	    parsers.re(
772	        r'^the first occurrence of the substring "shop-msg" in the content '
773	        r'appears after every §3\.2 (?P<role>PO|Architect) activity name '
774	        r'from scenario (?P<scen_idx>\d+) has appeared at least once$'
775	    )
776	)
777	def then_first_shop_msg_after_all_activities(
778	    role: str, scen_idx: str, context: dict
779	) -> None:
780	    content = context["template_content"]
781	    if role == "PO":
782	        activities = _LEAD_PO_ACTIVITY_NAMES_FROM_SCENARIO_10
783	    elif role == "Architect":
784	        activities = _LEAD_ARCHITECT_ACTIVITY_NAMES_FROM_SCENARIO_14
785	    else:
786	        raise AssertionError(f"unrecognized role {role!r}")
787	    shop_msg_idx = content.find("shop-msg")
788	    assert shop_msg_idx >= 0, (
789	        f"premise of Then violated: template "
790	        f"{context.get('shown_template_name')!r} has no 'shop-msg' substring"
791	    )
792	    missing: list[str] = []
793	    too_late: list[tuple[str, int, int]] = []
794	    for activity in activities:
795	        idx = content.find(activity)
796	        if idx < 0:
797	            missing.append(activity)
798	            continue
799	        if idx >= shop_msg_idx:
800	            too_late.append((activity, idx, shop_msg_idx))
801	    assert not missing, (
802	        f"premise of Then violated: template "
803	        f"{context.get('shown_template_name')!r} is missing §3.2 {role} "
804	        f"activity name(s): {missing!r}"
805	    )
806	    assert not too_late, (
807	        f"first 'shop-msg' at byte {shop_msg_idx} in template "
808	        f"{context.get('shown_template_name')!r} precedes §3.2 {role} "
809	        f"activity name(s) (each must appear at least once before):\n"
810	        + "\n".join(
811	            f"  - {a!r} first appears at byte {ai} (>= {smi})"
812	            for a, ai, smi in too_late
813	        )
814	    )
815	
816	
817	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
818	# Step definitions — inter-shop messaging encapsulation (lead-bwu)
819	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
820	#
821	# Scenarios 17-25 of features/inter_shop_messaging_encapsulation.feature
822	# pin two invariants across the four canonical templates and the two
823	# CLAUDE.md files of the shopsystem product:
824	#
825	#   1. CLI-only mailbox access: every prescribed inbox/outbox operation
826	#      names a `shop-msg` subcommand. No filesystem-direct inspection,
827	#      no filename-convention reasoning, no set-difference-on-filenames
828	#      characterization of "unprocessed".
829	#
830	#   2. bd-decoupling: bd participation is the local work-tracker
831	#      concern of each shop; no template or CLAUDE.md makes a bd
832	#      command a precondition for a shop-msg invocation, and no
833	#      template names `--bd-ref` (the flag has been replaced with the
834	#      optional, tracker-neutral `--provenance-ref`).
835	#
836	# Scenarios 21, 22, 25 reach OUTSIDE the BC root to read the lead-shop
837	# CLAUDE.md at <product>/CLAUDE.md and the BC's own CLAUDE.md at
838	# <product>/repos/shopsystem-templates/CLAUDE.md. The lead's dispatch
839	# (lead-bwu) explicitly names both files as in-scope surfaces — this
840	# read is sanctioned by the message.
841	
842	
843	def _product_root() -> Path:
844	    """Return the lead-shop (product) root directory.
845	
846	    The BC root for shopsystem-templates is two levels below the product
847	    root: <product>/repos/shopsystem-templates. Resolve by walking up
848	    from this file (tests/conftest.py inside the BC) two more levels.
849	    """
850	    return Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent.parent.parent
851	
852	
853	def _read_file_at_product_path(relative_path: str) -> str:
854	    """Read a file path that is given relative to the product root.
855	
856	    The scenarios spell paths as "CLAUDE.md" (lead shop) and
857	    "repos/shopsystem-templates/CLAUDE.md" (BC). Both anchor at the
858	    product root.
859	    """
860	    target = _product_root() / relative_path
861	    return target.read_text()
862	
863	
864	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
865	# Given steps — file-at-path premise (scenarios 21, 22, 25)
866	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
867	
868	
869	@given(
870	    parsers.parse(
871	        'the file at "{relative_path}" exists and is the canonical CLAUDE.md '
872	        'for the shopsystem-templates BC shop'
873	    )
874	)
875	def given_file_at_path_is_bc_claude_md(relative_path: str, context: dict) -> None:
876	    target = _product_root() / relative_path
877	    assert target.exists(), (
878	        f"premise of Given violated: file {target!s} does not exist"
879	    )
880	    context["file_path"] = str(target)
881	    context["file_relative_path"] = relative_path
882	
883	
884	@given(
885	    parsers.parse(
886	        'the file at "{relative_path}" exists at the lead shop\'s repository '
887	        'root and describes the lead shop\'s router behavior and '
888	        'feature-request handling'
889	    )
890	)
891	def given_file_at_path_is_lead_claude_md(relative_path: str, context: dict) -> None:
892	    target = _product_root() / relative_path
893	    assert target.exists(), (
894	        f"premise of Given violated: file {target!s} does not exist"
895	    )
896	    context["file_path"] = str(target)
897	    context["file_relative_path"] = relative_path
898	
899	
900	@given(
901	    parsers.parse(
902	        'the file at {relative_path} exists and is the canonical CLAUDE.md '
903	        'for the named shop'
904	    )
905	)
906	def given_file_at_path_is_named_shop_claude_md(
907	    relative_path: str, context: dict
908	) -> None:
909	    # Scenario Outline (scenario 25) substitutes the path from the
910	    # Examples table. The path arrives without surrounding quotes from
911	    # pytest-bdd's outline substitution, so strip optional whitespace.
912	    relative_path = relative_path.strip()
913	    target = _product_root() / relative_path
914	    assert target.exists(), (
915	        f"premise of Given violated: file {target!s} does not exist"
916	    )
917	    context["file_path"] = str(target)
918	    context["file_relative_path"] = relative_path

Baseline is green when PATH is set correctly. Now let me plan the implementation:

**Implementation plan:**

1. **New template files** (package data): `templates/CLAUDE.bc.md` and `templates/CLAUDE.lead.md` (CLAUDE.md primer per shop_type), plus `templates/gitignore.md` for the shared .gitignore.

2. **Public template-access surface**: Per scenario `0cce58eb573d3c91`, ask "the shop-templates package for the canonical CLAUDE.md primer template for shop type X through its public template-access surface". I'll add `read_claude_md_primer(shop_type)` (and `read_gitignore()`) as public surface in `cli.py` or a new module, plus a way to list canonical role set for a shop type.

3. **`shop-templates list`** scenarios mention "shop-templates list filtered to those that the bootstrap surface treats as bc roles". I need a programmatic role-set surface (e.g. `_canonical_role_set(shop_type)`). The existing `list` lists all 4 names; for now, "bc" → ["bc-implementer", "bc-reviewer"], "lead" → ["lead-po", "lead-architect"].

4. **`shop-templates bootstrap`** subcommand: args `--shop-type {bc,lead}`, `--shop-name NAME`, `--target DIR`. (Need to pick concrete argument shape — the scenarios say "with shop type X, shop name Y, and target directory Z". I'll use positional-style: `shop-templates bootstrap --shop-type bc --shop-name foo --target /tmp/foo` — this is non-prompting. The scenarios also pin: missing shop_type → non-zero exit with stderr identifying the missing arg AND listing accepted values "bc" and "lead".

5. **`shop-templates update`** subcommand: arg `--target DIR`. Re-pours `.claude/agents/*.md` only. Does NOT touch CLAUDE.md, .gitignore, .beads/.

6. **bd init subprocess**: bootstrap must invoke `bd init` as a subprocess; must NOT import bd/beads in-process. Scenario also says "during the invocation no symbol from a Python module whose top-level package name is 'bd' or 'beads' was imported into the running 'shop-templates' process". I need to ensure no such imports happen.

7. **Shop identity persistence**: scenarios reference "a target directory that was previously bootstrapped as a 'bc' shop named 'example-shop'". Update needs to know the shop type. I'll persist `.claude/shop.json` (or similar) at bootstrap. Wait, the scenarios only mention specific files — `.claude/agents/`, `.beads/`, `CLAUDE.md`, `.gitignore`. # Lead shop — shopsystem product

You are operating in the **lead shop** of the shopsystem product. The product
is the shopsystem framework itself; this repo is its outward face.

## Who you are — router for PO and Architect subagents

By default you are the **router** for the lead shop. The two judgment roles
— **PO** and **Architect** per [§3](03-lead-shop.md) — are dispatched as
subagents (per [`pdr/002`](pdr/002-lead-shop-roles-as-subagents.md)). Your
job is to classify each request and delegate; do not enact the roles
yourself.

- **Dispatch to the `lead-po` subagent** when the request requires:
  authoring or sharpening Gherkin scenarios; drafting briefs or PDRs;
  responding to BC `clarify` on scope or vocabulary.
- **Dispatch to the `lead-architect` subagent** when the request requires:
  selecting a message-type vehicle; composing `shop-msg send`; verifying
  BC pre-state empirically; responding to BC `clarify` on architecture;
  reconciling scenario registers; drafting ADRs; BC decomposition
---

# Inter-shop message runtime artifacts (per-message YAML; not source).

/outbox/

```md
# {{SHOP_NAME}} — BC shop instructions

This repository is the **{{SHOP_NAME}}** Bounded Context shop. As an agent
operating in this repo, you are operating inside a **BC shop** that uses
the inbox/outbox message protocol from §4 of the shop-system spec.

## Who you are — router for bc-implementer and bc-reviewer subagents

By default you are the **router** for this BC shop. The two role-discipline
positions — **Implementer** and **Reviewer** per the shop-system spec §4 /
§4.4 — are dispatched as subagents. Your job is to classify each request
and delegate; do not enact the roles yourself.

The canonical role set for this shop type is:

- **bc-implementer** — reads inbox messages, applies the sufficiency check
  matching the message type, and either emits `clarify` via `shop-msg
  respond clarify` or does the work (feature file under `features/`, step
  defs in `tests/conftest.py`, implementation under `src/`, BDD passing).

- **bc-reviewer** — dispatched AFTER the implementer's turn on an
  `assign_scenarios` (or scenario-carrying `request_bugfix`) message has
  finished and the BC is in its post-work state with no outbox file yet.
  The reviewer is the sole role authorized to emit `work_done` for
  scenario-based work.

Subagent definitions live at `.claude/agents/bc-implementer.md` and
`.claude/agents/bc-reviewer.md`. These are inline copies of the canonical
templates shipped by the `shopsystem-templates` BC; do not edit them
independently of the canonical source.

## BC inbox / outbox protocol

- **Inbox** (`inbox/`) holds messages from the lead shop. Filename
  convention: `<work_id>.yaml`. One file per dispatch.
- **Outbox** (`outbox/`) holds this BC's responses. Filename convention:
  `<work_id>-<response_type>.yaml`. The `shop-msg respond` CLI builds
  and validates these — never write outbox YAML by hand.
- A message is considered **unprocessed** when there is no outbox file
  for its `work_id`. Use `shop-msg pending inbox` to identify
  unprocessed work, and `shop-msg read inbox` to read a specific
  inbox message.

## Do not

- **No editing the BC's inbox/outbox by hand.** `shop-msg send` writes
  inboxes (lead shop's job); `shop-msg respond` writes outboxes
  (BC's job). Both validate against the schema.
- **No skipping the sufficiency check.** Each BC role template carries
  a sufficiency check matching the inbound message type; honor it.

```

```md
# {{SHOP_NAME}} — lead shop instructions

This repository is the **{{SHOP_NAME}}** lead shop. As an agent operating
in this repo, you are operating inside a **lead shop** under the shop-system
spec.

## Who you are — router for lead-po and lead-architect subagents

By default you are the **router** for this lead shop. The two judgment
roles — **PO** and **Architect** per §3 of the shop-system spec — are
dispatched as subagents. Your job is to classify each request and
delegate; do not enact the roles yourself.

The canonical role set for this shop type is:

- **lead-po** — authors or sharpens Gherkin scenarios; drafts briefs or
  PDRs; responds to BC `clarify` on scope or vocabulary.

- **lead-architect** — selects a message-type vehicle; composes
  `shop-msg send`; verifies BC pre-state empirically; responds to BC
  `clarify` on architecture; reconciles scenario registers; drafts ADRs;
  makes BC decomposition decisions.

Subagent definitions live at `.claude/agents/lead-po.md` and
`.claude/agents/lead-architect.md`. These are inline copies of the
canonical templates shipped by the `shopsystem-templates` BC; do not
edit them independently of the canonical source.

## How feature requests get handled

When a user request implies a new BC capability, a tightening, or a flat
change to an existing BC:

1. **PO authors intent first.** Brief → PDR → Gherkin scenarios.
   Implementation discussion before authored scenarios is the failure
   mode §3 exists to prevent.
2. **Architect verifies the relevant BC's pre-state empirically.**
   Reading the BC's code is hypothesis; running it is fact. Construct a
   concrete input that exhibits (or fails to exhibit) the behavior;
   observe; cite the demonstration in the dispatch description.
3. **Architect applies the message-type discriminator:**
   - No capability → `assign_scenarios`.
   - Capability exists but unpinned → `request_bugfix`.
   - Flat (refactor / doc / value-only) → `request_maintenance`.
4. **Architect dispatches via `shop-msg send`.** Never write inbox/outbox
   YAML by hand.

## Do not

- **No implementation code in this repo.** This is the lead shop; code
  lives in BCs.
- **No skipping the discriminator.** If a request smells like open-ended
  "what should we build?" — route it back through the process. First
  question: *what scenarios pin this?*

```

```python
"""shop-templates CLI entry point.

Subcommands:
    list
        Print available role-template names (one per line) to stdout.
    show <name>
        Print the named template's content to stdout. Exits non-zero
        with a stderr message when no template matches that name.
    bootstrap --shop-type {bc,lead} --shop-name NAME --target DIR
        Scaffold a new (existing-but-empty) shop repository at DIR:
        write the canonical role-prompt copies for the shop type under
        .claude/agents/, write a CLAUDE.md primer for the shop type,
        write a top-level .gitignore, and invoke `bd init` as a
        subprocess to initialize .beads/.
    update --target DIR --shop-type {bc,lead}
        Re-pour the bootstrap-managed agent files in DIR/.claude/agents/
        from the current canonical package data. Reconcile against the
        canonical role set for the shop type — add canonical files that
        are missing, remove managed files whose canonical template no
        longer exists. Does NOT touch CLAUDE.md, .gitignore, or .beads/.

Role-prompt templates live in the package as `templates/<name>.md` data
files. CLAUDE.md primer templates live as `templates/claude/<shop_type>.md`.
The .gitignore template lives as `templates/gitignore.template`. The CLI
is the public boundary; dispatchers, scaffold scripts, and any other
consumer reads templates via this surface rather than by path.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import subprocess
import sys
from importlib.resources import files
from pathlib import Path


_TEMPLATES_PKG = "shop_templates.templates"
_CLAUDE_TEMPLATES_PKG = "shop_templates.templates.claude"

# The canonical role-set assignment per shop type. The bootstrap surface
# uses this to decide which role files to pour into .claude/agents/ for
# a freshly-bootstrapped shop, and the update surface uses it to
# reconcile an existing shop's .claude/agents/ against the current
# canonical set.
_CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS: dict[str, tuple[str, ...]] = {
    "bc": ("bc-implementer", "bc-reviewer"),
    "lead": ("lead-po", "lead-architect"),
}

_SHOP_TYPES = tuple(_CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS.keys())


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Public template-access surface (importable; not just CLI-internal).
#
# Per scenario 0cce58eb573d3c91, the CLAUDE.md primer template must be
# accessible "through its public template-access surface" and must NOT
# be read from any path under the product's top-level working directory
# at lookup time — i.e., it must be served from importlib.resources
# package data, the same boundary the role-prompt templates use.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------


def _list_template_names() -> list[str]:
    """Return the sorted list of available role-template names.

    Walks only the top-level package directory; sub-directories (e.g.
    `templates/claude/`) are skipped. This keeps `shop-templates list`
    output stable as new categories of package data (CLAUDE primers,
    gitignore template) are added.
    """
    pkg_root = files(_TEMPLATES_PKG)
    names = []
    for resource in pkg_root.iterdir():
        # `is_file()` plus `.md` suffix isolates role-prompt templates
        # from sub-directories like `templates/claude/`.
        if not resource.is_file():
            continue
        name = resource.name
        if name.endswith(".md"):
            names.append(name[: -len(".md")])
    return sorted(names)


def _read_template(name: str) -> str | None:
    """Return the named role-template's content, or None if no such template."""
    try:
        return (files(_TEMPLATES_PKG) / f"{name}.md").read_text()
    except FileNotFoundError:
        return None


def read_claude_md_primer(shop_type: str) -> str:
    """Return the canonical CLAUDE.md primer template body for a shop type.

    The body is loaded from package data via importlib.resources; it is
    never read from a filesystem path under the product working
    directory. The returned string is the source of truth from which the
    bootstrap entry point generates a target directory's top-level
    CLAUDE.md.
    """
    if shop_type not in _CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS:
        raise ValueError(
            f"unknown shop type {shop_type!r}; accepted values: "
            f"{', '.join(_SHOP_TYPES)}"
        )
    resource = files(_CLAUDE_TEMPLATES_PKG) / f"{shop_type}.md"
    return resource.read_text()


def read_gitignore_template() -> str:
    """Return the canonical top-level .gitignore template body."""
    return (files(_TEMPLATES_PKG) / "gitignore.template").read_text()


def canonical_role_set(shop_type: str) -> tuple[str, ...]:
    """Return the canonical role-prompt-template names for a shop type.

    The bootstrap surface treats these names as the canonical role set
    for the given shop type — both `bootstrap` (initial pour) and
    `update` (reconciliation) work from this mapping.
    """
    if shop_type not in _CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS:
        raise ValueError(
            f"unknown shop type {shop_type!r}; accepted values: "
            f"{', '.join(_SHOP_TYPES)}"
        )
    return _CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS[shop_type]


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# `list` / `show` subcommands — pre-existing surface.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------


def _cmd_list(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
    for name in _list_template_names():
        print(name)
    return 0


def _cmd_show(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
    content = _read_template(args.name)
    if content is None:
        available = ", ".join(_list_template_names())
        print(
            f"shop-templates show: no template named {args.name!r}. Available: {available}",
            file=sys.stderr,
        )
        return 1
    # Use sys.stdout.write to preserve exact bytes (no trailing newline from print).
    sys.stdout.write(content)
    return 0


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# `bootstrap` subcommand
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------


def _render_claude_md(shop_type: str, shop_name: str) -> str:
    """Render the CLAUDE.md primer for a shop type by substituting the
    shop name. The primer must name the shop's identity (its shop_name
    and its role set) per scenario a15dac2f87549b8a.
    """
    primer = read_claude_md_primer(shop_type)
    return primer.replace("{{SHOP_NAME}}", shop_name)


def _bd_init_in(target: Path) -> int:
    """Invoke `bd init` as a subprocess in the target directory.

    Per scenario 0c6f1c5d9bc4226e, .beads/ MUST be initialized by a
    subprocess named "bd" with first argument "init", NOT by importing
    bd / beads internals into the running shop-templates process and
    NOT by writing .beads/ files from shop-templates directly. This
    function is the single place where that subprocess is spawned.
    """
    result = subprocess.run(
        ["bd", "init"],
        cwd=str(target),
        capture_output=True,
        text=True,
    )
    if result.returncode != 0:
        print(
            f"shop-templates bootstrap: `bd init` failed in {target!s} "
            f"(exit {result.returncode}); stderr:\n{result.stderr}",
            file=sys.stderr,
        )
    return result.returncode


def _validate_shop_type(shop_type: str | None, command: str) -> str | None:
    """Return None if shop_type is valid; else print a usage diagnostic to
    stderr and return an error sentinel string usable by callers.

    The diagnostic shape is pinned by scenarios d6e935bca62d0039 (missing
    shop type) and 7941cdc591cd2c3b (invalid shop type): stderr names
    the offending input AND lists the accepted values "bc" and "lead".
    """
    accepted = ", ".join(f'"{s}"' for s in _SHOP_TYPES)
    if shop_type is None:
        print(
            f"shop-templates {command}: missing required argument: shop type. "
            f"Accepted values: {accepted}.",
            file=sys.stderr,
        )
        return "missing"
    if shop_type not in _CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS:
        print(
            f"shop-templates {command}: invalid shop type {shop_type!r}. "
            f"Accepted values: {accepted}.",
            file=sys.stderr,
        )
        return "invalid"
    return None


def _cmd_bootstrap(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
    # Validate shop_type early so we can emit the usage diagnostic shape
    # the scenarios pin (named offending input + list of accepted values)
    # BEFORE touching the target directory. Scenarios d6e935bca62d0039
    # and 7941cdc591cd2c3b both require "writes no scaffold" when the
    # shop type is bad.
    err = _validate_shop_type(args.shop_type, "bootstrap")
    if err is not None:
        return 2

    target = Path(args.target)
    shop_type: str = args.shop_type
    shop_name: str = args.shop_name

    # Pour role-prompt files into .claude/agents/.
    agents_dir = target / ".claude" / "agents"
    agents_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    for role_name in _CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS[shop_type]:
        body = _read_template(role_name)
        if body is None:
            print(
                f"shop-templates bootstrap: internal error — canonical "
                f"role template {role_name!r} not found in package data",
                file=sys.stderr,
            )
            return 1
        (agents_dir / f"{role_name}.md").write_text(body)

    # Top-level CLAUDE.md — render the primer with the shop's identity.
    (target / "CLAUDE.md").write_text(_render_claude_md(shop_type, shop_name))

    # Top-level .gitignore — pour the canonical template.
    (target / ".gitignore").write_text(read_gitignore_template())

    # Initialize .beads/ via a `bd init` subprocess. shop-templates MUST
    # NOT import bd / beads internals and MUST NOT write to .beads/
    # directly (scenario 0c6f1c5d9bc4226e).
    rc = _bd_init_in(target)
    if rc != 0:
        return rc

    return 0


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# `update` subcommand
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------


def _cmd_update(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
    # Update requires shop_type as an explicit argument so the
    # reconciliation step (scenario 03b4e3fa31d72031) can compare the
    # current contents of .claude/agents/ against the current canonical
    # role set for the right shop type. Inferring the shop type from
    # .claude/agents/ contents is unsafe: a hand-mutated shop may have
    # zero files whose names are in either canonical set.
    err = _validate_shop_type(args.shop_type, "update")
    if err is not None:
        return 2

    target = Path(args.target)
    shop_type: str = args.shop_type
    agents_dir = target / ".claude" / "agents"
    agents_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    canonical = set(_CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS[shop_type])

    # The "naming convention" for bootstrap-managed agent files is:
    # filename = <role-name>.md where <role-name> is a name in the
    # canonical role set of SOME shop type (current or historical).
    # For reconciliation we treat any .md file in .claude/agents/ as a
    # candidate managed file; if its name is in the current canonical
    # role set for this shop type it stays (and gets re-poured); if
    # its name is in NEITHER canonical role set we leave it alone
    # (shop-authored content we don't recognize); if it matches a
    # canonical name from any role set (including the other shop
    # type's set, or a name previously in this set but no longer) we
    # remove it. This satisfies scenario 03b4e3fa31d72031's "remove
    # managed files whose canonical template no longer exists" while
    # leaving shop-authored files alone.
    all_canonical_names = set()
    for rs in _CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS.values():
        all_canonical_names.update(rs)
    # A file is "managed" if its base name (sans .md) is in the
    # historical set OR is a current canonical name we're about to
    # pour. For this implementation, treat managed = any .md file
    # whose name follows the role-prompt-template naming convention,
    # which we recognize via the heuristic: name contains "-" and is
    # all lowercase ASCII. That keeps shop-authored files like
    # "TODO.md" or "NOTES.md" out.
    def _is_managed_role_name(stem: str) -> bool:
        # Recognize names of the form "<prefix>-<suffix>" where both
        # halves are ASCII lowercase letters. This matches every
        # canonical role-name pattern shipped today (bc-implementer,
        # bc-reviewer, lead-po, lead-architect) and excludes typical
        # shop-authored markdown filenames.
        if "-" not in stem:
            return False
        if stem != stem.lower():
            return False
        if not all(c.islower() or c == "-" for c in stem):
            return False
        return True

    # Step 1: remove managed files whose names are NOT in the current
    # canonical role set for this shop type.
    for child in list(agents_dir.iterdir()):
        if not child.is_file():
            continue
        if not child.name.endswith(".md"):
            continue
        stem = child.name[:-len(".md")]
        if stem in canonical:
            continue
        if _is_managed_role_name(stem):
            child.unlink()

    # Step 2: re-pour every canonical role file. If a file is already
    # byte-equal to the canonical content, leave its on-disk
    # mtime alone (scenario 264322ae65312bc7).
    for role_name in canonical:
        body = _read_template(role_name)
        if body is None:
            print(
                f"shop-templates update: internal error — canonical "
                f"role template {role_name!r} not found in package data",
                file=sys.stderr,
            )
            return 1
        target_file = agents_dir / f"{role_name}.md"
        if target_file.exists():
            current = target_file.read_text()
            if current == body:
                continue
        target_file.write_text(body)

    # Per scenarios 56a0ac7107ba5c15, ca0f0a249d025267, 3f4d7d2256a97ae7,
    # and efae77e534588357: update does NOT touch CLAUDE.md, .gitignore,
    # .beads/, or any path outside .claude/agents/.
    return 0


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Argparse wiring
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------


def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(prog="shop-templates")
    sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", required=True)

    list_cmd = sub.add_parser("list", help="list available template names")
    list_cmd.set_defaults(func=_cmd_list)

    show_cmd = sub.add_parser("show", help="print a named template to stdout")
    show_cmd.add_argument("name", help="template name (e.g. bc-implementer)")
    show_cmd.set_defaults(func=_cmd_show)

    bootstrap_cmd = sub.add_parser(
        "bootstrap",
        help="scaffold an existing repo with the canonical shop structure",
    )
    # `--shop-type` intentionally has no default. Scenario d6e935bca62d0039
    # requires that omitting it produces a non-zero exit and a usage
    # diagnostic naming the missing argument AND the accepted values.
    # We declare it as a regular optional argument (not required=True at
    # the argparse layer) so argparse does NOT short-circuit with its
    # own auto-generated "the following arguments are required:..."
    # message — we want our own diagnostic shape on stderr.
    bootstrap_cmd.add_argument(
        "--shop-type",
        choices=None,  # validate manually so error shape is ours, not argparse's
        default=None,
        help='one of "bc" or "lead"',
    )
    bootstrap_cmd.add_argument(
        "--shop-name",
        default=None,
        help="the shop's own name, e.g. shopsystem-messaging",
    )
    bootstrap_cmd.add_argument(
        "--target",
        default=None,
        help="path to the existing repository to scaffold",
    )
    bootstrap_cmd.set_defaults(func=_cmd_bootstrap)

    update_cmd = sub.add_parser(
        "update",
        help="re-pour bootstrap-managed agent files from current package data",
    )
    update_cmd.add_argument(
        "--target",
        default=None,
        help="path to the previously-bootstrapped repository",
    )
    update_cmd.add_argument(
        "--shop-type",
        default=None,
        help='one of "bc" or "lead" (must match the original bootstrap)',
    )
    update_cmd.set_defaults(func=_cmd_update)

    return parser


def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
    return args.func(args)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main())

```

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-13 13:27:26Z)**_

Now I need to plan the bootstrap-scenarios step definitions carefully. Key concerns:

1. **Isolated target directories per scenario.** Scenarios use `/tmp/example-shop`, `/tmp/example-bc-shop`, `/tmp/example-lead-shop`. Multiple scenarios use the same path. Each scenario must start fresh — I'll redirect each named target to a fresh pytest tmp dir.

2. **Tracking subprocess names for "during the invocation".** Scenarios `0c6f1c5d9bc4226e` and `3f4d7d2256a97ae7` assert that `bd` subprocess was/wasn't invoked. To observe this from a black-box CLI invocation, I'll wrap the test's invocation in a subprocess sandbox: prepend a fake `bd` script that logs invocations to a temp file, then check the log. (This also gives "did `bd` get called" answer.) Need to put a fake `bd` on PATH that intercepts and forwards to real `bd` (for bootstrap to actually init beads).

   Actually scenarios `0c6f1c5d9bc4226e` requires:
   - "a subprocess named `bd` was executed with first argument `init`"
   - "no symbol from a Python module whose top-level package name is `bd` or `beads` was imported into the running shop-templates process"
   - ".beads/ contents were written by that `bd` subprocess and not by file-writes issued directly from the shop-templates process"

   For "no `bd`/`beads` imports": I'll launch shop-templates with a tracer or check `sys.modules` post hoc. But shop-templates runs in its own subprocess. I can inject a tracing env var, e.g. run via `python -m shop_templates --import-trace=/path/to/log` — but that requires CLI cooperation. Simpler: spawn shop-templates via `python -c "import sys; ...; import shop_templates.cli as c; sys.exit(c.main(...)); import bd or beads check"`. That doesn't quite work.

   Actually I can run shop-templates via the venv's python with PYTHONTRACEMALLOC or similar. Simplest: invoke shop-templates by spawning a small python wrapper that runs `shop_templates.cli.main()` and afterward checks `'bd' in sys.modules or 'beads' in sys.modules` and writes the result to a log. Then the test reads that log.

3. **For "no subprocess named bd was executed" during update**: use a fake `bd` shim that logs calls and check that the log is empty.

Both these checks can use the same approach: a temp directory containing a shim `bd` script; prepend to PATH; the script logs invocations to a file. Then I check the log to see whether `bd` was invoked with `init`. For bootstrap, the shim must also create `.beads/` to satisfy "the target directory contains a .beads/ directory". I'll have the shim create `.beads/` minimally (mkdir + dummy file).

Let me now write the step definitions. I'll structure them carefully.

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-13 13:30:46Z)**_

Down to 2 failures. Let me analyze each:

**Failure 1**: scenario `03b4e3fa31d72031`. The scenario uses Outline-style placeholders `<former-bc-role>` and `<new-bc-role>` — but it's a regular Scenario, NOT a Scenario Outline. There's no Examples table to substitute. The literal text `<new-bc-role>` is what the step receives. The scenario is actually pinning a *parametric* behavior without an Examples table — the placeholders here are descriptive placeholders, not Gherkin outline substitutions.

This is a sufficiency-check concern actually. Re-reading scenario `03b4e3fa31d72031`:

```
Scenario: update brings the set of bootstrap-managed agent files ... — adding canonical files that are missing and removing managed files whose canonical template no longer exists
  Given an existing git repository at a target directory "/tmp/example-shop" that was previously bootstrapped as a "bc" shop named "example-shop"
  And the current canonical role set for shop type "bc" is exactly the names listed by "shop-templates list" filtered to those that the bootstrap surface treats as "bc" roles
  And the directory ".claude/agents/" in the target directory contains a file named "<former-bc-role>.md" whose name is not in that current canonical role set
  And the directory ".claude/agents/" in the target directory does not contain a file named "<new-bc-role>.md" whose name is in that current canonical role set
```

The `<former-bc-role>` and `<new-bc-role>` are literal placeholders — meant for the implementer/reader to substitute values. There's no Examples table. The scenario step text actually arrives with the literal `<...>` brackets. This makes the scenario ambiguous on real values, but it's a meaningful behavioral pin: *for any* former-bc-role and *any* new-bc-role meeting those constraints, update should converge.

The Given step needs to bind sensible values. I'll have the Given step interpret these as opaque names and pick real concrete substitutes. But there's a tension: the second Given says "<new-bc-role>.md whose name IS in that current canonical role set" — and the third Given for "<former-bc-role>.md whose name IS NOT". So the implementation needs concrete values where one is in the canonical set and the other is not.

A clean way: introduce a temporary deviation in the canonical set. But we can't change the canonical role set at runtime easily. Simpler: pick `<former-bc-role>` = "bc-legacy-deprecated" (not in current canonical), and `<new-bc-role>` = "bc-implementer" (in current canonical). Then the test step verifies: after update, `bc-legacy-deprecated.md` is gone, `bc-implementer.md` is present and canonical. That's a faithful test of the scenario's intent.

Let me update the Given steps to substitute the literal `<former-bc-role>` and `<new-bc-role>` placeholders for concrete values.

# Report

## Sufficiency decision

Applied `assign_scenarios` sufficiency check to `inbox/lead-k8v.yaml`. All four conditions met:

1. **Well-formed Gherkin**: All 20 scenarios have Given/When/Then; outline scenarios have Examples tables.
2. **Concrete steps**: Every step is concrete (byte-for-byte equality, exit codes, subprocess observation, presence/absence of named paths). No vague phrasing.
3. **`@scenario_hash` tag present** on each.
4. **BC pre-state check (genuinely net-new)**: Empirically verified via `/workspaces/shopsystem-product/.venv/bin/shop-templates bootstrap` → exit 2 with "invalid choice: 'bootstrap' (choose from list, show)". No prior bootstrap/update surface; `templates/` only had four role-prompt files; no CLAUDE.md primer surface. Not a redundant pin — this is genuine new behavior.

Proceeded to work.

## Work done

**Feature file** added:
- `/workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-templates/features/bootstrap_cli_surface.feature` — all 20 dispatched scenarios verbatim with hash tags preserved.

**Step definitions** appended at the bottom of:
- `/workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-templates/tests/conftest.py` — new section "Step definitions — bootstrap_cli_surface.feature (lead-k8v dispatch)". Adds Givens for fresh per-test target dirs, Whens that invoke the CLI inside a sandbox with a fake `bd` shim (so `bd`-subprocess presence/absence is observable) and via a python wrapper that records `sys.modules` post-invocation (so the "no `bd`/`beads` imports" assertion is grounded). Also adds `import sys` to module imports.

**Package data** (new template surfaces):
- `/workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/templates/claude/bc.md` — BC-shop CLAUDE.md primer (names the bc-implementer and bc-reviewer roles; uses `{{SHOP_NAME}}` substitution marker).
- `/workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/templates/claude/lead.md` — lead-shop CLAUDE.md primer (names lead-po and lead-architect).
- `/workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/templates/gitignore.template` — canonical top-level `.gitignore`.

**Implementation** (rewrote):
- `/workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/cli.py` — adds `bootstrap` and `update` subcommands plus a public template-access surface (`read_claude_md_primer`, `read_gitignore_template`, `canonical_role_set`). Bootstrap invokes `bd init` via `subprocess.run(["bd", "init"], cwd=target)` — never imports bd/beads. Update reconciles `.claude/agents/` against `canonical_role_set(shop_type)`, leaves CLAUDE.md, `.gitignore`, `.beads/`, and shop-authored paths untouched. `_list_template_names()` was tightened to skip sub-directories (so the new `claude/` subdir doesn't pollute `shop-templates list`'s 4-name output).

**Build wiring**:
- `/workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-templates/pyproject.toml` — `[tool.setuptools.package-data]` now also includes `templates/claude/*.md` and `templates/gitignore.template`.

## Reviewer-facing notes

- BDD: `PATH=/workspaces/shopsystem-product/.venv/bin:$PATH python3 -m pytest tests/ -W ignore` → 97 passed (70 pre-existing + 27 new test instantiations, covering all 20 dispatched scenarios after Outline expansion).
- End-to-end smoke: `shop-templates bootstrap --shop-type bc --shop-name test --target /tmp/.../bc-test` succeeds with a real `bd init` and writes `.claude/agents/{bc-implementer,bc-reviewer}.md`, top-level CLAUDE.md (with `test` substituted into `{{SHOP_NAME}}`), `.gitignore`, and `.beads/` (initialized by `bd init` itself). `bd init` also creates `AGENTS.md` as its own side effect — not pinned by any scenario (and not in the explicit out-of-scope list `69496ea1e5c82ed3`).
- **No `work_done` outbox file written for `lead-k8v`** — per role contract, the Reviewer holds that gate for `assign_scenarios` work. The BC is left in its post-work state for review.

## Mechanism observations

None this dispatch. The schema, sufficiency-check shape, and dispatched scenario set were all load-bearing-and-in-scope; nothing surfaced that a future BC dispatch would need to know.

One small implementation note worth flagging to the Reviewer (not a mechanism observation — it's a property of this work item): scenario `03b4e3fa31d72031` uses literal `<former-bc-role>.md` / `<new-bc-role>.md` tokens in a regular Scenario (no Examples table). My step bindings substitute these with concrete values — `bc-legacy-deprecated` (former, not in canonical bc) and `bc-implementer` (new, in canonical bc). The Reviewer may want to scrutinize whether this faithfully exercises the scenario's "removes managed files whose canonical template no longer exists" intent.

## Report

**Outcome**: `clarify` emitted — scenario gap escalated.

**BDD re-run**: 97 passed, 0 failed (`source /workspaces/shopsystem-product/.venv/bin/activate && python -m pytest tests/`). The implementer's "97 passed" claim is correct WHEN the venv is on PATH; a naive `python3 -m pytest tests/` fails 33 tests because `shop-templates` is not on PATH — that's a test-env trap, not a regression.

**Adversarial probes**:
1. **No bd/beads import (`0c6f1c5d9bc4226e`)** — verified teeth: injected `sys.modules["bd"] = ...` into `cli.py`; the targeted test FAILED. Reverted clean. Real gap, not a real gap.
2. **`{{SHOP_NAME}}` substitution (`a15dac2f87549b8a`)** — verified teeth: replaced `primer.replace("{{SHOP_NAME}}", shop_name)` with `return primer`; both Examples failed (substring not in CLAUDE.md). Reverted clean. Real gap, not a real gap.
3. **Update leaves CLAUDE.md untouched (`56a0ac7107ba5c15`)** — verified teeth: injected a CLAUDE.md write into `_cmd_update`; targeted test FAILED. Reverted clean. Real gap, not a real gap.
4. **Update emits no bd subprocess (`3f4d7d2256a97ae7`)** — verified teeth: injected `subprocess.run(["bd", "status"])` into `_cmd_update`; targeted test FAILED. Reverted clean. Real gap, not a real gap.
5. **Tag preservation** — all 20 scenarios carry `@scenario_hash:<hash>` and `@bc:shopsystem-templates` byte-for-byte; verified by direct grep.
6. **`shop-templates list` output** — confirmed exactly 4 names sorted; `claude/` subdir and `gitignore.template` correctly excluded.
7. **Implementer's `03b4e3fa31d72031` placeholder binding** (`former-bc-role=bc-legacy-deprecated`, `new-bc-role=bc-implementer`) — load-bearing and consistent: the binding mirrors the canonical naming convention so the implementation's `_is_managed_role_name` heuristic recognizes it; satisfies the scenario's "removes managed files whose canonical template no longer exists" intent. Dismissed as out-of-scope for clarify.

**Probe that surfaced a real gap (scenario `0f50666b7e86f1ba` / bootstrap side-effect contract)**: Real `bd init` (verified empirically in a tmp git repo at `/tmp/...`) creates a top-level `AGENTS.md`, `.claude/settings.json`, APPENDS a `<!-- BEGIN BEADS INTEGRATION -->` block to any pre-existing CLAUDE.md, and APPENDS `.dolt/`, `*.db`, `.beads-credential-key` to `.gitignore`. The in-suite bd-shim writes only `.beads/__shim_init__`, so the test does not exercise these side effects. The dispatched scenarios pin neither (a) the presence/absence of `AGENTS.md`, nor (b) whether the post-bootstrap CLAUDE.md is exactly the primer body or primer + bd-integration-block.

**Proposed scenario tightening in `clarify`**: pin the bd-init side-effect contract — either commit to "post-bootstrap target contains a top-level `AGENTS.md`" (preserve bd's outputs) or commit to "post-bootstrap target contains no top-level `AGENTS.md`" (shop-templates suppresses it post-init); plus a Then step pinning whether CLAUDE.md after bootstrap equals the primer body or primer + bd-integration-block.

**Outbox file**: `/workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-templates/outbox/lead-k8v-clarify.yaml`.

    \ | shop_name               | target                       | role_a          \
    \  | role_b            |\n    | bc        | shopsystem-messaging    | /tmp/example-bc-shop\
    \         | bc-implementer    | bc-reviewer       |\n    | lead      | shopsystem-product\
    \      | /tmp/example-lead-shop       | lead-po           | lead-architect   \
    \ |\n\n"

  gherkin: "Feature: shop-templates bootstrap CLI surface (brief 002, items A-E)\n\

    \ initializes the \".beads/\" directory in the target directory by invoking \"\
    bd init\" as a subprocess, not by importing or reproducing bd's internal behavior\
    \ in-process\n  Given an existing git repository at a target directory \"/tmp/example-shop\"\
    \ with no \".beads/\" directory\n  When I invoke the \"shop-templates\" bootstrap\
    \ entry point with shop type \"bc\", shop name \"example-shop\", and target directory\
    \ \"/tmp/example-shop\"\n  Then the exit code is 0\n  And the target directory\
    \ contains a \".beads/\" directory\n  And during the invocation a subprocess named\
    \ \"bd\" was executed with first argument \"init\"\n  And during the invocation\
    \ no symbol from a Python module whose top-level package name is \"bd\" or \"\
    beads\" was imported into the running \"shop-templates\" process\n  And during\
    \ the invocation the contents of the \".beads/\" directory were written by that\
    \ \"bd\" subprocess and not by file-writes issued directly from the \"shop-templates\"\
    \ process\n\n"

- hash: 03b4e3fa31d72031
  tags:
  - '@scenario_hash:03b4e3fa31d72031'
  - '@bc:shopsystem-templates'
  gherkin: "Feature: shop-templates bootstrap CLI surface (brief 002, items A-E)\n\
    \n  @scenario_hash:03b4e3fa31d72031 @bc:shopsystem-templates\n  Scenario: update\
    \ brings the set of bootstrap-managed agent files in the target directory into\
    \ alignment with the current canonical role set for the shop type \u2014 adding\
    \ canonical files that are missing and removing managed files whose canonical\
    \ template no longer exists\n  Given an existing git repository at a target directory\
    \ \"/tmp/example-shop\" that was previously bootstrapped as a \"bc\" shop named\
    \ \"example-shop\"\n  And the current canonical role set for shop type \"bc\"\
    \ is exactly the names listed by \"shop-templates list\" filtered to those that\
    \ the bootstrap surface treats as \"bc\" roles\n  And the directory \".claude/agents/\"\
    \ in the target directory contains a file named \"<former-bc-role>.md\" whose\
    \ name is not in that current canonical role set\n  And the directory \".claude/agents/\"\
    \ in the target directory does not contain a file named \"<new-bc-role>.md\" whose\
    \ name is in that current canonical role set\n  When I invoke the \"shop-templates\"\
    \ update entry point against the target directory \"/tmp/example-shop\"\n  Then\
    \ the exit code is 0\n  And after the invocation the directory \".claude/agents/\"\
    \ in the target directory does not contain a file named \"<former-bc-role>.md\"\
    \n  And after the invocation the directory \".claude/agents/\" in the target directory\
    \ contains a file named \"<new-bc-role>.md\" whose content equals the current\
    \ canonical \"<new-bc-role>\" template package-data file contents byte-for-byte\n\
    \  And after the invocation the set of files in \".claude/agents/\" whose names\
    \ match the canonical role-set naming convention equals the current canonical\
    \ role set for shop type \"bc\"\n\n"

    \ names the shop's own identity \u2014 its shop name and its role set \u2014 and\
    \ is consistent with the canonical primer template for the chosen shop type\n\
    \  Given an existing git repository at a target directory \"<target>\" with no\
    \ top-level \"CLAUDE.md\"\n  When I invoke the \"shop-templates\" bootstrap entry\
    \ point with shop type \"<shop_type>\", shop name \"<shop_name>\", and target\
    \ directory \"<target>\"\n  Then the exit code is 0\n  And the target directory\
    \ contains a top-level file named \"CLAUDE.md\"\n  And the content of that file\
    \ contains the literal substring \"<shop_name>\"\n  And the content of that file\
    \ names every role in the canonical role set for shop type \"<shop_type>\" by\
    \ name\n  And the content of that file does not name any role from the canonical\
    \ role set of the other shop type\n\n  Examples:\n    | shop_type | shop_name\

 `./src/shop_templates/cli.py`

```text
1	"""shop-templates CLI entry point.
2	
3	Subcommands:
4	    list
5	        Print available role-template names (one per line) to stdout.
6	    show <name>
7	        Print the named template's content to stdout. Exits non-zero
8	        with a stderr message when no template matches that name.
9	    bootstrap --shop-type {bc,lead} --shop-name NAME --target DIR
10	        Scaffold a new (existing-but-empty) shop repository at DIR:
11	        write the canonical role-prompt copies for the shop type under
12	        .claude/agents/, write a CLAUDE.md primer for the shop type,
13	        write a top-level .gitignore, and invoke `bd init` as a
14	        subprocess to initialize .beads/.
15	    update --target DIR --shop-type {bc,lead}
16	        Re-pour the bootstrap-managed agent files in DIR/.claude/agents/
17	        from the current canonical package data. Reconcile against the
18	        canonical role set for the shop type — add canonical files that
19	        are missing, remove managed files whose canonical template no
20	        longer exists. Does NOT touch CLAUDE.md, .gitignore, or .beads/.
21	
22	Role-prompt templates live in the package as `templates/<name>.md` data
23	files. CLAUDE.md primer templates live as `templates/claude/<shop_type>.md`.
24	The .gitignore template lives as `templates/gitignore.template`. The CLI
25	is the public boundary; dispatchers, scaffold scripts, and any other
26	consumer reads templates via this surface rather than by path.
27	"""
28	from __future__ import annotations
29	
30	import argparse
31	import subprocess
32	import sys
33	from importlib.resources import files
34	from pathlib import Path
35	
36	
37	_TEMPLATES_PKG = "shop_templates.templates"
38	_CLAUDE_TEMPLATES_PKG = "shop_templates.templates.claude"
39	
40	# The canonical role-set assignment per shop type. The bootstrap surface
41	# uses this to decide which role files to pour into .claude/agents/ for
42	# a freshly-bootstrapped shop, and the update surface uses it to
43	# reconcile an existing shop's .claude/agents/ against the current
44	# canonical set.
45	_CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS: dict[str, tuple[str, ...]] = {
46	    "bc": ("bc-implementer", "bc-reviewer"),
47	    "lead": ("lead-po", "lead-architect"),
48	}
49	
50	_SHOP_TYPES = tuple(_CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS.keys())
51	
52	
53	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
54	# Public template-access surface (importable; not just CLI-internal).
55	#
56	# Per scenario 0cce58eb573d3c91, the CLAUDE.md primer template must be
57	# accessible "through its public template-access surface" and must NOT
58	# be read from any path under the product's top-level working directory
59	# at lookup time — i.e., it must be served from importlib.resources
60	# package data, the same boundary the role-prompt templates use.
61	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
62	
63	
64	def _list_template_names() -> list[str]:
65	    """Return the sorted list of available role-template names.
66	
67	    Walks only the top-level package directory; sub-directories (e.g.
68	    `templates/claude/`) are skipped. This keeps `shop-templates list`
69	    output stable as new categories of package data (CLAUDE primers,
70	    gitignore template) are added.
71	    """
72	    pkg_root = files(_TEMPLATES_PKG)
73	    names = []
74	    for resource in pkg_root.iterdir():
75	        # `is_file()` plus `.md` suffix isolates role-prompt templates
76	        # from sub-directories like `templates/claude/`.
77	        if not resource.is_file():
78	            continue
79	        name = resource.name
80	        if name.endswith(".md"):
81	            names.append(name[: -len(".md")])
82	    return sorted(names)
83	
84	
85	def _read_template(name: str) -> str | None:
86	    """Return the named role-template's content, or None if no such template."""
87	    try:
88	        return (files(_TEMPLATES_PKG) / f"{name}.md").read_text()
89	    except FileNotFoundError:
90	        return None
91	
92	
93	def read_claude_md_primer(shop_type: str) -> str:
94	    """Return the canonical CLAUDE.md primer template body for a shop type.
95	
96	    The body is loaded from package data via importlib.resources; it is
97	    never read from a filesystem path under the product working
98	    directory. The returned string is the source of truth from which the
99	    bootstrap entry point generates a target directory's top-level
100	    CLAUDE.md.
101	    """
102	    if shop_type not in _CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS:
103	        raise ValueError(
104	            f"unknown shop type {shop_type!r}; accepted values: "
105	            f"{', '.join(_SHOP_TYPES)}"
106	        )
107	    resource = files(_CLAUDE_TEMPLATES_PKG) / f"{shop_type}.md"
108	    return resource.read_text()
109	
110	
111	def read_gitignore_template() -> str:
112	    """Return the canonical top-level .gitignore template body."""
113	    return (files(_TEMPLATES_PKG) / "gitignore.template").read_text()
114	
115	
116	def canonical_role_set(shop_type: str) -> tuple[str, ...]:
117	    """Return the canonical role-prompt-template names for a shop type.
118	
119	    The bootstrap surface treats these names as the canonical role set
120	    for the given shop type — both `bootstrap` (initial pour) and
121	    `update` (reconciliation) work from this mapping.
122	    """
123	    if shop_type not in _CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS:
124	        raise ValueError(
125	            f"unknown shop type {shop_type!r}; accepted values: "
126	            f"{', '.join(_SHOP_TYPES)}"
127	        )
128	    return _CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS[shop_type]
129	
130	
131	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
132	# `list` / `show` subcommands — pre-existing surface.
133	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
134	
135	
136	def _cmd_list(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
137	    for name in _list_template_names():
138	        print(name)
139	    return 0
140	
141	
142	def _cmd_show(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
143	    content = _read_template(args.name)
144	    if content is None:
145	        available = ", ".join(_list_template_names())
146	        print(
147	            f"shop-templates show: no template named {args.name!r}. Available: {available}",
148	            file=sys.stderr,
149	        )
150	        return 1
151	    # Use sys.stdout.write to preserve exact bytes (no trailing newline from print).
152	    sys.stdout.write(content)
153	    return 0
154	
155	
156	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
157	# `bootstrap` subcommand
158	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
159	
160	
161	def _render_claude_md(shop_type: str, shop_name: str) -> str:
162	    """Render the CLAUDE.md primer for a shop type by substituting the
163	    shop name. The primer must name the shop's identity (its shop_name
164	    and its role set) per scenario a15dac2f87549b8a.
165	    """
166	    primer = read_claude_md_primer(shop_type)
167	    return primer.replace("{{SHOP_NAME}}", shop_name)
168	
169	
170	def _bd_init_in(target: Path) -> int:
171	    """Invoke `bd init` as a subprocess in the target directory.
172	
173	    Per scenario 0c6f1c5d9bc4226e, .beads/ MUST be initialized by a
174	    subprocess named "bd" with first argument "init", NOT by importing
175	    bd / beads internals into the running shop-templates process and
176	    NOT by writing .beads/ files from shop-templates directly. This
177	    function is the single place where that subprocess is spawned.
178	    """
179	    result = subprocess.run(
180	        ["bd", "init"],
181	        cwd=str(target),
182	        capture_output=True,
183	        text=True,
184	    )
185	    if result.returncode != 0:
186	        print(
187	            f"shop-templates bootstrap: `bd init` failed in {target!s} "
188	            f"(exit {result.returncode}); stderr:\n{result.stderr}",
189	            file=sys.stderr,
190	        )
191	    return result.returncode
192	
193	
194	def _validate_shop_type(shop_type: str | None, command: str) -> str | None:
195	    """Return None if shop_type is valid; else print a usage diagnostic to
196	    stderr and return an error sentinel string usable by callers.
197	
198	    The diagnostic shape is pinned by scenarios d6e935bca62d0039 (missing
199	    shop type) and 7941cdc591cd2c3b (invalid shop type): stderr names
200	    the offending input AND lists the accepted values "bc" and "lead".
201	    """
202	    accepted = ", ".join(f'"{s}"' for s in _SHOP_TYPES)
203	    if shop_type is None:
204	        print(
205	            f"shop-templates {command}: missing required argument: shop type. "
206	            f"Accepted values: {accepted}.",
207	            file=sys.stderr,
208	        )
209	        return "missing"
210	    if shop_type not in _CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS:
211	        print(
212	            f"shop-templates {command}: invalid shop type {shop_type!r}. "
213	            f"Accepted values: {accepted}.",
214	            file=sys.stderr,
215	        )
216	        return "invalid"
217	    return None
218	
219	
220	def _cmd_bootstrap(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
221	    # Validate shop_type early so we can emit the usage diagnostic shape
222	    # the scenarios pin (named offending input + list of accepted values)
223	    # BEFORE touching the target directory. Scenarios d6e935bca62d0039
224	    # and 7941cdc591cd2c3b both require "writes no scaffold" when the
225	    # shop type is bad.
226	    err = _validate_shop_type(args.shop_type, "bootstrap")
227	    if err is not None:
228	        return 2
229	
230	    target = Path(args.target)
231	    shop_type: str = args.shop_type
232	    shop_name: str = args.shop_name
233	
234	    # Pour role-prompt files into .claude/agents/.
235	    agents_dir = target / ".claude" / "agents"
236	    agents_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
237	    for role_name in _CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS[shop_type]:
238	        body = _read_template(role_name)
239	        if body is None:
240	            print(
241	                f"shop-templates bootstrap: internal error — canonical "
242	                f"role template {role_name!r} not found in package data",
243	                file=sys.stderr,
244	            )
245	            return 1
246	        (agents_dir / f"{role_name}.md").write_text(body)
247	
248	    # Top-level CLAUDE.md — render the primer with the shop's identity.
249	    (target / "CLAUDE.md").write_text(_render_claude_md(shop_type, shop_name))
250	
251	    # Top-level .gitignore — pour the canonical template.
252	    (target / ".gitignore").write_text(read_gitignore_template())
253	
254	    # Initialize .beads/ via a `bd init` subprocess. shop-templates MUST
255	    # NOT import bd / beads internals and MUST NOT write to .beads/
256	    # directly (scenario 0c6f1c5d9bc4226e).
257	    rc = _bd_init_in(target)
258	    if rc != 0:
259	        return rc
260	
261	    return 0
262	
263	
264	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
265	# `update` subcommand
266	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
267	
268	
269	def _cmd_update(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
270	    # Update requires shop_type as an explicit argument so the
271	    # reconciliation step (scenario 03b4e3fa31d72031) can compare the
272	    # current contents of .claude/agents/ against the current canonical
273	    # role set for the right shop type. Inferring the shop type from
274	    # .claude/agents/ contents is unsafe: a hand-mutated shop may have
275	    # zero files whose names are in either canonical set.
276	    err = _validate_shop_type(args.shop_type, "update")
277	    if err is not None:
278	        return 2
279	
280	    target = Path(args.target)
281	    shop_type: str = args.shop_type
282	    agents_dir = target / ".claude" / "agents"
283	    agents_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
284	
285	    canonical = set(_CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS[shop_type])
286	
287	    # The "naming convention" for bootstrap-managed agent files is:
288	    # filename = <role-name>.md where <role-name> is a name in the
289	    # canonical role set of SOME shop type (current or historical).
290	    # For reconciliation we treat any .md file in .claude/agents/ as a
291	    # candidate managed file; if its name is in the current canonical
292	    # role set for this shop type it stays (and gets re-poured); if
293	    # its name is in NEITHER canonical role set we leave it alone
294	    # (shop-authored content we don't recognize); if it matches a
295	    # canonical name from any role set (including the other shop
296	    # type's set, or a name previously in this set but no longer) we
297	    # remove it. This satisfies scenario 03b4e3fa31d72031's "remove
298	    # managed files whose canonical template no longer exists" while
299	    # leaving shop-authored files alone.
300	    all_canonical_names = set()
301	    for rs in _CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS.values():
302	        all_canonical_names.update(rs)
303	    # A file is "managed" if its base name (sans .md) is in the
304	    # historical set OR is a current canonical name we're about to
305	    # pour. For this implementation, treat managed = any .md file
306	    # whose name follows the role-prompt-template naming convention,
307	    # which we recognize via the heuristic: name contains "-" and is
308	    # all lowercase ASCII. That keeps shop-authored files like
309	    # "TODO.md" or "NOTES.md" out.
310	    def _is_managed_role_name(stem: str) -> bool:
311	        # Recognize names of the form "<prefix>-<suffix>" where both
312	        # halves are ASCII lowercase letters. This matches every
313	        # canonical role-name pattern shipped today (bc-implementer,
314	        # bc-reviewer, lead-po, lead-architect) and excludes typical
315	        # shop-authored markdown filenames.
316	        if "-" not in stem:
317	            return False
318	        if stem != stem.lower():
319	            return False
320	        if not all(c.islower() or c == "-" for c in stem):
321	            return False
322	        return True
323	
324	    # Step 1: remove managed files whose names are NOT in the current
325	    # canonical role set for this shop type.
326	    for child in list(agents_dir.iterdir()):
327	        if not child.is_file():
328	            continue
329	        if not child.name.endswith(".md"):
330	            continue
331	        stem = child.name[:-len(".md")]
332	        if stem in canonical:
333	            continue
334	        if _is_managed_role_name(stem):
335	            child.unlink()
336	
337	    # Step 2: re-pour every canonical role file. If a file is already
338	    # byte-equal to the canonical content, leave its on-disk
339	    # mtime alone (scenario 264322ae65312bc7).
340	    for role_name in canonical:
341	        body = _read_template(role_name)
342	        if body is None:
343	            print(
344	                f"shop-templates update: internal error — canonical "
345	                f"role template {role_name!r} not found in package data",
346	                file=sys.stderr,
347	            )
348	            return 1
349	        target_file = agents_dir / f"{role_name}.md"
350	        if target_file.exists():
351	            current = target_file.read_text()
352	            if current == body:
353	                continue
354	        target_file.write_text(body)
355	
356	    # Per scenarios 56a0ac7107ba5c15, ca0f0a249d025267, 3f4d7d2256a97ae7,
357	    # and efae77e534588357: update does NOT touch CLAUDE.md, .gitignore,
358	    # .beads/, or any path outside .claude/agents/.
359	    return 0
360	
361	
362	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
363	# Argparse wiring
364	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
365	
366	
367	def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
368	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(prog="shop-templates")
369	    sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", required=True)
370	
371	    list_cmd = sub.add_parser("list", help="list available template names")
372	    list_cmd.set_defaults(func=_cmd_list)
373	
374	    show_cmd = sub.add_parser("show", help="print a named template to stdout")
375	    show_cmd.add_argument("name", help="template name (e.g. bc-implementer)")
376	    show_cmd.set_defaults(func=_cmd_show)
377	
378	    bootstrap_cmd = sub.add_parser(
379	        "bootstrap",
380	        help="scaffold an existing repo with the canonical shop structure",
381	    )
382	    # `--shop-type` intentionally has no default. Scenario d6e935bca62d0039
383	    # requires that omitting it produces a non-zero exit and a usage
384	    # diagnostic naming the missing argument AND the accepted values.
385	    # We declare it as a regular optional argument (not required=True at
386	    # the argparse layer) so argparse does NOT short-circuit with its
387	    # own auto-generated "the following arguments are required:..."
388	    # message — we want our own diagnostic shape on stderr.
389	    bootstrap_cmd.add_argument(
390	        "--shop-type",
391	        choices=None,  # validate manually so error shape is ours, not argparse's
392	        default=None,
393	        help='one of "bc" or "lead"',
394	    )
395	    bootstrap_cmd.add_argument(
396	        "--shop-name",
397	        default=None,
398	        help="the shop's own name, e.g. shopsystem-messaging",
399	    )
400	    bootstrap_cmd.add_argument(
401	        "--target",
402	        default=None,
403	        help="path to the existing repository to scaffold",
404	    )
405	    bootstrap_cmd.set_defaults(func=_cmd_bootstrap)
406	
407	    update_cmd = sub.add_parser(
408	        "update",
409	        help="re-pour bootstrap-managed agent files from current package data",
410	    )
411	    update_cmd.add_argument(
412	        "--target",
413	        default=None,
414	        help="path to the previously-bootstrapped repository",
415	    )
416	    update_cmd.add_argument(
417	        "--shop-type",
418	        default=None,
419	        help='one of "bc" or "lead" (must match the original bootstrap)',
420	    )
421	    update_cmd.set_defaults(func=_cmd_update)
422	
423	    return parser
424	
425	
426	def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
427	    parser = build_parser()
428	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
429	    return args.func(args)
430	
431	
432	if __name__ == "__main__":
433	    sys.exit(main())
434
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-13 13:34:03Z)**_

Now I need to scrutinize the substitution choice for 03b4e3fa31d72031. The scenario says:

- `former-bc-role` is "not in canonical role set" → bound to `bc-legacy-deprecated` ✓
- `new-bc-role` is "in canonical role set" → bound to `bc-implementer` ✓

But there's a subtlety. The implementer bound `new-bc-role` to `bc-implementer`, but the Given step also says `bc-implementer.md` does NOT exist in the agents dir at that moment. And the previous Given step (`given_agents_dir_has_extraneous`) explicitly clears canonical files. So that consistency holds.

However, the deeper question: does the substitution `new-bc-role = bc-implementer` really test "removes managed files whose canonical template no longer exists"? The scenario's `former-bc-role` is meant to represent a role that USED to be canonical. Choosing `bc-legacy-deprecated` is a plausible binding because it's lowercase-with-hyphen and uses `bc-` prefix — so the implementation's `_is_managed_role_name` heuristic recognizes it as "managed". This validates the removal path. Let me trace through.

Let me also probe key invariants to verify they have teeth.
