 `./tests/conftest.py`

```text
2100	#      `bd init` is executed as a subprocess (not by importing bd in-
2101	#      process), and scenario 3f4d7d2256a97ae7 requires that update
2102	#      executes NO `bd` subprocess. We install a per-test shim
2103	#      directory at the front of PATH containing a fake `bd` script
2104	#      that (a) logs every invocation to a file and (b) creates the
2105	#      .beads/ directory in cwd (so `bd init` still produces the
2106	#      side-effect bootstrap callers depend on). After the invocation
2107	#      we read the log to check what subprocesses ran and which import
2108	#      check the shim recorded.
2109	
2110	
2111	_BOOTSTRAP_TARGET_ALIASES = (
2112	    "/tmp/example-shop",
2113	    "/tmp/example-bc-shop",
2114	    "/tmp/example-lead-shop",
2115	)
2116	
2117	
2118	def _real_target_for_alias(alias: str, context: dict) -> Path:
2119	    """Map a feature-file path alias (e.g. "/tmp/example-shop") to a
2120	    fresh per-test directory. Subsequent calls with the same alias
2121	    within a single scenario resolve to the same real path.
2122	    """
2123	    mapping = context.setdefault("target_alias_to_real", {})
2124	    if alias in mapping:
2125	        return mapping[alias]
2126	    # We allocate per-alias subdirectories inside a single pytest tmp
2127	    # path stash on context, so each scenario gets an isolated workspace.
2128	    base = context.get("bootstrap_workspace")
2129	    assert base is not None, (
2130	        "Given step ordering bug: target alias resolved before "
2131	        "bootstrap_workspace was prepared"
2132	    )
2133	    real = base / alias.lstrip("/").replace("/", "_")
2134	    real.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
2135	    # Initialize as a git repo so the Given's "existing git repository"
2136	    # premise holds.
2137	    subprocess.run(
2138	        ["git", "init", "-q", str(real)],
2139	        check=True,
2140	        capture_output=True,
2141	    )
2142	    mapping[alias] = real
2143	    return real
2144	
2145	
2146	@pytest.fixture(autouse=False)
2147	def _ensure_bootstrap_workspace(tmp_path: Path, context: dict) -> Path:
2148	    """Not autouse; invoked from Given steps that need a fresh workspace."""
2149	    context["bootstrap_workspace"] = tmp_path
2150	    return tmp_path
2151	
2152	
2153	def _make_bd_shim_dir(tmp_path: Path, context: dict) -> Path:
2154	    """Create a directory containing a fake `bd` executable that logs
2155	    invocations to a file and minimally simulates `bd init` (mkdir
2156	    .beads/ in cwd with a marker file inside). Return the directory
2157	    path so the caller can prepend it to PATH.
2158	
2159	    Per scenarios 0c6f1c5d9bc4226e and 3f4d7d2256a97ae7, tests need to
2160	    answer "did a `bd` subprocess run, and with what first argument".
2161	    The log file path is stashed on context as "bd_shim_log" and is
2162	    TRUNCATED on each call — so only the most-recent invocation's bd
2163	    calls are observable. (Tests that need to chain a Given-side
2164	    bootstrap into a When-side update don't want the Given-side `bd
2165	    init` call to pollute the When-side observation window.)
2166	    """
2167	    shim_dir = tmp_path / "bd-shim"
2168	    shim_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
2169	    log = tmp_path / "bd-shim.log"
2170	    # Truncate so each invocation observes only its own subprocess calls.
2171	    log.write_text("")
2172	    context["bd_shim_log"] = log
2173	    bd_path = shim_dir / "bd"
2174	    # The shim is intentionally tiny — it logs argv (one line per call:
2175	    # "<arg1> <arg2> ..." plus its cwd) and, if the first arg is "init",
2176	    # creates ".beads/__shim_init__" inside cwd as the visible
2177	    # side-effect bootstrap callers depend on.
2178	    bd_path.write_text(
2179	        "#!/usr/bin/env bash\n"
2180	        f"log={log}\n"
2181	        'echo "$@|cwd=$PWD" >> "$log"\n'
2182	        'if [[ "$1" == "init" ]]; then\n'
2183	        '  mkdir -p .beads\n'
2184	        '  : > .beads/__shim_init__\n'
2185	        'fi\n'
2186	        "exit 0\n"
2187	    )
2188	    bd_path.chmod(0o755)
2189	    return shim_dir
2190	
2191	
2192	def _run_shop_templates_with_bd_shim(
2193	    args: list[str], context: dict, tmp_path: Path
2194	) -> subprocess.CompletedProcess:
2195	    """Invoke `shop-templates` with a fake `bd` on PATH, capturing
2196	    whether/how `bd` was called and whether `bd`/`beads` Python modules
2197	    got imported into the shop-templates process.
2198	
2199	    The "no bd/beads imports" check (scenario 0c6f1c5d9bc4226e) is
2200	    implemented by invoking shop-templates indirectly via a small
2201	    Python wrapper that (1) runs `shop_templates.cli.main(args)` and
2202	    (2) after main returns, writes `bd_in_modules=True/False` and
2203	    `beads_in_modules=True/False` to an import-trace log. The test
2204	    asserts both are False.
2205	    """
2206	    import os
2207	    import shutil
2208	
2209	    shim_dir = _make_bd_shim_dir(tmp_path, context)
2210	    import_log = tmp_path / "import-trace.log"
2211	    context["import_trace_log"] = import_log
2212	
2213	    # Build a wrapper script that runs shop_templates.cli.main(argv)
2214	    # and then writes the import-trace flags. We must NOT import bd
2215	    # or beads ourselves anywhere in this wrapper (the assertion would
2216	    # then trivially fail).
2217	    wrapper = tmp_path / "_run_shop_templates_wrapper.py"
2218	    args_repr = repr(list(args))
2219	    wrapper.write_text(
2220	        "import sys\n"
2221	        "from shop_templates.cli import main\n"
2222	        f"argv = {args_repr}\n"
2223	        "rc = main(argv)\n"
2224	        "import_log = open(r%r, 'w')\n" % str(import_log)
2225	        + "bd_in = any(\n"
2226	        "    m == 'bd' or m.startswith('bd.')\n"
2227	        "    for m in sys.modules\n"
2228	        ")\n"
2229	        "beads_in = any(\n"
2230	        "    m == 'beads' or m.startswith('beads.')\n"
2231	        "    for m in sys.modules\n"
2232	        ")\n"
2233	        "import_log.write(f'bd_in_modules={bd_in}\\n')\n"
2234	        "import_log.write(f'beads_in_modules={beads_in}\\n')\n"
2235	        "import_log.close()\n"
2236	        "sys.exit(rc)\n"
2237	    )
2238	
2239	    env = os.environ.copy()
2240	    env["PATH"] = f"{shim_dir}{os.pathsep}{env.get('PATH', '')}"
2241	
2242	    # Use the same python interpreter that's running the test, so
2243	    # `shop_templates` is importable from the installed package.
2244	    py = sys.executable
2245	    return subprocess.run(
2246	        [py, str(wrapper)],
2247	        capture_output=True,
2248	        text=True,
2249	        env=env,
2250	    )
2251

2356	def given_target_dir_is_pristine(context: dict) -> None:
2357	    # Single-target version (used by scenarios that named a single

2378	def _do_bootstrap_for_test(
2379	    alias: str, shop_type: str, shop_name: str, context: dict, tmp_path: Path
2380	) -> Path:
2381	    """Helper: run the bootstrap CLI (with bd shim) against the target
2382	    aliased by `alias`, asserting it succeeded. Returns the real path.

2388	        [

2391	            shop_type,
2392	            "--shop-name",
2393	            shop_name,

2395	            str(real),

2397	        context,

2644	
2645	# Bindings for the scenario 03b4e3fa31d72031 placeholders. The
2646	# scenario uses literal "<former-bc-role>.md" and "<new-bc-role>.md"
2647	# tokens (it's a regular Scenario, not a Scenario Outline with
2648	# Examples) — they are placeholder names meant for the test harness
2649	# to bind to concrete values that satisfy the surrounding constraints
2650	# (former NOT in canonical; new IS in canonical). Pick:
2651	#   former-bc-role  = "bc-legacy-deprecated" (NOT in canonical bc set)
2652	#   new-bc-role     = "bc-implementer"        (IS in canonical bc set)
2653	_PLACEHOLDER_FORMER_BC_ROLE = "bc-legacy-deprecated"
2654	_PLACEHOLDER_NEW_BC_ROLE = "bc-implementer"
2655	
2656	
2657	def _bind_role_placeholder(literal: str) -> str:
2658	    """Substitute scenario-level role-name placeholders with the
2659	    concrete values defined above. The literal arrives with the
2660	    angle-bracket markers (e.g. "<former-bc-role>.md"); we replace
2661	    only the bracketed token and keep the rest of the filename.

Since my validator runs before `bd init` is invoked (lead-ijr placed `_validate_shop_name` early; same as `_validate_shop_type`), I'll place `_validate_target` even earlier, before any Path() construction. - When: `I invoke the "shop-templates" bootstrap entry point with --shop-type "bc" and --shop-name "shopsystem-example" but with no --target argument`
- Then: `the exit code is 2` (reusable), `stderr names "--target" as a missing required argument` (reusable), `stderr does not contain a Python traceback or the substring "TypeError"` (reusable), `the witness directory "/tmp/example-shop-no-target-witness" still contains no ".claude/agents/" directory and no ".beads/" directory and no top-level "CLAUDE.md" and no top-level ".gitignore"` (new wording).

Scenario `8fe363bd46cb766c`:
- Given: `a previously-bootstrapped shop at "/tmp/example-shop-update-no-target" of shop type "bc" whose ".claude/agents/" directory contains exactly the canonical "bc" role files`

- When: `I invoke the "shop-templates" update entry point with --shop-type "bc" but with no --target argument`
- Then: exit 2 (reusable), `stderr names "--target" as a missing required argument` (reusable), `stderr does not contain a Python traceback or the substring "TypeError"` (reusable), `every file under "/tmp/example-shop-update-no-target/.claude/agents/" has the same byte contents and mtime as the recorded snapshot`, `the top-level "CLAUDE.md", top-level ".gitignore", and ".beads/" directory under "/tmp/example-shop-update-no-target" are unchanged from before the invocation`.

Feature: shop-templates CLI rejects missing --target with argparse-style error (consistency pass)

  @scenario_hash:fe59a11a88a9ab60 @bc:shopsystem-templates
  Scenario: the bootstrap entry point exits with an argparse-style usage error when invoked without --target, with no Python traceback emitted
    Given an existing empty directory "/tmp/example-shop-no-target-witness" that contains no ".claude/agents/" directory, no ".beads/" directory, no top-level "CLAUDE.md", and no top-level ".gitignore"
    When I invoke the "shop-templates" bootstrap entry point with --shop-type "bc" and --shop-name "shopsystem-example" but with no --target argument
    Then the exit code is 2
    And stderr names "--target" as a missing required argument
    And stderr does not contain a Python traceback or the substring "TypeError"
    And the witness directory "/tmp/example-shop-no-target-witness" still contains no ".claude/agents/" directory and no ".beads/" directory and no top-level "CLAUDE.md" and no top-level ".gitignore"

  @scenario_hash:8fe363bd46cb766c @bc:shopsystem-templates
  Scenario: the update entry point exits with an argparse-style usage error when invoked without --target, with no Python traceback emitted and no modification to any pre-existing shop scaffold
    Given a previously-bootstrapped shop at "/tmp/example-shop-update-no-target" of shop type "bc" whose ".claude/agents/" directory contains exactly the canonical "bc" role files
    And a recorded snapshot of the byte contents and mtimes of every file under "/tmp/example-shop-update-no-target/.claude/agents/"
    When I invoke the "shop-templates" update entry point with --shop-type "bc" but with no --target argument
    Then the exit code is 2
    And stderr names "--target" as a missing required argument
    And stderr does not contain a Python traceback or the substring "TypeError"
    And every file under "/tmp/example-shop-update-no-target/.claude/agents/" has the same byte contents and mtime as the recorded snapshot
    And the top-level "CLAUDE.md", top-level ".gitignore", and ".beads/" directory under "/tmp/example-shop-update-no-target" are unchanged from before the invocation

```

Now add step definitions. I need:

Reusable steps (already in conftest):
- `the exit code is 2`
- `stderr names "{flag}" as a missing required argument`
- `stderr does not contain a Python traceback or the substring "TypeError"`

New steps needed:
1. Given: `an existing empty directory "{alias}" that contains no ".claude/agents/" directory, no ".beads/" directory, no top-level "CLAUDE.md", and no top-level ".gitignore"` — note: scenario says "empty directory" NOT "git repository", differing from the `bootstrap_missing_shop_name` case. The alias `/tmp/example-shop-no-target-witness` is not in `_BOOTSTRAP_TARGET_ALIASES`, but looking at `_real_target_for_alias`, that tuple is unused at runtime — it just maps any alias to a tmp dir. The function ALSO runs `git init` on the dir, which contradicts "empty directory". I'll write a parallel helper that doesn't git-init.

2. When: `I invoke the "shop-templates" bootstrap entry point with --shop-type "{shop_type}" and --shop-name "{shop_name}" but with no --target argument`

3. Then: `the witness directory "{alias}" still contains no ".claude/agents/" directory and no ".beads/" directory and no top-level "CLAUDE.md" and no top-level ".gitignore"`

4. Given: `a previously-bootstrapped shop at "{alias}" of shop type "{shop_type}" whose ".claude/agents/" directory contains exactly the canonical "{shop_type_again}" role files` — different alias quoted in the scenario; the existing "previously bootstrapped" Given doesn't take an explicit "exactly the canonical X role files" affirmation, but the existing precedent does the bootstrap which inherently produces exactly that. I need a new Given here since the existing one uses a different phrasing ("was previously bootstrapped as ..."). I will need a `--shop-name` value for the bootstrap; the scenario doesn't supply one, so I'll pick a fixed test value.

5. Given: `a recorded snapshot of the byte contents and mtimes of every file under "{path}"`

6. When: `I invoke the "shop-templates" update entry point with --shop-type "{shop_type}" but with no --target argument`

7. Then: `every file under "{path}" has the same byte contents and mtime as the recorded snapshot`

8. Then: `the top-level "CLAUDE.md", top-level ".gitignore", and ".beads/" directory under "{alias}" are unchanged from before the invocation` — for this we need to record pre-state of those during the prior Given.

Let me think about the "empty directory" semantics: the scenario says the directory exists but contains none of the listed shop-artifact paths. `_real_target_for_alias` git-inits the dir (which produces `.git/` but none of the scenario-named paths). That actually satisfies "contains no .claude/agents/, .beads/, CLAUDE.md, .gitignore". But the scenario explicitly says "empty directory" — I should honor that by not git-initing.

Let me also handle the existing-alias-map issue: `/tmp/example-shop-no-target-witness` isn't in the existing alias tuple, but `_real_target_for_alias` doesn't validate against it — it just maps. So reusing the helper would be fine but for the git-init side-effect. I'll add a dedicated helper for empty-witness directories.

1	"""Shared fixtures and pytest-bdd step definitions for shopsystem-templates.
2	
3	The scenarios under features/ pin the BC's outward contract — the
4	`shop-templates` CLI surface and the structural section headers each
5	role-template markdown file carries. The CLI is invoked via subprocess
6	(the same boundary downstream callers use); content checks read the
7	template via the same CLI subcommand so any drift between the CLI's
8	view of the package data and the file system surfaces here.
9	
10	Style mirrors shopsystem-messaging/tests/conftest.py and

```text
    )


@then(
    'stderr does not contain a Python traceback or the substring '
    '"TypeError"'
)
def then_stderr_has_no_traceback_or_typeerror(context: dict) -> None:
    stderr = context["cli_stderr"]
    # A Python traceback emitted by the default sys.excepthook always
    # begins with the literal "Traceback (most recent call last):" line.
    # Anything we emit ourselves via print(..., file=sys.stderr) won't
    # contain that header — that's the discriminator the scenario
    # depends on.
    assert "Traceback (most recent call last):" not in stderr, (
        f"unexpected Python traceback header in stderr:\n{stderr}"
    )
    assert "TypeError" not in stderr, (
        f"unexpected 'TypeError' substring in stderr:\n{stderr}"
    )
```
</details></tool-use>

7	        Print the named template's content to stdout. Exits non-zero
8	        with a stderr message when no template matches that name.
9	    bootstrap --shop-type {bc,lead} --shop-name NAME --target DIR
10	        Scaffold a new (existing-but-empty) shop repository at DIR:
11	        write the canonical role-prompt copies for the shop type under
12	        .claude/agents/, write a CLAUDE.md primer for the shop type,
13	        write a top-level .gitignore, and invoke `bd init` as a
14	        subprocess to initialize .beads/.
15	    update --target DIR --shop-type {bc,lead}
16	        Re-pour the bootstrap-managed agent files in DIR/.claude/agents/
17	        from the current canonical package data. Reconcile against the
18	        canonical role set for the shop type — add canonical files that
19	        are missing, remove managed files whose canonical template no
20	        longer exists. Does NOT touch CLAUDE.md, .gitignore, or .beads/.
21	
22	Role-prompt templates live in the package as `templates/<name>.md` data
23	files. CLAUDE.md primer templates live as `templates/claude/<shop_type>.md`.
24	The .gitignore template lives as `templates/gitignore.template`. The CLI
25	is the public boundary; dispatchers, scaffold scripts, and any other
26	consumer reads templates via this surface rather than by path.
27	"""
28	from __future__ import annotations
29	
30	import argparse
31	import subprocess
32	import sys
33	from importlib.resources import files
34	from pathlib import Path
35	
36	
37	_TEMPLATES_PKG = "shop_templates.templates"
38	_CLAUDE_TEMPLATES_PKG = "shop_templates.templates.claude"
39	
40	# The canonical role-set assignment per shop type. The bootstrap surface
41	# uses this to decide which role files to pour into .claude/agents/ for
42	# a freshly-bootstrapped shop, and the update surface uses it to
43	# reconcile an existing shop's .claude/agents/ against the current
44	# canonical set.
45	_CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS: dict[str, tuple[str, ...]] = {
46	    "bc": ("bc-implementer", "bc-reviewer"),
47	    "lead": ("lead-po", "lead-architect"),
48	}
49	
50	_SHOP_TYPES = tuple(_CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS.keys())
51	
52	
53	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
54	# Public template-access surface (importable; not just CLI-internal).
55	#
56	# Per scenario 0cce58eb573d3c91, the CLAUDE.md primer template must be
57	# accessible "through its public template-access surface" and must NOT
58	# be read from any path under the product's top-level working directory
59	# at lookup time — i.e., it must be served from importlib.resources
60	# package data, the same boundary the role-prompt templates use.
61	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
62	
63	
64	def _list_template_names() -> list[str]:
65	    """Return the sorted list of available role-template names.
66	
67	    Walks only the top-level package directory; sub-directories (e.g.
68	    `templates/claude/`) are skipped. This keeps `shop-templates list`
69	    output stable as new categories of package data (CLAUDE primers,
70	    gitignore template) are added.
71	    """
72	    pkg_root = files(_TEMPLATES_PKG)
73	    names = []
74	    for resource in pkg_root.iterdir():
75	        # `is_file()` plus `.md` suffix isolates role-prompt templates
76	        # from sub-directories like `templates/claude/`.
77	        if not resource.is_file():
78	            continue
79	        name = resource.name
80	        if name.endswith(".md"):
81	            names.append(name[: -len(".md")])
82	    return sorted(names)
83	
84	
85	def _read_template(name: str) -> str | None:
86	    """Return the named role-template's content, or None if no such template."""
87	    try:
88	        return (files(_TEMPLATES_PKG) / f"{name}.md").read_text()
89	    except FileNotFoundError:
90	        return None
91	
92	
93	def read_claude_md_primer(shop_type: str) -> str:
94	    """Return the canonical CLAUDE.md primer template body for a shop type.
95	
96	    The body is loaded from package data via importlib.resources; it is
97	    never read from a filesystem path under the product working
98	    directory. The returned string is the source of truth from which the
99	    bootstrap entry point generates a target directory's top-level
100	    CLAUDE.md.
101	    """
102	    if shop_type not in _CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS:
103	        raise ValueError(
104	            f"unknown shop type {shop_type!r}; accepted values: "
105	            f"{', '.join(_SHOP_TYPES)}"
106	        )
107	    resource = files(_CLAUDE_TEMPLATES_PKG) / f"{shop_type}.md"
108	    return resource.read_text()
109	
110	
111	def read_gitignore_template() -> str:
112	    """Return the canonical top-level .gitignore template body."""
113	    return (files(_TEMPLATES_PKG) / "gitignore.template").read_text()
114	
115	
116	def canonical_role_set(shop_type: str) -> tuple[str, ...]:
117	    """Return the canonical role-prompt-template names for a shop type.
118	
119	    The bootstrap surface treats these names as the canonical role set
120	    for the given shop type — both `bootstrap` (initial pour) and
121	    `update` (reconciliation) work from this mapping.
122	    """
123	    if shop_type not in _CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS:
124	        raise ValueError(
125	            f"unknown shop type {shop_type!r}; accepted values: "
126	            f"{', '.join(_SHOP_TYPES)}"
127	        )
128	    return _CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS[shop_type]
129	
130	
131	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
132	# `list` / `show` subcommands — pre-existing surface.
133	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
134	
135	
136	def _cmd_list(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
137	    for name in _list_template_names():
138	        print(name)
139	    return 0
140	
141	
142	def _cmd_show(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
143	    content = _read_template(args.name)
144	    if content is None:
145	        available = ", ".join(_list_template_names())
146	        print(
147	            f"shop-templates show: no template named {args.name!r}. Available: {available}",
148	            file=sys.stderr,
149	        )
150	        return 1
151	    # Use sys.stdout.write to preserve exact bytes (no trailing newline from print).
152	    sys.stdout.write(content)
153	    return 0
154	
155	
156	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
157	# `bootstrap` subcommand
158	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
159	
160	
161	def _render_claude_md(shop_type: str, shop_name: str) -> str:
162	    """Render the CLAUDE.md primer for a shop type by substituting the
163	    shop name. The primer must name the shop's identity (its shop_name
164	    and its role set) per scenario a15dac2f87549b8a.
165	    """
166	    primer = read_claude_md_primer(shop_type)
167	    return primer.replace("{{SHOP_NAME}}", shop_name)
168	
169	
170	def _bd_init_in(target: Path) -> int:
171	    """Invoke `bd init` as a subprocess in the target directory.
172	
173	    Per scenario 0c6f1c5d9bc4226e, .beads/ MUST be initialized by a
174	    subprocess named "bd" with first argument "init", NOT by importing
175	    bd / beads internals into the running shop-templates process and
176	    NOT by writing .beads/ files from shop-templates directly. This
177	    function is the single place where that subprocess is spawned.
178	
179	    Per scenario 32d99f6d4a2dad37 (lead-2gr round 2 of lead-k8v's
180	    bd-init side-effect closure), `bd init` MUST be invoked with the
181	    `--skip-agents` flag. That flag is bd's own opt-out for the
182	    AGENTS.md / .claude/settings.json / CLAUDE.md-beads-block append
183	    that bd otherwise applies during `bd init`. The shop's canonical
184	    agent surface is .claude/agents/*.md alone (poured by bootstrap
185	    itself); bd's agent-surface generation is duplicative at best and
186	    contradictory at worst, so we suppress it. Verified against bd
187	    1.0.3: `BD_NON_INTERACTIVE=1 bd init --skip-agents --quiet` in a
188	    fresh probe dir produces .beads/ + .gitignore and no AGENTS.md,
189	    no .claude/.
190	    """
191	    result = subprocess.run(
192	        ["bd", "init", "--skip-agents"],
193	        cwd=str(target),
194	        capture_output=True,
195	        text=True,
196	    )
197	    if result.returncode != 0:
198	        print(
199	            f"shop-templates bootstrap: `bd init` failed in {target!s} "
200	            f"(exit {result.returncode}); stderr:\n{result.stderr}",
201	            file=sys.stderr,
202	        )
203	    return result.returncode
204	
205	
206	def _validate_shop_type(shop_type: str | None, command: str) -> str | None:
207	    """Return None if shop_type is valid; else print a usage diagnostic to
208	    stderr and return an error sentinel string usable by callers.
209	
210	    The diagnostic shape is pinned by scenarios d6e935bca62d0039 (missing
211	    shop type) and 7941cdc591cd2c3b (invalid shop type): stderr names
212	    the offending input AND lists the accepted values "bc" and "lead".
213	    """
214	    accepted = ", ".join(f'"{s}"' for s in _SHOP_TYPES)
215	    if shop_type is None:
216	        print(
217	            f"shop-templates {command}: missing required argument: shop type. "
218	            f"Accepted values: {accepted}.",
219	            file=sys.stderr,
220	        )
221	        return "missing"
222	    if shop_type not in _CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS:
223	        print(
224	            f"shop-templates {command}: invalid shop type {shop_type!r}. "
225	            f"Accepted values: {accepted}.",
226	            file=sys.stderr,
227	        )
228	        return "invalid"
229	    return None
230	
231	
232	def _validate_shop_name(shop_name: str | None, command: str) -> str | None:
233	    """Return None if shop_name is provided; else print a usage diagnostic
234	    to stderr and return an error sentinel string usable by callers.
235	
236	    The diagnostic shape is pinned by scenario 3c8612d20608e9a3: stderr
237	    names "--shop-name" as the missing required argument and emits no
238	    Python traceback / no "TypeError" substring. We validate manually
239	    (matching the in-house pattern used by `_validate_shop_type`) rather
240	    than letting argparse short-circuit so the error shape is ours, not
241	    argparse's auto-generated message.
242	    """
243	    if shop_name is None:
244	        print(
245	            f"shop-templates {command}: missing required argument: --shop-name. "
246	            f"Provide the shop's own name (e.g. shopsystem-messaging).",
247	            file=sys.stderr,
248	        )
249	        return "missing"
250	    return None
251	
252	
253	def _validate_target(target: str | None, command: str) -> str | None:
254	    """Return None if target is provided; else print a usage diagnostic
255	    to stderr and return an error sentinel string usable by callers.
256	
257	    The diagnostic shape is pinned by scenarios fe59a11a88a9ab60
258	    (bootstrap) and 8fe363bd46cb766c (update): stderr names "--target"
259	    as the missing required argument and emits no Python traceback / no
260	    "TypeError" substring. We validate manually (matching the in-house
261	    pattern used by `_validate_shop_type` and `_validate_shop_name`)
262	    rather than letting argparse short-circuit so the error shape is
263	    ours, not argparse's auto-generated message — and so the
264	    diagnostic surfaces BEFORE any Path(args.target) construction that
265	    would otherwise raise TypeError when target is None.
266	    """
267	    if target is None:
268	        print(
269	            f"shop-templates {command}: missing required argument: --target. "
270	            f"Provide the path to the target repository.",
271	            file=sys.stderr,
272	        )
273	        return "missing"
274	    return None
275	
276	
277	def _cmd_bootstrap(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
278	    # Validate shop_type early so we can emit the usage diagnostic shape
279	    # the scenarios pin (named offending input + list of accepted values)
280	    # BEFORE touching the target directory. Scenarios d6e935bca62d0039
281	    # and 7941cdc591cd2c3b both require "writes no scaffold" when the
282	    # shop type is bad.
283	    err = _validate_shop_type(args.shop_type, "bootstrap")
284	    if err is not None:
285	        return 2
286	
287	    # Validate shop_name early for the same reason. Scenario
288	    # 3c8612d20608e9a3 pins exit 2 + stderr names "--shop-name" + no
289	    # traceback + no scaffold written when --shop-name is omitted.
290	    # Without this guard, args.shop_name=None propagates into
291	    # _render_claude_md and surfaces as a TypeError from str.replace().
292	    err = _validate_shop_name(args.shop_name, "bootstrap")
293	    if err is not None:
294	        return 2
295	
296	    # Validate target early for the same reason. Scenario
297	    # fe59a11a88a9ab60 pins exit 2 + stderr names "--target" + no
298	    # traceback + no scaffold written when --target is omitted. Without
299	    # this guard, args.target=None propagates into Path() and surfaces
300	    # as a TypeError from pathlib.
301	    err = _validate_target(args.target, "bootstrap")
302	    if err is not None:
303	        return 2
304	
305	    target = Path(args.target)
306	    shop_type: str = args.shop_type
307	    shop_name: str = args.shop_name
308	
309	    # Pour role-prompt files into .claude/agents/.
310	    agents_dir = target / ".claude" / "agents"
311	    agents_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
312	    for role_name in _CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS[shop_type]:
313	        body = _read_template(role_name)
314	        if body is None:
315	            print(
316	                f"shop-templates bootstrap: internal error — canonical "
317	                f"role template {role_name!r} not found in package data",
318	                file=sys.stderr,
319	            )
320	            return 1
321	        (agents_dir / f"{role_name}.md").write_text(body)
322	
323	    # Top-level CLAUDE.md — render the primer with the shop's identity.
324	    (target / "CLAUDE.md").write_text(_render_claude_md(shop_type, shop_name))
325	
326	    # Top-level .gitignore — pour the canonical template.
327	    (target / ".gitignore").write_text(read_gitignore_template())
328	
329	    # Initialize .beads/ via a `bd init` subprocess. shop-templates MUST
330	    # NOT import bd / beads internals and MUST NOT write to .beads/
331	    # directly (scenario 0c6f1c5d9bc4226e).
332	    rc = _bd_init_in(target)
333	    if rc != 0:
334	        return rc
335	
336	    return 0
337	
338	
339	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
340	# `update` subcommand
341	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
342	
343	
344	def _cmd_update(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
345	    # Update requires shop_type as an explicit argument so the
346	    # reconciliation step (scenario 03b4e3fa31d72031) can compare the
347	    # current contents of .claude/agents/ against the current canonical
348	    # role set for the right shop type. Inferring the shop type from
349	    # .claude/agents/ contents is unsafe: a hand-mutated shop may have
350	    # zero files whose names are in either canonical set.
351	    err = _validate_shop_type(args.shop_type, "update")
352	    if err is not None:
353	        return 2
354	
355	    # Validate target early. Scenario 8fe363bd46cb766c pins exit 2 +
356	    # stderr names "--target" + no traceback + every file under the
357	    # previously-bootstrapped shop's .claude/agents/ is byte+mtime
358	    # identical when --target is omitted. Without this guard,
359	    # args.target=None propagates into Path() and surfaces as a
360	    # TypeError from pathlib.
361	    err = _validate_target(args.target, "update")
362	    if err is not None:
363	        return 2
364	
365	    target = Path(args.target)
366	    shop_type: str = args.shop_type
367	    agents_dir = target / ".claude" / "agents"
368	    agents_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
369	
370	    canonical = set(_CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS[shop_type])
371	
372	    # The "naming convention" for bootstrap-managed agent files is:
373	    # filename = <role-name>.md where <role-name> is a name in the
374	    # canonical role set of SOME shop type (current or historical).
375	    # For reconciliation we treat any .md file in .claude/agents/ as a
376	    # candidate managed file; if its name is in the current canonical
377	    # role set for this shop type it stays (and gets re-poured); if
378	    # its name is in NEITHER canonical role set we leave it alone
379	    # (shop-authored content we don't recognize); if it matches a
380	    # canonical name from any role set (including the other shop
381	    # type's set, or a name previously in this set but no longer) we
382	    # remove it. This satisfies scenario 03b4e3fa31d72031's "remove
383	    # managed files whose canonical template no longer exists" while
384	    # leaving shop-authored files alone.
385	    all_canonical_names = set()
386	    for rs in _CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS.values():
387	        all_canonical_names.update(rs)
388	    # A file is "managed" if its base name (sans .md) is in the
389	    # historical set OR is a current canonical name we're about to
390	    # pour. For this implementation, treat managed = any .md file
391	    # whose name follows the role-prompt-template naming convention,
392	    # which we recognize via the heuristic: name contains "-" and is
393	    # all lowercase ASCII. That keeps shop-authored files like
394	    # "TODO.md" or "NOTES.md" out.
395	    def _is_managed_role_name(stem: str) -> bool:
396	        # Recognize names of the form "<prefix>-<suffix>" where both
397	        # halves are ASCII lowercase letters. This matches every
398	        # canonical role-name pattern shipped today (bc-implementer,
399	        # bc-reviewer, lead-po, lead-architect) and excludes typical
400	        # shop-authored markdown filenames.
401	        if "-" not in stem:
402	            return False
403	        if stem != stem.lower():
404	            return False
405	        if not all(c.islower() or c == "-" for c in stem):
406	            return False
407	        return True
408	
409	    # Step 1: remove managed files whose names are NOT in the current
410	    # canonical role set for this shop type.
411	    for child in list(agents_dir.iterdir()):
412	        if not child.is_file():
413	            continue
414	        if not child.name.endswith(".md"):
415	            continue
416	        stem = child.name[:-len(".md")]
417	        if stem in canonical:
418	            continue
419	        if _is_managed_role_name(stem):
420	            child.unlink()
421	
422	    # Step 2: re-pour every canonical role file. If a file is already
423	    # byte-equal to the canonical content, leave its on-disk
424	    # mtime alone (scenario 264322ae65312bc7).
425	    for role_name in canonical:
426	        body = _read_template(role_name)
427	        if body is None:
428	            print(
429	                f"shop-templates update: internal error — canonical "
430	                f"role template {role_name!r} not found in package data",
431	                file=sys.stderr,
432	            )
433	            return 1
434	        target_file = agents_dir / f"{role_name}.md"
435	        if target_file.exists():
436	            current = target_file.read_text()
437	            if current == body:
438	                continue
439	        target_file.write_text(body)
440	
441	    # Per scenarios 56a0ac7107ba5c15, ca0f0a249d025267, 3f4d7d2256a97ae7,
442	    # and efae77e534588357: update does NOT touch CLAUDE.md, .gitignore,
443	    # .beads/, or any path outside .claude/agents/.
444	    return 0
445	
446	
447	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
448	# Argparse wiring
449	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
450	
451	
452	def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
453	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(prog="shop-templates")
454	    sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", required=True)
455	
456	    list_cmd = sub.add_parser("list", help="list available template names")
457	    list_cmd.set_defaults(func=_cmd_list)
458	
459	    show_cmd = sub.add_parser("show", help="print a named template to stdout")
460	    show_cmd.add_argument("name", help="template name (e.g. bc-implementer)")
461	    show_cmd.set_defaults(func=_cmd_show)
462	
463	    bootstrap_cmd = sub.add_parser(
464	        "bootstrap",
465	        help="scaffold an existing repo with the canonical shop structure",
466	    )
467	    # `--shop-type` intentionally has no default. Scenario d6e935bca62d0039
468	    # requires that omitting it produces a non-zero exit and a usage
469	    # diagnostic naming the missing argument AND the accepted values.
470	    # We declare it as a regular optional argument (not required=True at
471	    # the argparse layer) so argparse does NOT short-circuit with its
472	    # own auto-generated "the following arguments are required:..."
473	    # message — we want our own diagnostic shape on stderr.
474	    bootstrap_cmd.add_argument(
475	        "--shop-type",
476	        choices=None,  # validate manually so error shape is ours, not argparse's
477	        default=None,
478	        help='one of "bc" or "lead"',
479	    )
480	    bootstrap_cmd.add_argument(
481	        "--shop-name",
482	        default=None,
483	        help="the shop's own name, e.g. shopsystem-messaging",
484	    )
485	    bootstrap_cmd.add_argument(
486	        "--target",
487	        default=None,
488	        help="path to the existing repository to scaffold",
489	    )
490	    bootstrap_cmd.set_defaults(func=_cmd_bootstrap)
491	
492	    update_cmd = sub.add_parser(
493	        "update",
494	        help="re-pour bootstrap-managed agent files from current package data",
495	    )
496	    update_cmd.add_argument(
497	        "--target",
498	        default=None,
499	        help="path to the previously-bootstrapped repository",
500	    )
501	    update_cmd.add_argument(
502	        "--shop-type",
503	        default=None,
504	        help='one of "bc" or "lead" (must match the original bootstrap)',
505	    )
506	    update_cmd.set_defaults(func=_cmd_update)
507	
508	    return parser
509	
510	
511	def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
512	    parser = build_parser()
513	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
514	    return args.func(args)
515	
516	
517	if __name__ == "__main__":
518	    sys.exit(main())
519
```

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-13 20:04:51Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Look at cli.py diff against last commit

```text
diff --git a/src/shop_templates/cli.py b/src/shop_templates/cli.py
index 8745e2f..c530d67 100644
--- a/src/shop_templates/cli.py
+++ b/src/shop_templates/cli.py
@@ -2,30 +2,80 @@
 
 Subcommands:
     list
-        Print available template names (one per line) to stdout.
+        Print available role-template names (one per line) to stdout.
     show <name>
         Print the named template's content to stdout. Exits non-zero
         with a stderr message when no template matches that name.
+    bootstrap --shop-type {bc,lead} --shop-name NAME --target DIR
+        Scaffold a new (existing-but-empty) shop repository at DIR:
+        write the canonical role-prompt copies for the shop type under
+        .claude/agents/, write a CLAUDE.md primer for the shop type,
+        write a top-level .gitignore, and invoke `bd init` as a
+        subprocess to initialize .beads/.
+    update --target DIR --shop-type {bc,lead}
+        Re-pour the bootstrap-managed agent files in DIR/.claude/agents/
+        from the current canonical package data. Reconcile against the
+        canonical role set for the shop type — add canonical files that
+        are missing, remove managed files whose canonical template no
+        longer exists. Does NOT touch CLAUDE.md, .gitignore, or .beads/.
 
-Templates live in the package as `templates/<name>.md` data files. The
-CLI is the public boundary; dispatchers, scaffold scripts, and any
-other consumer reads templates via this surface rather than by path.
+Role-prompt templates live in the package as `templates/<name>.md` data
+files. CLAUDE.md primer templates live as `templates/claude/<shop_type>.md`.
+The .gitignore template lives as `templates/gitignore.template`. The CLI
+is the public boundary; dispatchers, scaffold scripts, and any other
+consumer reads templates via this surface rather than by path.
 """
 from __future__ import annotations
 
 import argparse
+import subprocess
 import sys
 from importlib.resources import files
+from pathlib import Path
 
 
 _TEMPLATES_PKG = "shop_templates.templates"
+_CLAUDE_TEMPLATES_PKG = "shop_templates.templates.claude"
+
+# The canonical role-set assignment per shop type. The bootstrap surface
+# uses this to decide which role files to pour into .claude/agents/ for
+# a freshly-bootstrapped shop, and the update surface uses it to
+# reconcile an existing shop's .claude/agents/ against the current
+# canonical set.
+_CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS: dict[str, tuple[str, ...]] = {
+    "bc": ("bc-implementer", "bc-reviewer"),
+    "lead": ("lead-po", "lead-architect"),
+}
+
+_SHOP_TYPES = tuple(_CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS.keys())
+
+
+# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Public template-access surface (importable; not just CLI-internal).
+#
+# Per scenario 0cce58eb573d3c91, the CLAUDE.md primer template must be
+# accessible "through its public template-access surface" and must NOT
+# be read from any path under the product's top-level working directory
+# at lookup time — i.e., it must be served from importlib.resources
+# package data, the same boundary the role-prompt templates use.
+# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
 
 
 def _list_template_names() -> list[str]:
-    """Return the sorted list of available template names (no extension)."""
+    """Return the sorted list of available role-template names.
+
+    Walks only the top-level package directory; sub-directories (e.g.
+    `templates/claude/`) are skipped. This keeps `shop-templates list`
+    output stable as new categories of package data (CLAUDE primers,
+    gitignore template) are added.
+    """
     pkg_root = files(_TEMPLATES_PKG)
     names = []
     for resource in pkg_root.iterdir():
+        # `is_file()` plus `.md` suffix isolates role-prompt templates
+        # from sub-directories like `templates/claude/`.
+        if not resource.is_file():
+            continue
         name = resource.name
         if name.endswith(".md"):
             names.append(name[: -len(".md")])
@@ -33,13 +83,56 @@ def _list_template_names() -> list[str]:
 
 
 def _read_template(name: str) -> str | None:
-    """Return the named template's content, or None if no such template."""
+    """Return the named role-template's content, or None if no such template."""
     try:
         return (files(_TEMPLATES_PKG) / f"{name}.md").read_text()
     except FileNotFoundError:
         return None
 
 
+def read_claude_md_primer(shop_type: str) -> str:
+    """Return the canonical CLAUDE.md primer template body for a shop type.
+
+    The body is loaded from package data via importlib.resources; it is
+    never read from a filesystem path under the product working
+    directory. The returned string is the source of truth from which the
+    bootstrap entry point generates a target directory's top-level
+    CLAUDE.md.
+    """
+    if shop_type not in _CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS:
+        raise ValueError(
+            f"unknown shop type {shop_type!r}; accepted values: "
+            f"{', '.join(_SHOP_TYPES)}"
+        )
+    resource = files(_CLAUDE_TEMPLATES_PKG) / f"{shop_type}.md"
+    return resource.read_text()
+
+
+def read_gitignore_template() -> str:
+    """Return the canonical top-level .gitignore template body."""
+    return (files(_TEMPLATES_PKG) / "gitignore.template").read_text()
+
+
+def canonical_role_set(shop_type: str) -> tuple[str, ...]:
+    """Return the canonical role-prompt-template names for a shop type.
+
+    The bootstrap surface treats these names as the canonical role set
+    for the given shop type — both `bootstrap` (initial pour) and
+    `update` (reconciliation) work from this mapping.
+    """
+    if shop_type not in _CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS:
+        raise ValueError(
+            f"unknown shop type {shop_type!r}; accepted values: "
+            f"{', '.join(_SHOP_TYPES)}"
+        )
+    return _CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS[shop_type]
+
+
+# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
+# `list` / `show` subcommands — pre-existing surface.
+# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+
 def _cmd_list(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
     for name in _list_template_names():
         print(name)
@@ -60,6 +153,302 @@ def _cmd_show(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
     return 0
 
 
+# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
+# `bootstrap` subcommand
+# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+
+def _render_claude_md(shop_type: str, shop_name: str) -> str:
+    """Render the CLAUDE.md primer for a shop type by substituting the
+    shop name. The primer must name the shop's identity (its shop_name
+    and its role set) per scenario a15dac2f87549b8a.
+    """
+    primer = read_claude_md_primer(shop_type)
+    return primer.replace("{{SHOP_NAME}}", shop_name)
+
+
+def _bd_init_in(target: Path) -> int:
+    """Invoke `bd init` as a subprocess in the target directory.
+
+    Per scenario 0c6f1c5d9bc4226e, .beads/ MUST be initialized by a
+    subprocess named "bd" with first argument "init", NOT by importing
+    bd / beads internals into the running shop-templates process and
+    NOT by writing .beads/ files from shop-templates directly. This
+    function is the single place where that subprocess is spawned.
+
+    Per scenario 32d99f6d4a2dad37 (lead-2gr round 2 of lead-k8v's
+    bd-init side-effect closure), `bd init` MUST be invoked with the
+    `--skip-agents` flag. That flag is bd's own opt-out for the
+    AGENTS.md / .claude/settings.json / CLAUDE.md-beads-block append
+    that bd otherwise applies during `bd init`. The shop's canonical
+    agent surface is .claude/agents/*.md alone (poured by bootstrap
+    itself); bd's agent-surface generation is duplicative at best and
+    contradictory at worst, so we suppress it. Verified against bd
+    1.0.3: `BD_NON_INTERACTIVE=1 bd init --skip-agents --quiet` in a
+    fresh probe dir produces .beads/ + .gitignore and no AGENTS.md,
+    no .claude/.
+    """
+    result = subprocess.run(
+        ["bd", "init", "--skip-agents"],
+        cwd=str(target),
+        capture_output=True,
+        text=True,
+    )
+    if result.returncode != 0:
+        print(
+            f"shop-templates bootstrap: `bd init` failed in {target!s} "
+            f"(exit {result.returncode}); stderr:\n{result.stderr}",
+            file=sys.stderr,
+        )
+    return result.returncode
```

**Probe 3 (CRITICAL): Does `_cmd_update` short-circuit BEFORE any modification?** Let me verify by inspection then with a real probe — the short-circuit must precede the `agents_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)` and pour loop. Reading the code:

```python
def _cmd_update(args):
    err = _validate_shop_type(args.shop_type, "update")
    if err is not None:
        return 2
    err = _validate_target(args.target, "update")  # <-- HERE, before any side effects
    if err is not None:
        return 2
    target = Path(args.target)
    ...
    agents_dir.mkdir(...)   # only reached if both valid
```

Good — short-circuit precedes all mutation. The scenario step def empirically verifies byte+mtime equality, file set equality, and top-level scaffold + .beads/ equality, so this is well-tested. Let me also verify with a real probe by hand that mtime matches:

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-13 20:05:57Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Empirically verify update with no --target leaves scaffold untouched

```bash
/workspaces/shopsystem-product/.venv/bin/python <<'PYEOF'
import tempfile, os, sys
from pathlib import Path
sys.path.insert(0, "/workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-templates/src")
from shop_templates.cli import main

with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    target = Path(td) / "shop"
    target.mkdir()
    # bootstrap
    rc = main(["bootstrap", "--shop-type", "bc", "--shop-name", "probe-shop", "--target", str(target)])
    print(f"bootstrap rc={rc}")
    # snapshot mtimes & bytes
    snap = {}
    for p in target.rglob("*"):
        if p.is_file():
            st = p.stat()
            snap[str(p.relative_to(target))] = (p.read_bytes(), st.st_mtime_ns)
    # call update WITHOUT --target
    import io, contextlib
    stderr_buf = io.StringIO()
    with contextlib.redirect_stderr(stderr_buf):
        rc2 = main(["update", "--shop-type", "bc"])
    print(f"update rc={rc2}")
    print(f"stderr: {stderr_buf.getvalue().strip()!r}")
    # verify nothing changed
    changed = []
    for p in target.rglob("*"):
        if p.is_file():
            rel = str(p.relative_to(target))
            if rel not in snap:
                changed.append(("NEW", rel))
                continue
            b, m = snap[rel]
            st = p.stat()
            if p.read_bytes() != b:
                changed.append(("BYTES", rel))
            if st.st_mtime_ns != m:
                changed.append(("MTIME", rel, m, st.st_mtime_ns))
    for rel in snap:
        if not (target / rel).is_file():
            changed.append(("GONE", rel))
    if changed:
        print(f"CHANGED: {changed}")
    else:
        print("ALL UNCHANGED — short-circuit works")
PYEOF
```

```text
bootstrap rc=0
update rc=2
stderr: 'shop-templates update: missing required argument: --target. Provide the path to the target repository.'
ALL UNCHANGED — short-circuit works
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-13 20:06:28Z)**_

108 passed, 0 failed, 0 deselected, 0 skipped. The implementer's "1 deselected" was a misstatement (likely from running with `-k`); the real state is cleaner than reported.

**Adversarial reflection on scenario-intent fidelity:**

1. *Is this a "clever shortcut" satisfying only the literal text?* No. `_validate_target` is a proper input-shape gate paralleling `_validate_shop_name`/`_validate_shop_type`. It returns a sentinel, the caller returns exit 2, stderr names `--target` literally. The diagnostic shape, exit code, and short-circuit before mutation are all genuine properties — no hard-coded path-special-casing.

2. *Adjacent unpinned cases I considered:*
   - **Empty-string `--target ""`** — argparse accepts it as a valid string, `_validate_target` checks only `is None`, so `Path("")` runs. `Path("")` is `Path('.')` which would scaffold into CWD. Scenarios do NOT pin this. *Dismissing as out-of-scope:* the dispatch is narrowly scoped to "no `--target` argument at all" (the TypeError trigger), and empty-string is a different bug class (would not have crashed). Per dispatch description: "Two same-shape missing-required-arg gaps remain on `--target`" — only no-arg. Flagging would be over-reach; the implementer mirrored the exact pattern of `_validate_shop_name` which has the same empty-string gap.
   - **`--target=/nonexistent/deeply/nested/path`** — Path() accepts it; `.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)` may or may not succeed; scenarios don't pin. Out-of-scope; this is a separate "target validation depth" question.
   - **Update with `--target` but no shop-type** — already pinned (`_validate_shop_type` runs first). Validated by reading order.

3. *Step-def probing:*
   - `then_witness_still_pristine` checks `.claude/agents`, `.beads`, `CLAUDE.md`, `.gitignore` — comprehensive for "no scaffold written."
   - `then_snapshot_unchanged` checks file-set equality + byte equality + mtime equality + walks via rglob. Robust.
   - `then_top_level_scaffold_unchanged` additionally covers `.beads/` and top-level scaffold. Robust.
   - One subtle concern: `_real_witness_for_alias` creates the directory if it doesn't exist (`real.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)`) — the Given says "an existing empty directory" so this is fine, but it also means it pre-creates the alias-mapped directory as part of the Given. The Given then asserts emptiness immediately. Not a real gap.

4. *Step regex teeth:* The When regex for bootstrap matches `'I invoke the "shop-templates" bootstrap entry point with --shop-type "..." and --shop-name "..." but with no --target argument'` — quite specific; can't accidentally match the normal When. Good.

5. *Mechanism observation candidate:* Nothing surfaces here that's load-bearing for the next BC. The pattern is a clean parallel of the prior `--shop-name` fix, so the role templates' anti-rationalization language did its job; no mechanism observation needed.

Sign off.

- hash: 78847a74ac3108c5
  tags:
  - '@scenario_hash:78847a74ac3108c5'
  - '@bc:shopsystem-templates'
  gherkin: "Feature: Event-driven shop activation: canonical .claude/settings.json\
    \ + bootstrap extension (brief 003)\n\n  @scenario_hash:78847a74ac3108c5 @bc:shopsystem-templates\n\
    \  Scenario Outline: the canonical \".claude/settings.json\" template for each\
    \ shop type expresses the SessionStart activation hook so that, on a host where\
    \ \"inotifywait\" is not on PATH, the hook surfaces a visible failure rather than\
    \ silently degrading to a no-watcher state\n  Given a host environment where the\
    \ executable \"inotifywait\" is not present on PATH\n  And an existing git repository\
    \ at a target directory \"<target>\" that was previously bootstrapped as a \"\
    <shop_type>\" shop named \"<shop_name>\"\n  When a Claude Code session starts\
    \ in the target directory and the \"SessionStart\" hooks declared by \".claude/settings.json\"\
    \ execute\n  Then the session surfaces a visible diagnostic identifying \"inotifywait\"\
    \ or \"inotify-tools\" as the missing prerequisite\n  And the session does not\
    \ arrive at the agent's first turn in a state where the activation hook has silently\
    \ produced no watcher and no diagnostic\n\n  Examples:\n    | shop_type | shop_name\
    \               | target                       |\n    | bc        | shopsystem-messaging\
    \    | /tmp/example-bc-shop         |\n    | lead      | shopsystem-product  \
    \    | /tmp/example-lead-shop       |\n\n"
- hash: cc82fd037ee3f5c6
  tags:
  - '@scenario_hash:cc82fd037ee3f5c6'
  - '@bc:shopsystem-templates'
  gherkin: "Feature: Event-driven shop activation: canonical .claude/settings.json\
    \ + bootstrap extension (brief 003)\n\n  @scenario_hash:cc82fd037ee3f5c6 @bc:shopsystem-templates\n\
    \  Scenario: on session start in a bootstrapped lead shop, every BC CLI installed\
    \ into the product venv (for example \"shop-msg\", \"shop-templates\") resolves\
    \ invocations to the current source tree of that BC's clone under \"repos/\",\
    \ so an edit to a BC's source is reflected in the next CLI invocation without\
    \ an intervening manual reinstall\n  Given an existing git repository at a target\
    \ directory \"/tmp/example-lead-shop\" that was previously bootstrapped as a \"\
    lead\" shop named \"shopsystem-product\"\n  And the target directory contains\
    \ a sibling BC clone at \"repos/shopsystem-messaging/\" whose installed package\
    \ name is \"shop-msg\"\n  And a fresh edit has been made to a Python source file\
    \ under \"repos/shopsystem-messaging/src/\" that changes observable CLI behavior\
    \ of \"shop-msg\"\n  When a Claude Code session starts in the target directory\
    \ and the \"SessionStart\" hooks declared by \".claude/settings.json\" complete\n\
    \  And the agent invokes the \"shop-msg\" CLI through its normal entry point\n\
    \  Then the invocation exhibits the post-edit observable behavior\n  And no manual\
    \ \"pip install\" step is required between the source edit and the invocation\n\
    \n"

    \  Scenario Outline: each shop-type's canonical \".claude/settings.json\" template\
    \ names only its own shop-type-appropriate watch target and does not name the\
    \ other shop-type's watch target, so the two variants are not interchangeable\
    \ and a BC shop cannot accidentally arm a lead-shop watcher (or vice versa)\n\
    \  When I ask the \"shop-templates\" package for the canonical \".claude/settings.json\"\
    \ template for shop type \"<shop_type>\" through its public template-access surface\n\
    \  Then the returned body contains the literal substring \"<own_watch_target>\"\
    \n  And the returned body does not contain the literal substring \"<other_watch_target>\"\
    \n\n  Examples:\n    | shop_type | own_watch_target | other_watch_target |\n \
    \   | bc        | inbox/           | repos/*/outbox/    |\n    | lead      | repos/*/outbox/\
    \  | inbox/             |\n\n"

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
