_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-13 20:23:47Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `/workspaces/shopsystem-product/briefs/003-event-driven-shop-activation.md`

```text
1	# Brief 003 — Event-driven shop activation
2	
3	**Status:** draft (2026-05-13)
4	**Authors:** dstengle, Claude (lead-po)
5	**Beads:** [`lead-0h1`](#) (this brief)
6	**Anchored to:** user-driver observation 2026-05-12:
7	*"The biggest issue right now is coordinating work between multiple agents.
8	Extending shopsystem is somewhat slow without having things be more event
9	driven."* Empirically validated this session: an `inotifywait`-armed
10	Monitor watching the lead-shop's BC outboxes delivered sub-second
11	event-fire on `lead-79q` `work_done` arrival, reconciled without polling.
12	
13	## Point of intent
14	
15	Today, every shop — lead and BC — communicates via `shop-msg` mailboxes
16	on the filesystem, but **no shop is reactive to inbound messages**.
17	Reactivity is approximated by manual polling (`shop-msg pending`),
18	scheduled checks (`/loop`), or out-of-band human attention. The cost is
19	agent-coordination latency that scales with shop count and dispatch
20	cadence: a lead shop dispatching to four BCs and waiting on four
21	`work_done`s pays four manual-check round-trips per cycle, and a BC
22	implementer sitting on a fresh inbox message does not know it has work
23	until the human nudges it.
24	
25	The brief commits the shop-system to **event-driven activation as a
26	shop's default posture**: every shop, on every session, arms a watcher
27	on its inbound mailbox surface so that arrival of a message wakes the
28	session immediately. The activation mechanism rides on top of the
29	filesystem realization that `shop-msg` currently uses, but it does not
30	break encapsulation — the event tells the agent *that something
31	happened*; reading *what happened* still goes through `shop-msg`.
32	
33	The brief carries **two invariants**, **five scope items** (three core,
34	two adjacent), and an **explicit out-of-scope boundary**.
35	
36	## The two invariants
37	
38	### Invariant 1 — Every shop is reactive on session start
39	
40	No shop should require polling to discover inbound messages. Every
41	shop's session-start sequence arms a watcher on its inbound mailbox
42	surface so that filesystem-level arrival of an inbound message produces
43	an in-session notification to the active agent. The watcher is armed
44	**before the agent does any other work**, so the reactive posture holds
45	across the entire session — not just after a manual check.
46	
47	The watcher's watch target is **shop-type-specific**:
48	
49	- **BC shop** watches its own `inbox/` (where the lead drops inbound
50	  messages targeted at this BC).
51	- **Lead shop** watches `repos/*/outbox/` (where every BC drops its
52	  outbound responses; the lead-shop's reconciliation work fires when
53	  any BC posts `work_done`, `clarify`, or a `mechanism_observation`).
54	
55	The mechanism — what filesystem-event subsystem, what tool, what
56	pipeline shape — is **realization detail the Architect commits to in
57	scenarios**. The empirical work that grounds this brief used
58	`stdbuf -oL inotifywait -m -e create,moved_to ...` piped into a
59	buffer-line-aware filter. The validated shape is reproduced under scope
60	item B as the reference realization; deviation is allowed, but the
61	end-to-end property the invariant pins — sub-second event-fire when an
62	inbound message lands — must hold.
63	
64	### Invariant 2 — Activation honors brief 001's encapsulation
65	
66	The activation mechanism fires a **wake signal**; it does not deliver
67	**content**. When the watcher fires, the agent learns "something arrived
68	in the watched directory." The agent then reads the actual message via
69	`shop-msg read inbox <work_id>` (BC side) or `shop-msg read outbox
70	<work_id> --bc <name>` (lead side) — the same surface every other
71	consumer uses, per
72	[brief 001 invariant 1](001-inter-shop-messaging-encapsulation.md).
73	
74	The watch path the activation mechanism uses (e.g., `inbox/`,
75	`repos/*/outbox/`) is **incidentally** the filesystem realization
76	`shop-msg` happens to use today. That is acceptable for the activation
77	mechanism because it operates **below** the messaging layer — it is a
78	process-wake signal, not a consumer-of-messages. The watcher does not
79	parse YAML, does not extract `message_type`, does not act on the
80	content of the file it sees touched; it only signals "wake up."
81	
82	**Corollary — activation adapts when the transport changes.** When
83	`shop-msg`'s storage shifts (filesystem → SQLite → daemon → queue), the
84	activation mechanism's realization shifts with it. A future
85	`shop-msg watch` subcommand is the natural place to land that
86	abstraction; this brief does not commit to it, but the invariant
87	**permits and anticipates** it. The brief's scope is: **make every shop
88	reactive on the current transport, in a shape that can evolve when the
89	transport evolves**.
90	
91	## Three core scope items
92	
93	### A — Canonical `.claude/settings.json` template per shop type
94	
95	Two new package-data files ship via `shopsystem-templates`, parallel to
96	the role-prompt templates already in
97	`shop_templates.templates/`:
98	
99	- A **lead-shop variant** carrying a `SessionStart` hook that arms a
100	  Monitor watching `repos/*/outbox/`.
101	- A **BC-shop variant** carrying a `SessionStart` hook that arms a
102	  Monitor watching the shop's own `inbox/`.
103	
104	Each variant is canonical (PO-authored, framework-property), versioned
105	alongside the role templates, and **delivered to consumers exclusively
106	through the `shop-templates` CLI** — same encapsulation discipline
107	brief 002 invariant applies to the inline `.claude/agents/*.md` copies.
108	No shop hand-edits its `.claude/settings.json` to install the
109	activation hook; the canonical template is the source of truth.
110	
111	The two variants share the same JSON shape (so that the substitution /
112	update mechanics in brief 002 scope item C apply uniformly) and differ
113	only in the hook content their `SessionStart` carries. The shape choice
114	— `command`-type hook running a script, `prompt`-type hook executing
115	arm-Monitor instructions, or another shape Claude Code's hook API
116	supports — is **the Architect's call** during pre-state on Claude
117	Code's hook surface. The brief commits **what** the hook arms; it does
118	not commit **how** the hook is expressed in `settings.json`.
119	
120	The current lead-shop `.claude/settings.json` (this repo) already
121	carries a `SessionStart` `bd prime` hook. The canonical lead-shop
122	variant **composes** these — the existing `bd prime` hook (operational
123	hygiene) remains; the new activation hook is added alongside it. The
124	two-hook composition is the canonical shape, not a one-or-the-other
125	cut.
126	
127	### B — SessionStart hook content (reference realization)
128	
129	The hook's payload arms a Claude Code `Monitor` on a pipeline of the
130	shape validated in this session. The empirical-validated reference
131	realization is named here so the Architect has a concrete target to
132	codify in scenarios, but the Architect may deviate if pre-state on
133	Claude Code's Monitor surface or `inotify-tools` packaging reveals a
134	better shape.
135	
136	**Reference pipeline (lead side):**
137	
138	```
139	stdbuf -oL inotifywait -m -e create,moved_to --format '%w%f' repos/*/outbox/ 2>&1 \
140	  | stdbuf -oL /usr/bin/grep -E '\.yaml$|inotifywait:'
141	```
142	
143	**Reference pipeline (BC side):**
144	
145	```
146	stdbuf -oL inotifywait -m -e create,moved_to --format '%w%f' inbox/ 2>&1 \
147	  | stdbuf -oL /usr/bin/grep -E '\.yaml$|inotifywait:'
148	```
149	
150	Four properties this shape carries that the Architect must preserve
151	(deviation in form, not in property):
152	
153	1. **Line-buffered output.** `stdbuf -oL` on every pipeline stage
154	   forces `setvbuf` to line-buffering mode via `LD_PRELOAD`. Without
155	   it, libc default behavior block-buffers stdout when piped (not a
156	   TTY); single low-volume events sit in a ~4KB buffer and never
157	   reach the Monitor harness. The Architect MUST commit a
158	   stage-by-stage line-buffering guarantee, however realized.
159	2. **Alias bypass for `grep`.** The lead-shop's shell snapshot
160	   aliases `grep` to `ugrep -G --ignore-files ...`. `ugrep`'s
161	   `--line-buffered` flag does NOT flush reliably end-to-end in
162	   this harness; `/usr/bin/grep` does. Whatever filter the Architect
163	   commits to, it MUST bypass the shell alias and use a flushing
164	   implementation. Naming `/usr/bin/grep` explicitly is one valid
165	   shape; using a non-`grep` filter (e.g., `awk`) is another.
166	3. **Startup-chatter and error visibility.** `2>&1` merges
167	   `inotifywait`'s startup messages ("Setting up watches", "Watches
168	   established") and error output into stdout; the filter regex
169	   passes both message-arrival lines (`\.yaml$`) and `inotifywait:`
170	   diagnostic lines through so silent failures (no permission,
171	   `inotify-tools` not installed, watch-limit exhausted) surface as
172	   notifications rather than producing a black-hole watcher.
173	4. **Monitor-as-persistent-arming.** The `inotifywait -m` (monitor
174	   mode) is the right shape so the watcher persists for the session;
175	   the Monitor harness is configured `persistent: true` so the
176	   pipeline restarts on its own exit. The brief commits the
177	   property — arming is once-per-session, behavior is for-the-session
178	   — and lets the Architect name the parameter shape.
179	
180	Watch directories are named **explicitly per shop type** in the
181	canonical hook content (the lead-side hook hardcodes `repos/*/outbox/`;
182	the BC-side hook hardcodes `inbox/`). The brief does NOT introduce
183	parametrisation of the watch path: a lead is a lead and a BC is a BC,
184	and their watch targets are framework property, not per-shop
185	customisation.
186	
187	### C — Bootstrap writes `.claude/settings.json`
188	
189	[Brief 002](002-shop-bootstrap-cli-surface.md) commits a CLI-driven
190	bootstrap surface for new shops. This brief **extends** that surface:
191	the generated surface produced by `shop-templates` bootstrap now
192	includes `.claude/settings.json`, per the shop-type variant defined in
193	scope item A.
194	
195	Specifically:
196	
197	- The **generated surface** under brief 002 scope item B gains one
198	  more entry: `.claude/settings.json` (per shop type). The file is
199	  laid down at init alongside `.claude/agents/*.md`, `CLAUDE.md`,
200	  `.beads/`, and `.gitignore`.
201	- The **update operation** under brief 002 scope item C re-pours
202	  `.claude/settings.json` from the current canonical variant when
203	  the canonical evolves. Same posture as `.claude/agents/*.md`: the
204	  file is **bootstrap-managed** (per brief 002 scope item D's
205	  managed-vs-shop-owned cut), not hand-edited and not init-only.
206	  Rationale: the activation hook is framework property — it enforces
207	  reactivity discipline the same way role prompts enforce role
208	  discipline. Letting a shop drift from canonical means the shop is
209	  silently no longer reactive, which is the failure mode this brief
210	  exists to prevent.
211	- A shop that wants to **extend** its `settings.json` with hooks
212	  beyond the canonical set faces the same constraint
213	  `.claude/agents/*.md` faces: the canonical content is replaceable;
214	  extensions live elsewhere. Whether "elsewhere" means an
215	  additional file, a project-local override, or something else is a
216	  Claude-Code-hook-surface question the Architect addresses in
217	  scenarios (or escalates if pre-state surfaces it as a PDR).
218	
219	Treating `.claude/settings.json` as bootstrap-managed (not init-only,
220	unlike `CLAUDE.md`) is a **delta from brief 002 scope item D**'s
221	managed/init-only cut. The cut still applies — content that exists to
222	enforce framework discipline is managed; content that exists to express
223	per-shop intent is shop-owned — but `.claude/settings.json` falls on
224	the managed side. The activation hook IS framework discipline; nothing
225	about it expresses per-shop intent.
226	
227	## Two adjacent scope items
228	
229	### D — Prereqs naming
230	
231	The activation mechanism depends on host-level packages: `inotify-tools`
232	(for `inotifywait`) and `coreutils` (for `stdbuf`, almost always
233	already present on Linux). The bootstrap surface **names these
234	prereqs** in the generated shop scaffold so a downstream operator
235	running into a missing tool has a documented expectation to point at.
236	
237	Realization of the naming is **the Architect's call**: the prereqs may
238	live in a section of the generated `CLAUDE.md` (per brief 002 scope
239	item E's substitution surface), in a generated `PREREQS.md`, or as
240	inline comments in `.claude/settings.json` (if Claude Code's
241	settings.json schema permits comments — which is itself a pre-state
242	question). The brief commits that the prereqs are **named in the
243	generated surface**; the surface entry is the Architect's pick.
244	
245	**Out of scope under D:** installing the prereqs. Bootstrap is a
246	shop-shape concern; installing host-level packages is host
247	infrastructure (devcontainer `postCreate`, Dockerfile, NixOS module,
248	or whatever the operator runs). The brief acknowledges devcontainer
249	postCreate exists as the natural install site but does NOT commit
250	this brief to writing it.
251	
252	### E — Venv install drift mitigation
253	
254	[`lead-xq0`](#) (P2) names the venv drift problem: the product venv's
255	installed CLIs (`shop-msg`, `shop-templates`) drift from BC source as
256	BCs land work, because the install mode is non-editable. The Architect
257	on `lead-79q` worked around the drift by direct `ls` of `inbox/` —
258	a brief-001-invariant-1 violation forced by tooling staleness.
259	Re-installing manually via `pip install -e repos/shopsystem-messaging/`
260	fixed the surface.
261	
262	This brief folds the mitigation into the activation surface. **PO
263	stance committed:** the canonical `SessionStart` hook (scope item B)
264	**also** ensures the product venv reflects current BC source, so that
265	the first thing every fresh session does is sync the venv before
266	arming the watcher and before the agent attempts any `shop-msg`
267	operation. The committed property is: **on session start, the venv's
268	installed BCs are equivalent to their current source.**
269	
270	Realization of the property is **the Architect's call**. Three
271	candidate shapes pre-state should evaluate:
272	
273	1. **Hook-time `pip install -e repos/*/`.** The SessionStart hook
274	   runs the install before arming Monitor. Pro: closes drift even if
275	   bootstrap installed non-editable. Con: paid every session; slow if
276	   BCs proliferate.
277	2. **Bootstrap-time editable install.** Brief 002's bootstrap (or a
278	   sibling product-level bootstrap that composes per-shop bootstrap
279	   with venv setup) installs BCs editable from the start, so source
280	   changes flow without reinstall. Pro: zero per-session cost. Con:
281	   does not help shops created before this lands; needs a one-time
282	   migration.
283	3. **Both.** Bootstrap installs editable AND the hook re-runs install
284	   if it detects drift (e.g., via `pip show` version comparison or
285	   a sentinel file). Pro: belt-and-suspenders. Con: complexity for
286	   what should be a default.
287	
288	**PO leaning (not a commit):** option 2 is the right shape long-term;
289	option 1 is a useful safety net during the transition; option 3 is
290	the realization the Architect is likely to commit to if pre-state
291	surfaces real drift cases not covered by 2 alone. **The PO commits
292	the property (venv-source equivalence on session start) and the
293	folding (this brief subsumes `lead-xq0`); the Architect commits the
294	shape.**
295	
296	If pre-state on E surfaces design tension large enough that the cut
297	between scope-of-this-brief and product-level-bootstrap (brief 002's
298	explicit out-of-scope) becomes blurry — for example, if "venv setup"
299	turns out to belong cleanly to the product-level workflow and not to
300	per-shop bootstrap or to per-shop SessionStart — the Architect flags
301	it and **E escalates to its own brief** (or a PDR), without blocking
302	A-D. A-D are independent of E and can dispatch regardless of how the
303	E call resolves.
304	
305	## Out of scope — named explicitly
306	
307	**Session supervision.** The user starts and restarts Claude Code
308	sessions manually. The brief commits per-session reactivity, not
309	session lifecycle management. No automated session restart, no
310	devcontainer auto-launch of Claude Code, no tmux/screen wrapper, no
311	systemd unit. If a session crashes or is closed, the next session
312	arms the watcher again on start; until then, the shop is unreactive.
313	
314	**Cloud / distributed activation.** Cross-host activation (a watcher
315	in shop X firing on a message dropped by shop Y on a different machine)
316	is a follow-on brief. Filesystem events do not cross hosts; a future
317	brief commits a different activation primitive (HTTP webhook, queue
318	notify, `shop-msg watch` subcommand fronting a daemon) when the
319	shop-system becomes distributed. Brief 003 commits to **local-filesystem
320	activation appropriate to the current transport**.
321	
322	**Long-running persistent-session optimization.** Each Claude Code
323	session is a normal human-started session. The brief does NOT
324	introduce a daemon mode, a persistent agent process, or a
325	context-window optimisation strategy for long sessions. The user
326	manages session length via the normal Claude Code controls (compact,
327	restart); the brief is invisible to those concerns.
328	
329	**Replacing `inotify` with something else.** The brief's activation
330	mechanism IS `inotifywait` on the filesystem, because that is the
331	realization the empirical work validated against the transport
332	`shop-msg` currently uses. A future `shop-msg watch` subcommand that
333	abstracts the watch primitive away from the filesystem is **adjacent
334	future work**, anticipated by invariant 2's corollary but not
335	committed by this brief.
336	
337	**devcontainer `postCreate` host installation.** Per scope item D,
338	host-level packages (`inotify-tools`, `coreutils`) are named in the
339	generated shop scaffold so an operator has a documented expectation,
340	but the brief does NOT commit to writing the devcontainer or any
341	other host infrastructure that installs them. That is host
342	infrastructure, a separate concern from shop-shape.
343	
344	**Lead-shop drain automation.** `shop-msg drain` (auto-loading inbound
345	messages into `bd`) remains deferred per
346	[brief 001 out-of-scope](001-inter-shop-messaging-encapsulation.md).
347	Event-driven activation makes manual-drain friction more visible —
348	the agent wakes immediately, instead of discovering messages on a
349	schedule — but the brief does not commit drain automation. A
350	follow-on opens when manual-drain friction surfaces under real-product
351	BC use, per brief 001's framing.
352	
353	## Sequencing
354	
355	- **A, B, C, D** are a coherent unit. They target `shopsystem-templates`
356	  and extend the brief 002 bootstrap surface. They can be authored as
357	  scenarios and dispatched together once the Architect has verified
358	  pre-state on (1) Claude Code's `SessionStart` hook surface — what
359	  payload shapes the hook supports, whether `prompt`-type hooks can
360	  arm Monitor, whether `command`-type hooks are appropriate for the
361	  arming work; (2) `inotify-tools`' availability assumption (is it
362	  reasonable to require it on every host where a shopsystem shop
363	  runs?); (3) brief 002's current bootstrap pre-state, so the
364	  `.claude/settings.json` generation slots in cleanly.
365	
366	- **E** depends on the venv install-mode pre-state. The Architect's
367	  pre-state on E (specifically: confirming the current installs are
368	  non-editable, confirming the bootstrap install path, identifying
369	  which of the three candidate shapes pre-state actually supports)
370	  may surface design tension that escalates E to its own brief or to
371	  a PDR. **The PDR escalation trigger fires AT the Architect's
372	  pre-state verification on E**, per the same pattern brief 002
373	  applies to its scope item E.
374	
375	- **Dispatch may proceed immediately on brief close.** No in-flight
376	  chain blocks brief 003 — brief 002's dispatch chain
377	  ([`lead-1uo`](#) / [`lead-03r`](#)) is complete, the BC inbox is
378	  empty, and PDR-001's role-complete restructure
379	  ([`lead-kq0`](#)) is in progress but does not collide (this brief
380	  adds new package data, it does not modify the templates the
381	  restructure is working on).
382	
383	- **Soft sequencing constraint relative to brief 002:** brief 003
384	  scope item C extends brief 002's bootstrap surface. The two
385	  briefs' scope items co-locate in the same BC's work. Authoring
386	  may proceed in parallel; dispatch ordering should let brief 002's
387	  bootstrap-surface scenarios land first so that brief 003's
388	  `.claude/settings.json` addition slots into an already-bootstrapped
389	  generated surface rather than racing it. **This is a dispatch
390	  ordering hint, not a hard constraint.** The Architect may bundle
391	  the two briefs' scope into a single `assign_scenarios` if pre-state
392	  surfaces that as cleaner.
393	
394	## Vehicle hints (Architect's call)
395	
396	For the Architect's awareness during pre-state verification — not as
397	a prejudgment of the discriminator:
398	
399	- The activation hook as a whole is **net-new framework property** on
400	  `shopsystem-templates` — no canonical `.claude/settings.json`
401	  template exists today. Net-new capability points to
402	  `assign_scenarios`.
403	- Scope item C extends brief 002's `shop-templates` bootstrap surface;
404	  if brief 002's surface is already dispatched and partly built when
405	  brief 003 dispatches, C lands as `request_bugfix` (tightening the
406	  generated surface to include the new file) — otherwise it folds into
407	  the brief 002 `assign_scenarios`.
408	- Scope item E's vehicle depends on its realization. Hook-time
409	  `pip install` (option 1) is a behavior change to the generated
410	  hook, parallel to A/B/C — same `assign_scenarios`. Bootstrap-time
411	  editable install (option 2) tightens brief 002's bootstrap behavior
412	  — likely `request_bugfix`. The Architect picks after pre-state.
413	
414	These are hints. The Architect's `PRE-STATE DETERMINES VEHICLE —
415	VERIFIED EMPIRICALLY` posture stands.
416	
417	## Grounding artifacts
418	
419	- [`briefs/001-inter-shop-messaging-encapsulation.md`](001-inter-shop-messaging-encapsulation.md)
420	  — invariant 1 (`shop-msg` as sole messaging surface); the
421	  encapsulation invariant this brief's activation mechanism must
422	  respect (event signals the wake; CLI delivers content).
423	- [`briefs/002-shop-bootstrap-cli-surface.md`](002-shop-bootstrap-cli-surface.md)
424	  — the bootstrap surface this brief extends (`.claude/settings.json`
425	  is added to the generated surface defined in scope item B; update
426	  semantics from scope item C apply).
427	- [`findings/from-prototype-1.md` finding 6](../findings/from-prototype-1.md)
428	  — "package CLIs are the integration boundary"; activation operates
429	  **below** the messaging layer (process-wake signal) and so does not
430	  enter the consumer-of-messages role finding 6 governs.
431	- [`lead-xq0`](#) — venv drift bug; scope item E folds it in.
432	- [`lead-zmi`](#) — shop-msg PATH ergonomics; **adjacent**, not
433	  folded. Same theme (tooling-availability ergonomics affecting
434	  inter-shop work), different facet (PATH vs install mode).
435	  Independent resolution.
436	- [`.claude/settings.json`](../.claude/settings.json) — current
437	  lead-shop hook content (`bd prime` on `SessionStart` and
438	  `PreCompact`); the canonical lead-shop variant under scope item A
439	  composes with this content.
440	- [`repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/templates/`](../repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/templates/)
441	  — the package-data directory the new canonical `settings.json`
442	  variants ship from.
443	
444	## What this leaves open
445	
446	The brief commits **intent**, not scenarios. Scenarios come after the
447	Architect verifies pre-state and picks vehicles per the discriminator.
448	Specifically:
449	
450	- **Claude Code hook surface details for A and B.** What hook type
451	  (`command` vs `prompt`) carries the arm-Monitor payload, what the
452	  exact `settings.json` JSON shape looks like, and whether the
453	  prompt-content lives inline in JSON or in a referenced script — all
454	  are pre-state-determined. The brief commits **what** the hook does,
455	  not **how** Claude Code carries the doing.
456	- **`inotify-tools` packaging assumption.** Whether to commit
457	  `inotify-tools` as a hard prereq (named in D, with the hook failing
458	  loudly on missing-tool) versus a soft prereq (the hook falls back
459	  to a polling shape on missing-tool) is a realization decision. The
460	  brief commits the property (reactivity on session start); falling
461	  back to polling **silently** violates invariant 1 — so any fallback
462	  must be loud (notification surface) rather than transparent.
463	- **Venv-install realization for E.** The three candidate shapes
464	  (hook-time, bootstrap-time, both) are named with PO leaning toward
465	  option 2 + 1 as belt-and-suspenders, but the commit is the property,
466	  not the shape. Pre-state on the install path picks the realization.
467	- **PDR-vs-brief escalation for E.** As named in the section, if
468	  pre-state on E surfaces that venv setup belongs cleanly to a
469	  product-level workflow rather than per-shop bootstrap or per-shop
470	  SessionStart, E escalates to its own brief or PDR before scenarios
471	  are authored against it. A-D proceed regardless.
472	
473	These are vehicle-level and design-tension questions, not intent-level.
474	The PO's commit is the two invariants + the five scope items + the
475	explicit out-of-scope boundary.
476

All 108 tests pass with the proper PATH. Good baseline. Now let me design my implementation.

Let me first look at the schema/structure of settings.json the BC will produce. Brief 003 §C says lead-shop variant carries `bd prime` AND the inotifywait hook composed together. Let me think about scenario 8 — surfacing diagnostics. The reference pipeline pipes to `grep` with regex matching `inotifywait:` startup-error lines. If `inotifywait` is not on PATH, the bash command will produce a "command not found" error on stderr (which `2>&1` merges to stdout). That alone may not produce a "visible diagnostic identifying inotifywait or inotify-tools as the missing prerequisite" — bash's standard error would say "inotifywait: command not found" — that does name "inotifywait". So `2>&1` plus the pipeline structure already satisfies it.

But the scenario says "the session surfaces a visible diagnostic" — the hook ran but failed. In a Claude Code SessionStart hook, the hook executes a shell command; if it exits non-zero or writes stderr, Claude Code surfaces that. The test should simulate by running the hook command directly with `inotifywait` absent from PATH and assert that the resulting stdout/stderr contains "inotifywait".

Now let me write the new settings.json templates. The bc variant should watch `inbox/`, the lead variant should watch `repos/*/outbox/` and compose with `bd prime`.

Now, design the JSON content. Scenario 2 (2a09d13c21d48aa0) requires these substrings: `SessionStart`, `stdbuf -oL inotifywait`, `-m -e create,moved_to`, `2>&1`, `<watch_target>` (replaced per-shop with `inbox/` or `repos/*/outbox/`). Also forbids bare `grep` without `/usr/bin/` prefix or equivalent alias-bypassing form.

Scenario 3 (21769e2a22c36bc7) for lead: `bd prime` AND `stdbuf -oL inotifywait`, and they must be "two distinct hook entries under SessionStart".

Scenario A (a3338bb773fda65e): "bc" template contains "inbox/" but not "repos/*/outbox/" and vice versa. Note: substring "inbox/" appears IN "repos/*/outbox/" lookalike? No — "inbox/" is "inbox/" and "outbox/" is "outbox/". Should be fine. But careful: "repos/*/outbox/" contains "outbox/" not "inbox/", so for bc shop "inbox/" appears and "repos/*/outbox/" does not. For lead "repos/*/outbox/" appears and "inbox/" does not. Wait — would the BC variant's JSON anywhere mention "inbox" without being part of the watch path? Let me ensure the variant doesn't mention the other-side path anywhere.

Let me design the BC variant:

```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "SessionStart": [
      {
        "matcher": "",
        "hooks": [
          {
            "type": "command",
            "command": "stdbuf -oL inotifywait -m -e create,moved_to --format '%w%f' inbox/ 2>&1 | stdbuf -oL /usr/bin/grep -E '\\.yaml$|inotifywait:'"
          }
        ]
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

For lead, compose `bd prime` AND inotifywait as two distinct hook entries:

```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "SessionStart": [
      {
        "matcher": "",
        "hooks": [
          {
            "type": "command",
            "command": "bd prime"
          },
          {
            "type": "command",
            "command": "stdbuf -oL inotifywait -m -e create,moved_to --format '%w%f' repos/*/outbox/ 2>&1 | stdbuf -oL /usr/bin/grep -E '\\.yaml$|inotifywait:'"
          }
        ]
      }
    ],
    "PreCompact": [
      {
        "matcher": "",
        "hooks": [

Actually if `stdbuf -oL inotifywait` is invoked and `inotifywait` is missing, `stdbuf` writes its diagnostic to stderr like `stdbuf: failed to run command 'inotifywait': No such file or directory`. Yes — because `inotifywait:` is a substring of `'inotifywait':` (the trailing `'` is between `t` and `:`, no wait: `'inotifywait':` — that's apostrophe, then `inotifywait`, apostrophe, colon. So `inotifywait:` is NOT a substring (the colon is preceded by `'` not by `t`).

Hmm. The diagnostic might not pass through grep. Let me adjust the regex or approach. The reference pipeline in the brief was:

```
stdbuf -oL inotifywait -m -e create,moved_to --format '%w%f' repos/*/outbox/ 2>&1 \
  | stdbuf -oL /usr/bin/grep -E '\.yaml$|inotifywait:'
```

This expects `inotifywait`'s own diagnostics like `inotifywait: Cannot read inotify events`. But missing `inotifywait` entirely means stdbuf complains, not inotifywait. So the regex wouldn't catch stdbuf's diagnostic.

For scenario 78847a74ac3108c5 to be satisfied, the diagnostic must "surface a visible diagnostic identifying 'inotifywait' or 'inotify-tools' as the missing prerequisite". If stdbuf prints `stdbuf: failed to run command 'inotifywait'` to fd 2 of stdbuf — but actually wait, this is more nuanced. Let me trace:

The hook command is a shell pipeline:
```
stdbuf -oL inotifywait ... 2>&1 | stdbuf -oL /usr/bin/grep ...
```

The `2>&1` applies to the left side of the pipe. If `inotifywait` (the executable that `stdbuf` tries to exec) is missing, `stdbuf` exits non-zero with stderr to fd 2 of stdbuf, which is dup'd to fd 1 of stdbuf BEFORE the pipe directs fd 1 of stdbuf into grep. So grep does receive the line.

But grep then filters by the regex `\.yaml$|inotifywait:`. The stdbuf message is something like `stdbuf: failed to run command 'inotifywait': No such file or directory`. Does this match `inotifywait:`? Let me check: in the message, the sequence `'inotifywait':` has an apostrophe before the colon. So the literal substring `inotifywait:` (lowercase i, n, o, t, i, f, y, w, a, i, t, colon) is NOT present (it's `inotifywait':` — the `:` is preceded by `'`, not by `t`).

To make scenario 8 robust, I have two choices:
(a) Use a regex/filter that matches more broadly. E.g., `inotifywait|stdbuf|grep`.
(b) Wrap the hook command to detect missing executable up front via `command -v inotifywait` and print a clear error.

Option (b) is cleaner. Let me design:

```
command -v inotifywait >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo "shopsystem activation: inotifywait not found on PATH; install 'inotify-tools' to enable event-driven session activation" >&2; exit 1; }; stdbuf -oL inotifywait -m -e create,moved_to --format '%w%f' inbox/ 2>&1 | stdbuf -oL /usr/bin/grep -E '\.yaml$|inotifywait:'
```

But this complicates the substring tests. The substring `stdbuf -oL inotifywait` still appears (after the guard). The substring `-m -e create,moved_to` still appears. The substring `2>&1` still appears. The substring `inbox/` still appears. And the substring `repos/*/outbox/` does not for bc. And no bare `grep` without `/usr/bin/`.

But also, this guard means the hook surfaces a visible diagnostic naming `inotifywait` AND `inotify-tools`.

```text
490	    # "TODO.md" or "NOTES.md" out.
491	    def _is_managed_role_name(stem: str) -> bool:
492	        # Recognize names of the form "<prefix>-<suffix>" where both
493	        # halves are ASCII lowercase letters. This matches every
494	        # canonical role-name pattern shipped today (bc-implementer,
495	        # bc-reviewer, lead-po, lead-architect) and excludes typical
496	        # shop-authored markdown filenames.
497	        if "-" not in stem:
498	            return False
499	        if stem != stem.lower():
500	            return False
501	        if not all(c.islower() or c == "-" for c in stem):
502	            return False
503	        return True
504	
505	    # Step 1: remove managed files whose names are NOT in the current
506	    # canonical role set for this shop type.
507	    for child in list(agents_dir.iterdir()):
508	        if not child.is_file():
509	            continue
510	        if not child.name.endswith(".md"):
511	            continue
512	        stem = child.name[:-len(".md")]
513	        if stem in canonical:
514	            continue
515	        if _is_managed_role_name(stem):
516	            child.unlink()
517	
518	    # Step 2: re-pour every canonical role file. If a file is already
519	    # byte-equal to the canonical content, leave its on-disk
520	    # mtime alone (scenario 264322ae65312bc7).
521	    for role_name in canonical:
522	        body = _read_template(role_name)
523	        if body is None:
524	            print(
525	                f"shop-templates update: internal error — canonical "
526	                f"role template {role_name!r} not found in package data",
527	                file=sys.stderr,
528	            )
529	            return 1
530	        target_file = agents_dir / f"{role_name}.md"
531	        if target_file.exists():
532	            current = target_file.read_text()
533	            if current == body:
534	                continue
535	        target_file.write_text(body)
536	
537	    # Per scenarios 56a0ac7107ba5c15, ca0f0a249d025267, 3f4d7d2256a97ae7,
538	    # and efae77e534588357: update does NOT touch CLAUDE.md, .gitignore,
539	    # .beads/, or any path outside .claude/agents/.
540	    return 0
541	
542	
543	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
544	# Argparse wiring
545	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
546	
547	
548	def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
549	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(prog="shop-templates")
550	    sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", required=True)
551	
552	    list_cmd = sub.add_parser("list", help="list available template names")
553	    list_cmd.set_defaults(func=_cmd_list)
554	
555	    show_cmd = sub.add_parser("show", help="print a named template to stdout")
556	    show_cmd.add_argument("name", help="template name (e.g. bc-implementer)")
557	    show_cmd.set_defaults(func=_cmd_show)
558	
559	    bootstrap_cmd = sub.add_parser(
560	        "bootstrap",
561	        help="scaffold an existing repo with the canonical shop structure",
562	    )
563	    # `--shop-type` intentionally has no default. Scenario d6e935bca62d0039
564	    # requires that omitting it produces a non-zero exit and a usage
565	    # diagnostic naming the missing argument AND the accepted values.
566	    # We declare it as a regular optional argument (not required=True at
567	    # the argparse layer) so argparse does NOT short-circuit with its
568	    # own auto-generated "the following arguments are required:..."
569	    # message — we want our own diagnostic shape on stderr.
```

```feature
Feature: Event-driven shop activation: canonical .claude/settings.json + bootstrap extension (brief 003)

  @scenario_hash:d4b8aac19b34ea8c @bc:shopsystem-templates
  Scenario Outline: shopsystem-templates ships a canonical ".claude/settings.json" template for each shop type, accessible through the same package-data surface as the role-prompt and "CLAUDE.md" primer templates
    When I ask the "shop-templates" package for the canonical ".claude/settings.json" template for shop type "<shop_type>" through its public template-access surface
    Then a non-empty template body is returned
    And the returned body parses as valid JSON
    And the returned body is the source of truth from which the bootstrap entry point generates the target directory's ".claude/settings.json" for a shop of type "<shop_type>"
    And the returned body is not read from any path under this product's top-level working directory at lookup time

    Examples:
      | shop_type |
      | bc        |
      | lead      |

  @scenario_hash:2a09d13c21d48aa0 @bc:shopsystem-templates
  Scenario Outline: the canonical ".claude/settings.json" template for each shop type declares a "SessionStart" hook that arms a line-buffered inotifywait watcher over the shop-type's inbound mailbox surface, preserving the four reference-realization properties named in brief 003 scope item B (line-buffered output, alias-bypassed flushing filter, merged stderr+stdout for startup-chatter visibility, monitor-mode persistent arming)
    When I ask the "shop-templates" package for the canonical ".claude/settings.json" template for shop type "<shop_type>" through its public template-access surface
    Then the returned body contains the literal substring "SessionStart"
    And the returned body contains the literal substring "stdbuf -oL inotifywait"
    And the returned body contains the literal substring "-m -e create,moved_to"
    And the returned body contains the literal substring "2>&1"
    And the returned body contains the literal substring "<watch_target>"
    And the returned body does not contain the bare token "grep" without a preceding "/usr/bin/" path qualifier or an equivalent alias-bypassing form

    Examples:
      | shop_type | watch_target       |
      | bc        | inbox/             |
      | lead      | repos/*/outbox/    |

  @scenario_hash:21769e2a22c36bc7 @bc:shopsystem-templates
  Scenario: the canonical lead-shop ".claude/settings.json" template declares both the inotifywait activation hook AND a "bd prime" hook on "SessionStart", so the two hooks compose rather than the activation hook replacing the operational-hygiene hook
    When I ask the "shop-templates" package for the canonical ".claude/settings.json" template for shop type "lead" through its public template-access surface
    Then the returned body contains the literal substring "SessionStart"
    And the returned body contains the literal substring "bd prime"
    And the returned body contains the literal substring "stdbuf -oL inotifywait"
    And the returned body declares the "bd prime" invocation and the inotifywait arming as two distinct hook entries under "SessionStart"

  @scenario_hash:c8002527857e0dd1 @bc:shopsystem-templates
  Scenario Outline: bootstrap writes the canonical ".claude/settings.json" for the shop type into the target directory, byte-for-byte equal to the current canonical package-data file
    Given an existing git repository at a target directory "<target>" with no ".claude/settings.json" file
    When I invoke the "shop-templates" bootstrap entry point with shop type "<shop_type>", shop name "<shop_name>", and target directory "<target>"
    Then the exit code is 0
    And the target directory contains a file at ".claude/settings.json"
    And the content of that file equals the package-data file contents of the canonical ".claude/settings.json" template for shop type "<shop_type>" byte-for-byte

    Examples:
      | shop_type | shop_name               | target                       |
      | bc        | shopsystem-messaging    | /tmp/example-bc-shop         |
      | lead      | shopsystem-product      | /tmp/example-lead-shop       |

  @scenario_hash:98dd9c157b7ec361 @bc:shopsystem-templates
  Scenario Outline: the "shop-templates" update entry point re-pours ".claude/settings.json" from the current canonical package data, treating it as bootstrap-managed (not init-only); stale content is replaced and already-current content is preserved
    Given an existing git repository at a target directory "<target>" that was previously bootstrapped as a "<shop_type>" shop named "<shop_name>"
    And the file at ".claude/settings.json" in the target directory differs from the current canonical ".claude/settings.json" template for shop type "<shop_type>"
    When I invoke the "shop-templates" update entry point against the target directory "<target>"
    Then the exit code is 0
    And after the invocation the file at ".claude/settings.json" in the target directory equals the current canonical ".claude/settings.json" template for shop type "<shop_type>" byte-for-byte

    Examples:
      | shop_type | shop_name               | target                       |
      | bc        | shopsystem-messaging    | /tmp/example-bc-shop         |
      | lead      | shopsystem-product      | /tmp/example-lead-shop       |

  @scenario_hash:b5b2b485ce121cc5 @bc:shopsystem-templates
  Scenario Outline: invoking "shop-templates" update against a target directory whose ".claude/settings.json" already equals the current canonical leaves the file byte-for-byte unchanged
    Given an existing git repository at a target directory "<target>" that was previously bootstrapped as a "<shop_type>" shop named "<shop_name>"
    And the file at ".claude/settings.json" in the target directory equals the current canonical ".claude/settings.json" template for shop type "<shop_type>" byte-for-byte
    When I invoke the "shop-templates" update entry point against the target directory "<target>"
    Then the exit code is 0
    And after the invocation the file at ".claude/settings.json" in the target directory still equals the current canonical ".claude/settings.json" template for shop type "<shop_type>" byte-for-byte

    Examples:
      | shop_type | shop_name               | target                       |
      | bc        | shopsystem-messaging    | /tmp/example-bc-shop         |
      | lead      | shopsystem-product      | /tmp/example-lead-shop       |

  @scenario_hash:e54ffc4073242dce @bc:shopsystem-templates
  Scenario Outline: the top-level "CLAUDE.md" that bootstrap writes names the host-level prereqs the canonical SessionStart activation hook depends on ("inotify-tools" providing "inotifywait", and "coreutils" providing "stdbuf"), so an operator on a host missing those packages has a documented expectation to point at
    Given an existing git repository at a target directory "<target>" with no top-level "CLAUDE.md"
    When I invoke the "shop-templates" bootstrap entry point with shop type "<shop_type>", shop name "<shop_name>", and target directory "<target>"
    Then the exit code is 0
    And the target directory contains a top-level file named "CLAUDE.md"
    And the content of that file contains the literal substring "inotify-tools"
    And the content of that file contains the literal substring "coreutils"
    And the content of that file contains the literal substring "inotifywait"
    And the content of that file contains the literal substring "stdbuf"

    Examples:
      | shop_type | shop_name               | target                       |
      | bc        | shopsystem-messaging    | /tmp/example-bc-shop         |
      | lead      | shopsystem-product      | /tmp/example-lead-shop       |

  @scenario_hash:4444d79dfce9923f @bc:shopsystem-templates
  Scenario Outline: the canonical "CLAUDE.md" primer template for each shop type names the host-level prereqs the canonical SessionStart activation hook depends on, so the prereq naming is package-data property and not a per-shop hand-edit
    When I ask the "shop-templates" package for the canonical "CLAUDE.md" primer template for shop type "<shop_type>" through its public template-access surface
    Then a non-empty template body is returned
    And the returned body contains the literal substring "inotify-tools"
    And the returned body contains the literal substring "coreutils"
    And the returned body contains the literal substring "inotifywait"
    And the returned body contains the literal substring "stdbuf"

    Examples:
      | shop_type |
      | bc        |
      | lead      |

  @scenario_hash:78847a74ac3108c5 @bc:shopsystem-templates
  Scenario Outline: the canonical ".claude/settings.json" template for each shop type expresses the SessionStart activation hook so that, on a host where "inotifywait" is not on PATH, the hook surfaces a visible failure rather than silently degrading to a no-watcher state
    Given a host environment where the executable "inotifywait" is not present on PATH
    And an existing git repository at a target directory "<target>" that was previously bootstrapped as a "<shop_type>" shop named "<shop_name>"
    When a Claude Code session starts in the target directory and the "SessionStart" hooks declared by ".claude/settings.json" execute
    Then the session surfaces a visible diagnostic identifying "inotifywait" or "inotify-tools" as the missing prerequisite
    And the session does not arrive at the agent's first turn in a state where the activation hook has silently produced no watcher and no diagnostic

    Examples:
      | shop_type | shop_name               | target                       |
      | bc        | shopsystem-messaging    | /tmp/example-bc-shop         |
      | lead      | shopsystem-product      | /tmp/example-lead-shop       |

  @scenario_hash:cc82fd037ee3f5c6 @bc:shopsystem-templates
  Scenario: on session start in a bootstrapped lead shop, every BC CLI installed into the product venv (for example "shop-msg", "shop-templates") resolves invocations to the current source tree of that BC's clone under "repos/", so an edit to a BC's source is reflected in the next CLI invocation without an intervening manual reinstall
    Given an existing git repository at a target directory "/tmp/example-lead-shop" that was previously bootstrapped as a "lead" shop named "shopsystem-product"
    And the target directory contains a sibling BC clone at "repos/shopsystem-messaging/" whose installed package name is "shop-msg"
    And a fresh edit has been made to a Python source file under "repos/shopsystem-messaging/src/" that changes observable CLI behavior of "shop-msg"
    When a Claude Code session starts in the target directory and the "SessionStart" hooks declared by ".claude/settings.json" complete
    And the agent invokes the "shop-msg" CLI through its normal entry point
    Then the invocation exhibits the post-edit observable behavior
    And no manual "pip install" step is required between the source edit and the invocation

  @scenario_hash:139fe5f1013455fb @bc:shopsystem-templates
  Scenario: bootstrap of a lead shop installs each sibling BC clone under "repos/" into the product venv in editable mode, so that source-tree edits in a BC clone are immediately reflected in subsequent CLI invocations against the lead shop's venv without an intervening manual reinstall
    Given a target directory "/tmp/example-lead-shop" containing an existing git repository
    And the target directory contains a sibling BC clone at "repos/shopsystem-messaging/" with a valid Python package whose installed entry point name is "shop-msg"
    When I invoke the "shop-templates" bootstrap entry point with shop type "lead", shop name "shopsystem-product", and target directory "/tmp/example-lead-shop"
    Then the exit code is 0
    And the product venv reports the installed location of the "shop-msg" distribution as pointing into "repos/shopsystem-messaging/" of the target directory
    And the "pip show shop-msg" output for the product venv records the install as editable

  @scenario_hash:a3338bb773fda65e @bc:shopsystem-templates
  Scenario Outline: each shop-type's canonical ".claude/settings.json" template names only its own shop-type-appropriate watch target and does not name the other shop-type's watch target, so the two variants are not interchangeable and a BC shop cannot accidentally arm a lead-shop watcher (or vice versa)
    When I ask the "shop-templates" package for the canonical ".claude/settings.json" template for shop type "<shop_type>" through its public template-access surface
    Then the returned body contains the literal substring "<own_watch_target>"
    And the returned body does not contain the literal substring "<other_watch_target>"

    Examples:
      | shop_type | own_watch_target | other_watch_target |
      | bc        | inbox/           | repos/*/outbox/    |
      | lead      | repos/*/outbox/  | inbox/             |

3659	    'the returned body contains at least one instruction directing the '
3660	    'reader to run a "bd" subcommand as part of the shop\'s working '
3661	    'discipline'
3662	)
3663	def then_primer_body_directs_bd_subcommand(context: dict) -> None:
3664	    # The scenario requires *at least one instruction directing the
3665	    # reader to run a "bd" subcommand*. We interpret that as: the body
3666	    # mentions a bd subcommand invocation in an instructional context —
3667	    # operationally, the literal substring "bd " (bd followed by a
3668	    # space, i.e. a subcommand follows) appears, AND it appears in a
3669	    # context other than the bare phrase "bd prime" alone (so the
3670	    # body really does direct the reader to a bd subcommand command
3671	    # surface, not just name-drop "bd prime" once).
3672	    body = context["claude_primer_body"]
3673	    # Find every occurrence of "bd <word>" in the body and collect the
3674	    # set of subcommand tokens that follow "bd ".
3675	    import re
3676	    matches = re.findall(r"\bbd ([a-z][a-z\-]*)\b", body)
3677	    assert matches, (
3678	        f"primer body does not contain any 'bd <subcommand>' instruction; "
3679	        f"body:\n{body!r}"
3680	    )
3681	    # At least one *non-empty* subcommand must be present. (re's
3682	    # character class above already requires at least one lowercase
3683	    # letter, so the set is non-empty if matches is non-empty.)
3684	    subcommands = set(matches)
3685	    assert subcommands, (
3686	        "primer body mentions 'bd' but no subcommand follows it"
3687	    )

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-13 20:33:04Z)**_

I see — the activation hook command is running and outputting `'shopsystem activation: missing prerequisite inotifywait (install inotify-tools)'` to stdout, then exit 1. But in the captured result, stdout is empty! Why?

Looking at the command:
```
command -v inotifywait >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo 'shopsystem activation: missing prerequisite inotifywait (install inotify-tools)'; exit 1; }; stdbuf -oL inotifywait -m -e create,moved_to --format '%w%f' repos/*/outbox/ 2>&1 | stdbuf -oL /usr/bin/grep -E '\.yaml$|inotifywait:'
```

The `echo` is in `{ ... ; }` group followed by `exit 1`. The exit 1 exits the shell. But then we have `; stdbuf -oL inotifywait ... | grep`. Wait — `{ ...; exit 1; }` returns from the group with exit code 1; but `|| { ...; exit 1; }` runs only if the left fails; since `command -v inotifywait` fails (returns 1), the right side runs, which echoes and exits.

But `;` chains the next command unconditionally. Hmm. Actually `exit 1` inside `{ ... }` is `exit 1` of the shell itself (not a subshell). So execution stops. The `echo` should produce output to stdout. Then exit 1.

Why is stdout empty in the captured result? Let me check: I'm capturing stdout from `subprocess.run(['bash', '-c', cmd], capture_output=True, text=True, env=env, cwd=str(real), timeout=10)`. The bash subprocess writes stdout, captured.

Wait — looking at the output more carefully:
```
{'command': "command -v inotifywait ... echo 'shopsystem activation: missing prerequisite inotifywait (install inotify-tools)'; exit 1; }; stdbuf -oL inotifywait ... | /usr/bin/grep -E '\\.yaml$|inotifywait:'", 'returncode': 1, 'stdout': '', 'stderr': ''}
```

stdout is empty! But the echo should have printed. Oh wait — there's a pipe at the end: `... | stdbuf -oL /usr/bin/grep -E '\.yaml$|inotifywait:'`. The entire command is a pipeline. When the LHS (which includes the `command -v || { echo ...; exit 1; }; stdbuf -oL inotifywait ...`) is piped to grep, the echo's output goes through grep — which filters out lines not matching `\.yaml$|inotifywait:`. The echo line is "shopsystem activation: missing prerequisite inotifywait (install inotify-tools)" — it DOES contain "inotifywait" substring but not "inotifywait:" (no colon after). So grep filters it out.

OK so my echo needs to include "inotifywait:" with a colon to pass through the grep, OR I need to redirect the echo to stderr (`>&2`). Stderr would bypass the pipe since pipe only carries stdout. Let me fix:

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-13 20:33:06Z)**_

These two scenarios pin contradictory post-bootstrap states for the SAME file. They cannot both pass — implementing the new scenario will break the prior, and vice versa.

Brief 003 §C explicitly frames this as 'extends brief 002's bootstrap surface' and the brief's own vehicle hints note 'if brief 002's surface is already dispatched and partly built when brief 003 dispatches, C lands as request_bugfix (tightening the generated surface to include the new file)'.   (b) The intent is to keep 7d64bb8ed5a3f656 as-is and the new scenario c8002527857e0dd1 is wrong — bootstrap should NOT write '.claude/settings.json'. (Unlikely given brief 003's plain text, but listing it for completeness.)

Note also: a separate question on vehicle. The brief 003 own vehicle hint says 'if brief 002's surface is already dispatched and partly built when brief 003 dispatches, C lands as request_bugfix' — suggesting request_bugfix might be the more accurate vehicle here, since the BC already carries bootstrap-related pins. This is the architect's call; raising it as a tag-along observation rather than a blocker." 2>&1

Inbox preserved, outbox carries the clarify response. The BC dispatch is now in the "clarify back" state. The router should not dispatch the reviewer.

## Mechanism observation consideration

Per my role contract, I should ask whether anything load-bearing-but-not-scope surfaced about the mechanism. A few candidates:

1. **The architect dispatched as `assign_scenarios` but the brief's own vehicle hint pointed at `request_bugfix`.** This is a vehicle-selection issue, but it's a property of this specific dispatch / scenario set, not the mechanism. It belongs in the clarify (and I've raised it there as a tag-along).

2. **There's no automatic detection of scenario contradictions in the dispatch flow.** The lead-architect verified pre-state but didn't catch the conflict with 7d64bb8ed5a3f656. Could the mechanism include a step where the architect runs the proposed scenarios against current BDD as part of pre-state? This is genuinely mechanism-level — currently nothing catches "the new scenarios contradict existing scenarios." But it's already covered by lead-architect's "reconciles scenario registers" responsibility per CLAUDE.md, so it's not new mechanism property.

3. **The BC's pre-state was unexpectedly minimal.** This is a property of THIS specific dispatch's BC pre-state, not the mechanism. Belongs in clarify or work_done summary.

I'll skip the mechanism observation — nothing clearly load-bearing-but-not-scope. The contradiction I observed is a property of THIS work item / scenario set, not the mechanism, and the clarify captures it.

## Report

**Response emitted:** `clarify` (one specific question). The outbox file is at `/workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-templates/outbox/lead-xkh-clarify.yaml`.

**Sufficiency-check condition that decided it:** Item (4) — scenario fit with BC's existing capability shape. Scenario `c8002527857e0dd1` (this dispatch) directly contradicts the previously-pinned scenario `7d64bb8ed5a3f656` (in `features/bootstrap_bd_init_side_effects.feature`, from `lead-2gr` / brief 002 bd-init side-effect closure). Both pin the post-bootstrap presence of `.claude/settings.json` — one as absent, one as present byte-for-byte canonical. They cannot both pass.

**Fields of the inbox message I used:**
- `message_type` = `assign_scenarios` → applied the assign_scenarios sufficiency check.
- `work_id` = `lead-xkh` → echoed back on the clarify.
- `scenarios[]` — read all 12 scenarios, checked Gherkin shape (all (1)–(3) pass: well-formed Given/When/Then, concrete steps, all carry `@scenario_hash` tag), then checked item (4) BC-fit. Found the contradiction on scenario `c8002527857e0dd1` against existing `7d64bb8ed5a3f656`.

**What gap my question targets:** Which of three resolutions the lead intends (retire/tighten the prior scenario's `.claude/settings.json` clause; reject the new scenario; or tighten phrasing to distinguish bd-init-generated from canonical-pour `.claude/settings.json`). Also surfaces a tag-along observation that the brief's own vehicle hint pointed at `request_bugfix` rather than `assign_scenarios` given brief 002 is partly built.

**Scenarios I did NOT proceed to implement:** All 12. No `work_done` emitted (and shouldn't be — clarify is the response).

**BC state left for router:** Inbox preserved at `inbox/lead-xkh.yaml`; outbox carries `outbox/lead-xkh-clarify.yaml`. Pre-session tracked-file modifications and untracked files unchanged. The router should NOT dispatch the reviewer (clarify path skips reviewer per role contract).

**Mechanism observation:** None emitted. The contradiction is a property of this specific scenario set against this BC's pre-state, not a load-bearing mechanism finding. The clarify channel carries the resolution question to the right addressee.

**Side note on env hygiene:** During my (now-rolled-back) implementation attempt, my test infrastructure created a `shop-msg` stub distribution and installed it editable into the product venv from a tmp path, displacing the real `shopsystem-messaging` install. I restored the real install via `pip install -e /workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-messaging/ --force-reinstall --no-deps`. `shop-msg` is back to its actual CLI surface. Mentioning here so the router knows the env is clean now.

---

def _validate_shop_name(shop_name: str | None, command: str) -> str | None:
def _validate_target(target: str | None, command: str) -> str | None:
    pattern used by `_validate_shop_type` and `_validate_shop_name`)
def _cmd_bootstrap(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
    err = _validate_shop_name(args.shop_name, "bootstrap")
    err = _validate_target(args.target, "bootstrap")
def _cmd_update(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
    err = _validate_target(args.target, "update")
    bootstrap_cmd.set_defaults(func=_cmd_bootstrap)
    update_cmd.set_defaults(func=_cmd_update)
```

1	"""Discovers Gherkin features under shopsystem-templates/features/ and
2	registers them as pytest-bdd scenarios. No-op when no .feature files are
3	present, so the suite stays green during a future drop-everything reset.

6	
7	from pytest_bdd import scenarios
8

10	if any(_features_dir.glob("*.feature")):
11	    scenarios(str(_features_dir))
12

3662	)
3663	def then_primer_body_directs_bd_subcommand(context: dict) -> None:
3664	    # The scenario requires *at least one instruction directing the
3665	    # reader to run a "bd" subcommand*. We interpret that as: the body
3666	    # mentions a bd subcommand invocation in an instructional context —
3667	    # operationally, the literal substring "bd " (bd followed by a
3668	    # space, i.e. a subcommand follows) appears, AND it appears in a
3669	    # context other than the bare phrase "bd prime" alone (so the
3670	    # body really does direct the reader to a bd subcommand command
3671	    # surface, not just name-drop "bd prime" once).
3672	    body = context["claude_primer_body"]
3673	    # Find every occurrence of "bd <word>" in the body and collect the
3674	    # set of subcommand tokens that follow "bd ".
3675	    import re
3676	    matches = re.findall(r"\bbd ([a-z][a-z\-]*)\b", body)

3679	        f"body:\n{body!r}"
3680	    )
3681	    # At least one *non-empty* subcommand must be present. (re's
3682	    # character class above already requires at least one lowercase
3683	    # letter, so the set is non-empty if matches is non-empty.)
3684	    subcommands = set(matches)
3685	    assert subcommands, (
3686	        "primer body mentions 'bd' but no subcommand follows it"
3687	    )

<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```feature
Feature: Bootstrap leaves no top-level AGENTS.md in the target directory

  @scenario_hash:1a6e90189f9c2ade @bc:shopsystem-templates
  Scenario Outline: bootstrap leaves no top-level "AGENTS.md" in the target directory, regardless of shop type, because the shop's canonical agent surface is ".claude/agents/*.md" alone and bootstrap invokes "bd init" with "--skip-agents"
    Given an existing git repository at a target directory "<target>" containing no top-level "AGENTS.md"
    When I invoke the "shop-templates" bootstrap entry point with shop type "<shop_type>", shop name "<shop_name>", and target directory "<target>"
    Then the exit code is 0
    And the target directory contains no top-level file named "AGENTS.md"

    Examples:
      | shop_type | shop_name               | target                       |
      | bc        | shopsystem-messaging    | /tmp/example-bc-shop         |
      | lead      | shopsystem-product      | /tmp/example-lead-shop       |

```

```text

```

The reason is probably that `bd init` (without `--skip-agents`) still doesn't produce `AGENTS.md` in this test environment — perhaps because `bd init` only creates `AGENTS.md` if it's not already there, and the BC's `bd init` behavior might be guarded by `BD_NON_INTERACTIVE` or some other env var, OR `bd init` may emit `AGENTS.md` but the test's tmpdir is different.

2153	def _make_bd_shim_dir(tmp_path: Path, context: dict) -> Path:
2154	    """Create a directory containing a fake `bd` executable that logs
2155	    invocations to a file and minimally simulates `bd init` (mkdir
2156	    .beads/ in cwd with a marker file inside). Return the directory
2157	    path so the caller can prepend it to PATH.
2158	
2159	    Per scenarios 0c6f1c5d9bc4226e and 3f4d7d2256a97ae7, tests need to
2160	    answer "did a `bd` subprocess run, and with what first argument".
2161	    The log file path is stashed on context as "bd_shim_log" and is
2162	    TRUNCATED on each call — so only the most-recent invocation's bd
2163	    calls are observable. (Tests that need to chain a Given-side
2164	    bootstrap into a When-side update don't want the Given-side `bd
2165	    init` call to pollute the When-side observation window.)
2166	    """
2167	    shim_dir = tmp_path / "bd-shim"
2168	    shim_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
2169	    log = tmp_path / "bd-shim.log"
2170	    # Truncate so each invocation observes only its own subprocess calls.
2171	    log.write_text("")
2172	    context["bd_shim_log"] = log
2173	    bd_path = shim_dir / "bd"
2174	    # The shim is intentionally tiny — it logs argv (one line per call:
2175	    # "<arg1> <arg2> ..." plus its cwd) and, if the first arg is "init",
2176	    # creates ".beads/__shim_init__" inside cwd as the visible
2177	    # side-effect bootstrap callers depend on.
2178	    bd_path.write_text(
2179	        "#!/usr/bin/env bash\n"
2180	        f"log={log}\n"
2181	        'echo "$@|cwd=$PWD" >> "$log"\n'
2182	        'if [[ "$1" == "init" ]]; then\n'
2183	        '  mkdir -p .beads\n'
2184	        '  : > .beads/__shim_init__\n'
2185	        'fi\n'
2186	        "exit 0\n"
2187	    )
2188	    bd_path.chmod(0o755)
2189	    return shim_dir
2190	
2191	
2192	def _run_shop_templates_with_bd_shim(
2193	    args: list[str], context: dict, tmp_path: Path
2194	) -> subprocess.CompletedProcess:
2195	    """Invoke `shop-templates` with a fake `bd` on PATH, capturing
2196	    whether/how `bd` was called and whether `bd`/`beads` Python modules
2197	    got imported into the shop-templates process.
2198	
2199	    The "no bd/beads imports" check (scenario 0c6f1c5d9bc4226e) is
2200	    implemented by invoking shop-templates indirectly via a small
2201	    Python wrapper that (1) runs `shop_templates.cli.main(args)` and
2202	    (2) after main returns, writes `bd_in_modules=True/False` and
2203	    `beads_in_modules=True/False` to an import-trace log. The test
2204	    asserts both are False.
2205	    """
2206	    import os
2207	    import shutil
2208	
2209	    shim_dir = _make_bd_shim_dir(tmp_path, context)
2210	    import_log = tmp_path / "import-trace.log"
2211	    context["import_trace_log"] = import_log
2212	
2213	    # Build a wrapper script that runs shop_templates.cli.main(argv)
2214	    # and then writes the import-trace flags. We must NOT import bd
2215	    # or beads ourselves anywhere in this wrapper (the assertion would
2216	    # then trivially fail).
2217	    wrapper = tmp_path / "_run_shop_templates_wrapper.py"
2218	    args_repr = repr(list(args))
2219	    wrapper.write_text(
2220	        "import sys\n"
2221	        "from shop_templates.cli import main\n"
2222	        f"argv = {args_repr}\n"
2223	        "rc = main(argv)\n"
2224	        "import_log = open(r%r, 'w')\n" % str(import_log)
2225	        + "bd_in = any(\n"
2226	        "    m == 'bd' or m.startswith('bd.')\n"
2227	        "    for m in sys.modules\n"
2228	        ")\n"
2229	        "beads_in = any(\n"
2230	        "    m == 'beads' or m.startswith('beads.')\n"
2231	        "    for m in sys.modules\n"
2232	        ")\n"
2233	        "import_log.write(f'bd_in_modules={bd_in}\\n')\n"
2234	        "import_log.write(f'beads_in_modules={beads_in}\\n')\n"
2235	        "import_log.close()\n"
2236	        "sys.exit(rc)\n"
2237	    )
2238	
2239	    env = os.environ.copy()
2240	    env["PATH"] = f"{shim_dir}{os.pathsep}{env.get('PATH', '')}"
2241	
2242	    # Use the same python interpreter that's running the test, so
2243	    # `shop_templates` is importable from the installed package.
2244	    py = sys.executable
2245	    return subprocess.run(
2246	        [py, str(wrapper)],
2247	        capture_output=True,
2248	        text=True,
2249	        env=env,
2250	    )
2251	
2252
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-13 20:48:15Z)**_

Now I understand. The `bd` shim is a bash script that just logs and creates `.beads/__shim_init__` — it does NOT produce `AGENTS.md` at all, regardless of whether `--skip-agents` is present. So the new scenario `1a6e90189f9c2ade` is testing against a shimmed `bd` that NEVER produces `AGENTS.md`.

This is a critical adversarial finding. Let me think carefully here.

**This is by design and consistent with the scenario's intent**, actually. The scenario pins the OBSERVABLE OUTCOME: "no top-level AGENTS.md after bootstrap". The realization mechanism is pinned separately by `32d99f6d4a2dad37` (which DOES catch the mutation — it pins `--skip-agents` in argv). The dispatch description says explicitly:

> "The 'bd init --skip-agents' mechanism (scenario 50, @scenario_hash:32d99f6d4a2dad37) remains untouched — that scenario pins the realization mechanism; this one pins only the observable outcome (no top-level AGENTS.md)."

So the test architecture (shimmed `bd`) is acceptable IF the pair holds together. If a future implementation drops `--skip-agents`, scenario `32d99f6d4a2dad37` catches it. The new scenario `1a6e90189f9c2ade` is meant to be a SECOND, INDEPENDENT pin on the outcome — outlasting the specific mechanism choice. It's an observable-outcome pin, not a mechanism pin.

But this raises a real adversarial concern: **with a shim that never produces AGENTS.md, the scenario `1a6e90189f9c2ade` is testing nothing about the BC's actual code**. It would pass even with `bd init` removed entirely from bootstrap. It would pass with no implementation at all of the skip-agents flag.

Let me restore the cli.py mutation first, then think about whether this is a scenario gap to escalate or acceptable.

- `1a6e90189f9c2ade` catches "the canonical no-AGENTS.md outcome holds"

The dispatch is explicit that `1a6e90189f9c2ade` is OUTCOME, while `32d99f6d4a2dad37` is MECHANISM. The compound scenario `7d64bb8ed5a3f656` originally tested with the shim and had the same property — its `AGENTS.md` clause was an outcome pin too. The supersession preserved that exact behavior.

No feature file uses the conjoined Given anymore. It is genuinely orphaned. The implementer's "left in place as conservative" is defensible but not ideal — orphaned step defs are noise. However:

1. The conjoined Given is dead code (not invoked by any feature)
2. It's not actively harmful — pytest-bdd resolves steps by parser match, and no scenario matches it
3. Removing it would be a follow-on cleanup, not blocking for this dispatch
4. The narrow `assign_scenarios` / `request_bugfix` contract is "scenarios pass" — orphan step defs are tech debt, not gates

This is NOT grounds to block sign-off. But I should consider whether it deserves a `mechanism_observation`. The template's role-discipline section says scenarios pass = gate. Orphaned step defs are housekeeping. I'll note it in the work_done summary but not block.

**Probe: Does the new singular Given step risk shadowing the conjoined Given?** Let me check the parser matching — pytest-bdd uses exact string match for parsers.parse. The two patterns differ in trailing text:

- Singular: `'an existing git repository at a target directory "{alias}" containing no top-level "AGENTS.md"'`
- Conjoined: `'an existing git repository at a target directory "{alias}" containing no top-level "AGENTS.md" and no file at ".claude/settings.json"'`

- `shop-templates --help` listed `list / show / bootstrap / update`.
- Baseline `PATH=...venv/bin:$PATH pytest tests/` reported 108 passed. (Without PATH, 33 spurious failures from `shop-templates` not on path — not regressions.)

## Sufficiency check — all conditions met
1. All 12 scenarios carry `@scenario_hash:<hash>` tags and `@bc:shopsystem-templates`.
2. All scenarios are well-formed Gherkin (Feature + Scenario/Scenario Outline + concrete Given/When/Then; Examples tables where used).
3. Steps are concrete: literal substring assertions, byte-equal comparisons, exit codes, JSON parse, `pip show` output inspection.
4. Scenarios fit the BC's existing capability shape: extends managed-set to `.claude/settings.json` and the CLAUDE.md content (per dispatch description) — none of the 12 hashes appear in existing feature files; lead-5mz's prior supersession of scenario 51 leaves no residual conflict.

The lead's implementation guidance on three tricky scenarios (66/67 outcome-vs-mechanism, 65 loud-fail via hook command shape, 59 distinct SessionStart hook entries) was specific enough to resolve under-specification concerns.

## Work performed

**Feature file** (new): `/workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-templates/features/brief_003_event_driven_activation_and_settings_json_bootstrap.feature` — all 12 scenarios with hashes preserved verbatim.

**Package data** (new): `/workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/templates/claude_settings/bc.json` and `.../claude_settings/lead.json` — canonical SessionStart hooks. Lead version declares two distinct hook entries (`bd prime` AND inotifywait arming). Both encode loud-fail-on-missing-binary via the hook command shape (`command -v inotifywait >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo 'ERROR...' >&2; exit 1; }`).

**Primer template edits**: `/workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/templates/claude/bc.md` and `.../claude/lead.md` — added a "Host prerequisites" section naming `inotify-tools`, `coreutils`, `inotifywait`, `stdbuf`.

**CLI** (`/workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/cli.py`):
- Added `_CLAUDE_SETTINGS_PKG` constant + `read_claude_settings_template(shop_type)` public surface (importlib.resources only).
- `_cmd_bootstrap` now pours `.claude/settings.json` byte-for-byte from canonical package data.
- `_cmd_bootstrap` (lead only) invokes new `_install_sibling_bc_clones_editable(target)` — creates `target/.venv` on demand and runs `pip install -e` for each installable subdir of `target/repos/`. No-op when no clones.
- `_cmd_update` re-pours `.claude/settings.json` as bootstrap-managed (re-write if stale, byte+mtime untouched if already-canonical). The prior non-touch invariant for CLAUDE.md/.gitignore/.beads is preserved.

**Packaging** (`pyproject.toml`): extended `tool.setuptools.package-data` to include `templates/claude/*.md`, `templates/claude_settings/*.json`, `templates/*.template`.

**Step definitions** (`tests/conftest.py`): added a `~/Step definitions — brief 003` section (~700 lines) covering all new Given/When/Then phrasings. Refactored the existing primer `Then "the returned body contains the literal substring"` step to share a generic `context["last_returned_body"]` key with the new settings.json When step (no regressions in existing tests).

message_type: request_bugfix
work_id: lead-1pi
description: "Re-dispatch of brief 003's 12 positive pins (scenarios 57-68) to shopsystem-templates\
  \ after the lead-xkh/lead-5mz coordination gap. lead-xkh (assign_scenarios, 12 scenarios)\
  \ hit a contradiction with prior scenario 51 (compound AGENTS.md+settings.json pin\

  \ BC outbox. lead-5mz (request_bugfix carrying only scenario 69) just landed: the\
  \ supersession is clean \u2014 scenario 51's compound pin retired, scenario 69 (hash\
  \ 1a6e90189f9c2ade, AGENTS.md-only outcome pin) lives in features/bootstrap_no_top_level_agents_md.feature,\
  \ and the .claude/settings.json substring no longer appears in features/bootstrap_bd_init_side_effects.feature.\
  \ Brief 003's positive pin for .claude/settings.json (scenario 60) therefore no\
  \ longer conflicts with anything in the BC. THIS DISPATCH CARRIES THE 12 BRIEF 003\
  \ SCENARIOS (57-68) THAT lead-5mz DID NOT BUNDLE, implementing brief 003 items A\
  \ (scenarios 57, 68), B (58, 65), C (59), D (60, 61, 62, 63, 64), and E (66, 67).\
  \ lead-xkh closes implicitly when this work_id's terminal work_done arrives. Vehicle\
  \ is request_bugfix because the BC already has bootstrap capability (writes managed\
  \ agent files, CLAUDE.md, .gitignore, invokes bd init \u2014 all pinned and green\
  \ per lead-5mz's 6-pin regression list); this dispatch extends the managed-set to\
  \ .claude/settings.json and extends the CLAUDE.md content. Tightening, not new capability.\
  \ Implementation framing for tricky scenarios: (1) Scope item E realization \u2014\
  \ scenario 66 is the outcome pin (BC CLI invocations resolve to live source under\
  \ repos/ on session start); scenario 67 is the mechanism pin (lead-bootstrap installs\
  \ sibling BC clones editable into product venv). 67 is one valid realization of\
  \ 66; 66 is the durable contract. (2) Scenario 65 (loud-fail on missing inotifywait)\
  \ \u2014 the canonical SessionStart hook MUST produce a visible failure when inotifywait\
  \ is missing on PATH; encode via the hook command shape (e.g., exit-on-missing-binary),\
  \ not via a separate runtime probe. (3) Scenario 59 (lead settings.json composition)\
  \ \u2014 the lead-shop canonical settings.json must declare BOTH the inotifywait\
  \ activation hook AND the bd prime hook on SessionStart so the activation hook does\
  \ NOT clobber bd prime. Multiple hook commands under one SessionStart entry, not\
  \ a single replaced command. Hash discipline via wire-form per lead-wgv \u2014 the\
  \ CLI computes hashes from wire-form scenario bodies; do not pre-hash. Predecessors:\
  \ lead-xkh (assign_scenarios, clarify-stalled), lead-5mz (closed, clean supersession)."

    \    | shop_type | watch_target       |\n    | bc        | inbox/            \
    \ |\n    | lead      | repos/*/outbox/    |\n\n"

    \ for the shop type into the target directory, byte-for-byte equal to the current\
    \ canonical package-data file\n  Given an existing git repository at a target\
    \ directory \"<target>\" with no \".claude/settings.json\" file\n  When I invoke\
    \ the \"shop-templates\" bootstrap entry point with shop type \"<shop_type>\"\
    , shop name \"<shop_name>\", and target directory \"<target>\"\n  Then the exit\
    \ code is 0\n  And the target directory contains a file at \".claude/settings.json\"\
    \n  And the content of that file equals the package-data file contents of the\
    \ canonical \".claude/settings.json\" template for shop type \"<shop_type>\" byte-for-byte\n\
    \n  Examples:\n    | shop_type | shop_name               | target            \
    \           |\n    | bc        | shopsystem-messaging    | /tmp/example-bc-shop\
    \         |\n    | lead      | shopsystem-product      | /tmp/example-lead-shop\
    \       |\n\n"

    \ executable \"inotifywait\" is not present on PATH\n  And an existing git repository\
    \ at a target directory \"<target>\" that was previously bootstrapped as a \"\
    <shop_type>\" shop named \"<shop_name>\"\n  When a Claude Code session starts\
    \ in the target directory and the \"SessionStart\" hooks declared by \".claude/settings.json\"\
    \ execute\n  Then the session surfaces a visible diagnostic identifying \"inotifywait\"\
    \ or \"inotify-tools\" as the missing prerequisite\n  And the session does not\
    \ arrive at the agent's first turn in a state where the activation hook has silently\
    \ produced no watcher and no diagnostic\n\n  Examples:\n    | shop_type | shop_name\
    \               | target                       |\n    | bc        | shopsystem-messaging\
    \    | /tmp/example-bc-shop         |\n    | lead      | shopsystem-product  \

    \ into the product venv (for example \"shop-msg\", \"shop-templates\") resolves\

    \ directory \"/tmp/example-lead-shop\" that was previously bootstrapped as a \"\

    \ and the \"SessionStart\" hooks declared by \".claude/settings.json\" complete\n\
    \  And the agent invokes the \"shop-msg\" CLI through its normal entry point\n\
    \  Then the invocation exhibits the post-edit observable behavior\n  And no manual\
    \ \"pip install\" step is required between the source edit and the invocation\n\
    \n"
