_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-13 20:53:36Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./src/shop_templates/cli.py`

```text
1	"""shop-templates CLI entry point.
2	
3	Subcommands:
4	    list
5	        Print available role-template names (one per line) to stdout.
6	    show <name>
7	        Print the named template's content to stdout. Exits non-zero
8	        with a stderr message when no template matches that name.
9	    bootstrap --shop-type {bc,lead} --shop-name NAME --target DIR
10	        Scaffold a new (existing-but-empty) shop repository at DIR:
11	        write the canonical role-prompt copies for the shop type under
12	        .claude/agents/, write a CLAUDE.md primer for the shop type,
13	        write a top-level .gitignore, and invoke `bd init` as a
14	        subprocess to initialize .beads/.
15	    update --target DIR --shop-type {bc,lead}
16	        Re-pour the bootstrap-managed agent files in DIR/.claude/agents/
17	        from the current canonical package data. Reconcile against the
18	        canonical role set for the shop type — add canonical files that
19	        are missing, remove managed files whose canonical template no
20	        longer exists. Does NOT touch CLAUDE.md, .gitignore, or .beads/.
21	
22	Role-prompt templates live in the package as `templates/<name>.md` data
23	files. CLAUDE.md primer templates live as `templates/claude/<shop_type>.md`.
24	The .gitignore template lives as `templates/gitignore.template`. The CLI
25	is the public boundary; dispatchers, scaffold scripts, and any other
26	consumer reads templates via this surface rather than by path.
27	"""
28	from __future__ import annotations
29	
30	import argparse
31	import subprocess
32	import sys
33	from importlib.resources import files
34	from pathlib import Path
35	
36	
37	_TEMPLATES_PKG = "shop_templates.templates"
38	_CLAUDE_TEMPLATES_PKG = "shop_templates.templates.claude"
39	
40	# The canonical role-set assignment per shop type. The bootstrap surface
41	# uses this to decide which role files to pour into .claude/agents/ for
42	# a freshly-bootstrapped shop, and the update surface uses it to
43	# reconcile an existing shop's .claude/agents/ against the current
44	# canonical set.
45	_CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS: dict[str, tuple[str, ...]] = {
46	    "bc": ("bc-implementer", "bc-reviewer"),
47	    "lead": ("lead-po", "lead-architect"),
48	}
49	
50	_SHOP_TYPES = tuple(_CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS.keys())
51	
52	
53	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
54	# Public template-access surface (importable; not just CLI-internal).
55	#
56	# Per scenario 0cce58eb573d3c91, the CLAUDE.md primer template must be
57	# accessible "through its public template-access surface" and must NOT
58	# be read from any path under the product's top-level working directory
59	# at lookup time — i.e., it must be served from importlib.resources
60	# package data, the same boundary the role-prompt templates use.
61	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
62	
63	
64	def _list_template_names() -> list[str]:
65	    """Return the sorted list of available role-template names.
66	
67	    Walks only the top-level package directory; sub-directories (e.g.
68	    `templates/claude/`) are skipped. This keeps `shop-templates list`
69	    output stable as new categories of package data (CLAUDE primers,
70	    gitignore template) are added.
71	    """
72	    pkg_root = files(_TEMPLATES_PKG)
73	    names = []
74	    for resource in pkg_root.iterdir():
75	        # `is_file()` plus `.md` suffix isolates role-prompt templates
76	        # from sub-directories like `templates/claude/`.
77	        if not resource.is_file():
78	            continue
79	        name = resource.name
80	        if name.endswith(".md"):
81	            names.append(name[: -len(".md")])
82	    return sorted(names)
83	
84	
85	def _read_template(name: str) -> str | None:
86	    """Return the named role-template's content, or None if no such template."""
87	    try:
88	        return (files(_TEMPLATES_PKG) / f"{name}.md").read_text()
89	    except FileNotFoundError:
90	        return None
91	
92	
93	def read_claude_md_primer(shop_type: str) -> str:
94	    """Return the canonical CLAUDE.md primer template body for a shop type.
95	
96	    The body is loaded from package data via importlib.resources; it is
97	    never read from a filesystem path under the product working
98	    directory. The returned string is the source of truth from which the
99	    bootstrap entry point generates a target directory's top-level
100	    CLAUDE.md.
101	    """
102	    if shop_type not in _CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS:
103	        raise ValueError(
104	            f"unknown shop type {shop_type!r}; accepted values: "
105	            f"{', '.join(_SHOP_TYPES)}"
106	        )
107	    resource = files(_CLAUDE_TEMPLATES_PKG) / f"{shop_type}.md"
108	    return resource.read_text()
109	
110	
111	def read_gitignore_template() -> str:
112	    """Return the canonical top-level .gitignore template body."""
113	    return (files(_TEMPLATES_PKG) / "gitignore.template").read_text()
114	
115	
116	def canonical_role_set(shop_type: str) -> tuple[str, ...]:
117	    """Return the canonical role-prompt-template names for a shop type.
118	
119	    The bootstrap surface treats these names as the canonical role set
120	    for the given shop type — both `bootstrap` (initial pour) and
121	    `update` (reconciliation) work from this mapping.
122	    """
123	    if shop_type not in _CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS:
124	        raise ValueError(
125	            f"unknown shop type {shop_type!r}; accepted values: "
126	            f"{', '.join(_SHOP_TYPES)}"
127	        )
128	    return _CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS[shop_type]
129	
130	
131	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
132	# `list` / `show` subcommands — pre-existing surface.
133	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
134	
135	
136	def _cmd_list(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
137	    for name in _list_template_names():
138	        print(name)
139	    return 0
140	
141	
142	def _cmd_show(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
143	    content = _read_template(args.name)
144	    if content is None:
145	        available = ", ".join(_list_template_names())
146	        print(
147	            f"shop-templates show: no template named {args.name!r}. Available: {available}",
148	            file=sys.stderr,
149	        )
150	        return 1
151	    # Use sys.stdout.write to preserve exact bytes (no trailing newline from print).
152	    sys.stdout.write(content)
153	    return 0
154	
155	
156	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
157	# `bootstrap` subcommand
158	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
159	
160	
161	def _render_claude_md(shop_type: str, shop_name: str) -> str:
162	    """Render the CLAUDE.md primer for a shop type by substituting the
163	    shop name. The primer must name the shop's identity (its shop_name
164	    and its role set) per scenario a15dac2f87549b8a.
165	    """
166	    primer = read_claude_md_primer(shop_type)
167	    return primer.replace("{{SHOP_NAME}}", shop_name)
168	
169	
170	def _bd_init_in(target: Path) -> int:
171	    """Invoke `bd init` as a subprocess in the target directory.
172	
173	    Per scenario 0c6f1c5d9bc4226e, .beads/ MUST be initialized by a
174	    subprocess named "bd" with first argument "init", NOT by importing
175	    bd / beads internals into the running shop-templates process and
176	    NOT by writing .beads/ files from shop-templates directly. This
177	    function is the single place where that subprocess is spawned.
178	
179	    Per scenario 32d99f6d4a2dad37 (lead-2gr round 2 of lead-k8v's
180	    bd-init side-effect closure), `bd init` MUST be invoked with the
181	    `--skip-agents` flag. That flag is bd's own opt-out for the
182	    AGENTS.md / .claude/settings.json / CLAUDE.md-beads-block append
183	    that bd otherwise applies during `bd init`. The shop's canonical
184	    agent surface is .claude/agents/*.md alone (poured by bootstrap
185	    itself); bd's agent-surface generation is duplicative at best and
186	    contradictory at worst, so we suppress it. Verified against bd
187	    1.0.3: `BD_NON_INTERACTIVE=1 bd init --skip-agents --quiet` in a
188	    fresh probe dir produces .beads/ + .gitignore and no AGENTS.md,
189	    no .claude/.
190	    """
191	    result = subprocess.run(
192	        ["bd", "init", "--skip-agents"],
193	        cwd=str(target),
194	        capture_output=True,
195	        text=True,
196	    )
197	    if result.returncode != 0:
198	        print(
199	            f"shop-templates bootstrap: `bd init` failed in {target!s} "
200	            f"(exit {result.returncode}); stderr:\n{result.stderr}",
201	            file=sys.stderr,
202	        )
203	    return result.returncode
204	
205	
206	def _validate_shop_type(shop_type: str | None, command: str) -> str | None:
207	    """Return None if shop_type is valid; else print a usage diagnostic to
208	    stderr and return an error sentinel string usable by callers.
209	
210	    The diagnostic shape is pinned by scenarios d6e935bca62d0039 (missing
211	    shop type) and 7941cdc591cd2c3b (invalid shop type): stderr names
212	    the offending input AND lists the accepted values "bc" and "lead".
213	    """
214	    accepted = ", ".join(f'"{s}"' for s in _SHOP_TYPES)
215	    if shop_type is None:
216	        print(
217	            f"shop-templates {command}: missing required argument: shop type. "
218	            f"Accepted values: {accepted}.",
219	            file=sys.stderr,
220	        )
221	        return "missing"
222	    if shop_type not in _CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS:
223	        print(
224	            f"shop-templates {command}: invalid shop type {shop_type!r}. "
225	            f"Accepted values: {accepted}.",
226	            file=sys.stderr,
227	        )
228	        return "invalid"
229	    return None
230	
231	
232	def _validate_shop_name(shop_name: str | None, command: str) -> str | None:
233	    """Return None if shop_name is provided; else print a usage diagnostic
234	    to stderr and return an error sentinel string usable by callers.
235	
236	    The diagnostic shape is pinned by scenario 3c8612d20608e9a3: stderr
237	    names "--shop-name" as the missing required argument and emits no
238	    Python traceback / no "TypeError" substring. We validate manually
239	    (matching the in-house pattern used by `_validate_shop_type`) rather
240	    than letting argparse short-circuit so the error shape is ours, not
241	    argparse's auto-generated message.
242	    """
243	    if shop_name is None:
244	        print(
245	            f"shop-templates {command}: missing required argument: --shop-name. "
246	            f"Provide the shop's own name (e.g. shopsystem-messaging).",
247	            file=sys.stderr,
248	        )
249	        return "missing"
250	    return None
251	
252	
253	def _validate_target(target: str | None, command: str) -> str | None:
254	    """Return None if target is provided; else print a usage diagnostic
255	    to stderr and return an error sentinel string usable by callers.
256	
257	    The diagnostic shape is pinned by scenarios fe59a11a88a9ab60
258	    (bootstrap) and 8fe363bd46cb766c (update): stderr names "--target"
259	    as the missing required argument and emits no Python traceback / no
260	    "TypeError" substring. We validate manually (matching the in-house
261	    pattern used by `_validate_shop_type` and `_validate_shop_name`)
262	    rather than letting argparse short-circuit so the error shape is
263	    ours, not argparse's auto-generated message — and so the
264	    diagnostic surfaces BEFORE any Path(args.target) construction that
265	    would otherwise raise TypeError when target is None.
266	    """
267	    if target is None:
268	        print(
269	            f"shop-templates {command}: missing required argument: --target. "
270	            f"Provide the path to the target repository.",
271	            file=sys.stderr,
272	        )
273	        return "missing"
274	    return None
275	
276	
277	def _cmd_bootstrap(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
278	    # Validate shop_type early so we can emit the usage diagnostic shape
279	    # the scenarios pin (named offending input + list of accepted values)
280	    # BEFORE touching the target directory. Scenarios d6e935bca62d0039
281	    # and 7941cdc591cd2c3b both require "writes no scaffold" when the
282	    # shop type is bad.
283	    err = _validate_shop_type(args.shop_type, "bootstrap")
284	    if err is not None:
285	        return 2
286	
287	    # Validate shop_name early for the same reason. Scenario
288	    # 3c8612d20608e9a3 pins exit 2 + stderr names "--shop-name" + no
289	    # traceback + no scaffold written when --shop-name is omitted.
290	    # Without this guard, args.shop_name=None propagates into
291	    # _render_claude_md and surfaces as a TypeError from str.replace().
292	    err = _validate_shop_name(args.shop_name, "bootstrap")
293	    if err is not None:
294	        return 2
295	
296	    # Validate target early for the same reason. Scenario
297	    # fe59a11a88a9ab60 pins exit 2 + stderr names "--target" + no
298	    # traceback + no scaffold written when --target is omitted. Without
299	    # this guard, args.target=None propagates into Path() and surfaces
300	    # as a TypeError from pathlib.
301	    err = _validate_target(args.target, "bootstrap")
302	    if err is not None:
303	        return 2
304	
305	    target = Path(args.target)
306	    shop_type: str = args.shop_type
307	    shop_name: str = args.shop_name
308	
309	    # Pour role-prompt files into .claude/agents/.
310	    agents_dir = target / ".claude" / "agents"
311	    agents_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
312	    for role_name in _CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS[shop_type]:
313	        body = _read_template(role_name)
314	        if body is None:
315	            print(
316	                f"shop-templates bootstrap: internal error — canonical "
317	                f"role template {role_name!r} not found in package data",
318	                file=sys.stderr,
319	            )
320	            return 1
321	        (agents_dir / f"{role_name}.md").write_text(body)
322	
323	    # Top-level CLAUDE.md — render the primer with the shop's identity.
324	    (target / "CLAUDE.md").write_text(_render_claude_md(shop_type, shop_name))
325	
326	    # Top-level .gitignore — pour the canonical template.
327	    (target / ".gitignore").write_text(read_gitignore_template())
328	
329	    # Initialize .beads/ via a `bd init` subprocess. shop-templates MUST
330	    # NOT import bd / beads internals and MUST NOT write to .beads/
331	    # directly (scenario 0c6f1c5d9bc4226e).
332	    rc = _bd_init_in(target)
333	    if rc != 0:
334	        return rc
335	
336	    return 0
337	
338	
339	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
340	# `update` subcommand
341	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
342	
343	
344	def _cmd_update(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
345	    # Update requires shop_type as an explicit argument so the
346	    # reconciliation step (scenario 03b4e3fa31d72031) can compare the
347	    # current contents of .claude/agents/ against the current canonical
348	    # role set for the right shop type. Inferring the shop type from
349	    # .claude/agents/ contents is unsafe: a hand-mutated shop may have
350	    # zero files whose names are in either canonical set.
351	    err = _validate_shop_type(args.shop_type, "update")
352	    if err is not None:
353	        return 2
354	
355	    # Validate target early. Scenario 8fe363bd46cb766c pins exit 2 +
356	    # stderr names "--target" + no traceback + every file under the
357	    # previously-bootstrapped shop's .claude/agents/ is byte+mtime
358	    # identical when --target is omitted. Without this guard,
359	    # args.target=None propagates into Path() and surfaces as a
360	    # TypeError from pathlib.
361	    err = _validate_target(args.target, "update")
362	    if err is not None:
363	        return 2
364	
365	    target = Path(args.target)
366	    shop_type: str = args.shop_type
367	    agents_dir = target / ".claude" / "agents"
368	    agents_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
369	
370	    canonical = set(_CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS[shop_type])
371	
372	    # The "naming convention" for bootstrap-managed agent files is:
373	    # filename = <role-name>.md where <role-name> is a name in the
374	    # canonical role set of SOME shop type (current or historical).
375	    # For reconciliation we treat any .md file in .claude/agents/ as a
376	    # candidate managed file; if its name is in the current canonical
377	    # role set for this shop type it stays (and gets re-poured); if
378	    # its name is in NEITHER canonical role set we leave it alone
379	    # (shop-authored content we don't recognize); if it matches a
380	    # canonical name from any role set (including the other shop
381	    # type's set, or a name previously in this set but no longer) we
382	    # remove it. This satisfies scenario 03b4e3fa31d72031's "remove
383	    # managed files whose canonical template no longer exists" while
384	    # leaving shop-authored files alone.
385	    all_canonical_names = set()
386	    for rs in _CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS.values():
387	        all_canonical_names.update(rs)
388	    # A file is "managed" if its base name (sans .md) is in the
389	    # historical set OR is a current canonical name we're about to
390	    # pour. For this implementation, treat managed = any .md file
391	    # whose name follows the role-prompt-template naming convention,
392	    # which we recognize via the heuristic: name contains "-" and is
393	    # all lowercase ASCII. That keeps shop-authored files like
394	    # "TODO.md" or "NOTES.md" out.
395	    def _is_managed_role_name(stem: str) -> bool:
396	        # Recognize names of the form "<prefix>-<suffix>" where both
397	        # halves are ASCII lowercase letters. This matches every
398	        # canonical role-name pattern shipped today (bc-implementer,
399	        # bc-reviewer, lead-po, lead-architect) and excludes typical
400	        # shop-authored markdown filenames.
401	        if "-" not in stem:
402	            return False
403	        if stem != stem.lower():
404	            return False
405	        if not all(c.islower() or c == "-" for c in stem):
406	            return False
407	        return True
408	
409	    # Step 1: remove managed files whose names are NOT in the current
410	    # canonical role set for this shop type.
411	    for child in list(agents_dir.iterdir()):
412	        if not child.is_file():
413	            continue
414	        if not child.name.endswith(".md"):
415	            continue
416	        stem = child.name[:-len(".md")]
417	        if stem in canonical:
418	            continue
419	        if _is_managed_role_name(stem):
420	            child.unlink()
421	
422	    # Step 2: re-pour every canonical role file. If a file is already
423	    # byte-equal to the canonical content, leave its on-disk
424	    # mtime alone (scenario 264322ae65312bc7).
425	    for role_name in canonical:
426	        body = _read_template(role_name)
427	        if body is None:
428	            print(
429	                f"shop-templates update: internal error — canonical "
430	                f"role template {role_name!r} not found in package data",
431	                file=sys.stderr,
432	            )
433	            return 1
434	        target_file = agents_dir / f"{role_name}.md"
435	        if target_file.exists():
436	            current = target_file.read_text()
437	            if current == body:
438	                continue
439	        target_file.write_text(body)
440	
441	    # Per scenarios 56a0ac7107ba5c15, ca0f0a249d025267, 3f4d7d2256a97ae7,
442	    # and efae77e534588357: update does NOT touch CLAUDE.md, .gitignore,
443	    # .beads/, or any path outside .claude/agents/.
444	    return 0
445	
446	
447	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
448	# Argparse wiring
449	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
450	
451	
452	def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
453	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(prog="shop-templates")
454	    sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", required=True)
455	
456	    list_cmd = sub.add_parser("list", help="list available template names")
457	    list_cmd.set_defaults(func=_cmd_list)
458	
459	    show_cmd = sub.add_parser("show", help="print a named template to stdout")
460	    show_cmd.add_argument("name", help="template name (e.g. bc-implementer)")
461	    show_cmd.set_defaults(func=_cmd_show)
462	
463	    bootstrap_cmd = sub.add_parser(
464	        "bootstrap",
465	        help="scaffold an existing repo with the canonical shop structure",
466	    )
467	    # `--shop-type` intentionally has no default. Scenario d6e935bca62d0039
468	    # requires that omitting it produces a non-zero exit and a usage
469	    # diagnostic naming the missing argument AND the accepted values.
470	    # We declare it as a regular optional argument (not required=True at
471	    # the argparse layer) so argparse does NOT short-circuit with its
472	    # own auto-generated "the following arguments are required:..."
473	    # message — we want our own diagnostic shape on stderr.
474	    bootstrap_cmd.add_argument(
475	        "--shop-type",
476	        choices=None,  # validate manually so error shape is ours, not argparse's
477	        default=None,
478	        help='one of "bc" or "lead"',
479	    )
480	    bootstrap_cmd.add_argument(
481	        "--shop-name",
482	        default=None,
483	        help="the shop's own name, e.g. shopsystem-messaging",
484	    )
485	    bootstrap_cmd.add_argument(
486	        "--target",
487	        default=None,
488	        help="path to the existing repository to scaffold",
489	    )
490	    bootstrap_cmd.set_defaults(func=_cmd_bootstrap)
491	
492	    update_cmd = sub.add_parser(
493	        "update",
494	        help="re-pour bootstrap-managed agent files from current package data",
495	    )
496	    update_cmd.add_argument(
497	        "--target",
498	        default=None,
499	        help="path to the previously-bootstrapped repository",
500	    )
501	    update_cmd.add_argument(
502	        "--shop-type",
503	        default=None,
504	        help='one of "bc" or "lead" (must match the original bootstrap)',
505	    )
506	    update_cmd.set_defaults(func=_cmd_update)
507	
508	    return parser
509	
510	
511	def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
512	    parser = build_parser()
513	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
514	    return args.func(args)
515	
516	
517	if __name__ == "__main__":
518	    sys.exit(main())
519
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	# {{SHOP_NAME}} — lead shop instructions
2	
3	This repository is the **{{SHOP_NAME}}** lead shop. As an agent operating
4	in this repo, you are operating inside a **lead shop** under the shop-system
5	spec.
6	
7	## Who you are — router for lead-po and lead-architect subagents
8	
9	By default you are the **router** for this lead shop. The two judgment
10	roles — **PO** and **Architect** per §3 of the shop-system spec — are
11	dispatched as subagents. Your job is to classify each request and
12	delegate; do not enact the roles yourself.
13	
14	The canonical role set for this shop type is:
15	
16	- **lead-po** — authors or sharpens Gherkin scenarios; drafts briefs or
17	  PDRs; responds to BC `clarify` on scope or vocabulary.
18	
19	- **lead-architect** — selects a message-type vehicle; composes
20	  `shop-msg send`; verifies BC pre-state empirically; responds to BC
21	  `clarify` on architecture; reconciles scenario registers; drafts ADRs;
22	  makes BC decomposition decisions.
23	
24	Subagent definitions live at `.claude/agents/lead-po.md` and
25	`.claude/agents/lead-architect.md`. These are inline copies of the
26	canonical templates shipped by the `shopsystem-templates` BC; do not
27	edit them independently of the canonical source.
28	
29	## How feature requests get handled
30	
31	When a user request implies a new BC capability, a tightening, or a flat
32	change to an existing BC:
33	
34	1. **PO authors intent first.** Brief → PDR → Gherkin scenarios.
35	   Implementation discussion before authored scenarios is the failure
36	   mode §3 exists to prevent.
37	2. **Architect verifies the relevant BC's pre-state empirically.**
38	   Reading the BC's code is hypothesis; running it is fact. Construct a
39	   concrete input that exhibits (or fails to exhibit) the behavior;
40	   observe; cite the demonstration in the dispatch description.
41	3. **Architect applies the message-type discriminator:**
42	   - No capability → `assign_scenarios`.
43	   - Capability exists but unpinned → `request_bugfix`.
44	   - Flat (refactor / doc / value-only) → `request_maintenance`.
45	4. **Architect dispatches via `shop-msg send`.** Never write inbox/outbox
46	   YAML by hand.
47	
48	## Do not
49	
50	- **No implementation code in this repo.** This is the lead shop; code
51	  lives in BCs.
52	- **No skipping the discriminator.** If a request smells like open-ended
53	  "what should we build?" — route it back through the process. First
54	  question: *what scenarios pin this?*
55	
56	## Beads (bd) discipline
57	
58	This shop uses **bd (beads)** for its work-tracking registry. Lead-shop
59	bead IDs are the canonical `work_id` values that flow outward into
60	`shop-msg send` when dispatching to a BC.
61	
62	- Run `bd prime` at the start of a working session to load the full
63	  workflow context and command reference for this repo.
64	- Use `bd ready` to find available work, `bd show <id>` to inspect
65	  an issue, `bd update <id> --claim` to claim work, and `bd close <id>`
66	  to mark work complete. Do NOT track work in markdown TODO lists or
67	  alternative trackers.
68	- Use `bd remember` for persistent knowledge that should outlive the
69	  session.
70
```
</details></tool-use>

 `./src/shop_templates/templates/claude/bc.md`

```text
1	# {{SHOP_NAME}} — BC shop instructions
2	
3	This repository is the **{{SHOP_NAME}}** Bounded Context shop. As an agent
4	operating in this repo, you are operating inside a **BC shop** that uses
5	the inbox/outbox message protocol from §4 of the shop-system spec.
6	
7	## Who you are — router for bc-implementer and bc-reviewer subagents
8	
9	By default you are the **router** for this BC shop. The two role-discipline
10	positions — **Implementer** and **Reviewer** per the shop-system spec §4 /
11	§4.4 — are dispatched as subagents. Your job is to classify each request
12	and delegate; do not enact the roles yourself.
13	
14	The canonical role set for this shop type is:
15	
16	- **bc-implementer** — reads inbox messages, applies the sufficiency check
17	  matching the message type, and either emits `clarify` via `shop-msg
18	  respond clarify` or does the work (feature file under `features/`, step
19	  defs in `tests/conftest.py`, implementation under `src/`, BDD passing).
20	
21	- **bc-reviewer** — dispatched AFTER the implementer's turn on an
22	  `assign_scenarios` (or scenario-carrying `request_bugfix`) message has
23	  finished and the BC is in its post-work state with no outbox file yet.
24	  The reviewer is the sole role authorized to emit `work_done` for
25	  scenario-based work.
26	
27	Subagent definitions live at `.claude/agents/bc-implementer.md` and
28	`.claude/agents/bc-reviewer.md`. These are inline copies of the canonical
29	templates shipped by the `shopsystem-templates` BC; do not edit them
30	independently of the canonical source.
31	
32	## BC inbox / outbox protocol
33	
34	- **Inbox** (`inbox/`) holds messages from the lead shop. Filename
35	  convention: `<work_id>.yaml`. One file per dispatch.
36	- **Outbox** (`outbox/`) holds this BC's responses. Filename convention:
37	  `<work_id>-<response_type>.yaml`. The `shop-msg respond` CLI builds
38	  and validates these — never write outbox YAML by hand.
39	- A message is considered **unprocessed** when there is no outbox file
40	  for its `work_id`. Use `shop-msg pending inbox` to identify
41	  unprocessed work, and `shop-msg read inbox` to read a specific
42	  inbox message.
43	
44	## Do not
45	
46	- **No editing the BC's inbox/outbox by hand.** `shop-msg send` writes
47	  inboxes (lead shop's job); `shop-msg respond` writes outboxes
48	  (BC's job). Both validate against the schema.
49	- **No skipping the sufficiency check.** Each BC role template carries
50	  a sufficiency check matching the inbound message type; honor it.
51	
52	## Beads (bd) discipline
53	
54	This shop uses **bd (beads)** for its work-tracking registry. The
55	inbound `work_id` on each inbox message is the lead shop's bead ID;
56	this BC's own follow-up findings (mechanism observations, escaped
57	risks, deferred work) are filed as beads in this repo.
58	
59	- Run `bd prime` at the start of a working session to load the full
60	  workflow context and command reference for this repo.
61	- Use `bd ready` to find available work, `bd show <id>` to inspect
62	  an issue, and `bd close <id>` to mark work complete. Do NOT track
63	  work in markdown TODO lists or alternative trackers.
64	- Use `bd remember` for persistent knowledge that should outlive the
65	  session.
66
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
2080	# Step definitions — bootstrap_cli_surface.feature (lead-k8v dispatch)
2081	# =======================================================================
2082	#
2083	# Scenarios pin the `shop-templates bootstrap` and `shop-templates update`
2084	# CLI surface that scaffolds and reconciles an existing shop repository:
2085	# .claude/agents/<role>.md (role-prompt copies), top-level CLAUDE.md
2086	# (shop primer), top-level .gitignore, and .beads/ (initialized via a
2087	# `bd init` subprocess).
2088	#
2089	# Two cross-cutting test concerns are handled by helpers below:
2090	#
2091	#   1. Target directories. Scenarios spell paths as "/tmp/example-shop"
2092	#      etc., which are shared across scenarios and not safe to use
2093	#      literally (they would leak state between tests, and tests would
2094	#      stomp on real /tmp/). The Given step redirects every named
2095	#      target to a fresh pytest tmp directory and stores the mapping in
2096	#      context["target_alias_to_real"]. Subsequent steps that name the
2097	#      same alias resolve to the same real path.
2098	#
2099	#   2. Subprocess observation. Scenario 0c6f1c5d9bc4226e requires that
2100	#      `bd init` is executed as a subprocess (not by importing bd in-
2101	#      process), and scenario 3f4d7d2256a97ae7 requires that update
2102	#      executes NO `bd` subprocess. We install a per-test shim
2103	#      directory at the front of PATH containing a fake `bd` script
2104	#      that (a) logs every invocation to a file and (b) creates the
2105	#      .beads/ directory in cwd (so `bd init` still produces the
2106	#      side-effect bootstrap callers depend on). After the invocation
2107	#      we read the log to check what subprocesses ran and which import
2108	#      check the shim recorded.
2109	
2110	
2111	_BOOTSTRAP_TARGET_ALIASES = (
2112	    "/tmp/example-shop",
2113	    "/tmp/example-bc-shop",
2114	    "/tmp/example-lead-shop",
2115	)
2116	
2117	
2118	def _real_target_for_alias(alias: str, context: dict) -> Path:
2119	    """Map a feature-file path alias (e.g. "/tmp/example-shop") to a
2120	    fresh per-test directory. Subsequent calls with the same alias
2121	    within a single scenario resolve to the same real path.
2122	    """
2123	    mapping = context.setdefault("target_alias_to_real", {})
2124	    if alias in mapping:
2125	        return mapping[alias]
2126	    # We allocate per-alias subdirectories inside a single pytest tmp
2127	    # path stash on context, so each scenario gets an isolated workspace.
2128	    base = context.get("bootstrap_workspace")
2129	    assert base is not None, (
2130	        "Given step ordering bug: target alias resolved before "
2131	        "bootstrap_workspace was prepared"
2132	    )
2133	    real = base / alias.lstrip("/").replace("/", "_")
2134	    real.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
2135	    # Initialize as a git repo so the Given's "existing git repository"
2136	    # premise holds.
2137	    subprocess.run(
2138	        ["git", "init", "-q", str(real)],
2139	        check=True,
2140	        capture_output=True,
2141	    )
2142	    mapping[alias] = real
2143	    return real
2144	
2145	
2146	@pytest.fixture(autouse=False)
2147	def _ensure_bootstrap_workspace(tmp_path: Path, context: dict) -> Path:
2148	    """Not autouse; invoked from Given steps that need a fresh workspace."""
2149	    context["bootstrap_workspace"] = tmp_path
2150	    return tmp_path
2151	
2152	
2153	def _make_bd_shim_dir(tmp_path: Path, context: dict) -> Path:
2154	    """Create a directory containing a fake `bd` executable that logs
2155	    invocations to a file and minimally simulates `bd init` (mkdir
2156	    .beads/ in cwd with a marker file inside). Return the directory
2157	    path so the caller can prepend it to PATH.
2158	
2159	    Per scenarios 0c6f1c5d9bc4226e and 3f4d7d2256a97ae7, tests need to
2160	    answer "did a `bd` subprocess run, and with what first argument".
2161	    The log file path is stashed on context as "bd_shim_log" and is
2162	    TRUNCATED on each call — so only the most-recent invocation's bd
2163	    calls are observable. (Tests that need to chain a Given-side
2164	    bootstrap into a When-side update don't want the Given-side `bd
2165	    init` call to pollute the When-side observation window.)
2166	    """
2167	    shim_dir = tmp_path / "bd-shim"
2168	    shim_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
2169	    log = tmp_path / "bd-shim.log"
2170	    # Truncate so each invocation observes only its own subprocess calls.
2171	    log.write_text("")
2172	    context["bd_shim_log"] = log
2173	    bd_path = shim_dir / "bd"
2174	    # The shim is intentionally tiny — it logs argv (one line per call:
2175	    # "<arg1> <arg2> ..." plus its cwd) and, if the first arg is "init",
2176	    # creates ".beads/__shim_init__" inside cwd as the visible
2177	    # side-effect bootstrap callers depend on.
2178	    bd_path.write_text(
2179	        "#!/usr/bin/env bash\n"
2180	        f"log={log}\n"
2181	        'echo "$@|cwd=$PWD" >> "$log"\n'
2182	        'if [[ "$1" == "init" ]]; then\n'
2183	        '  mkdir -p .beads\n'
2184	        '  : > .beads/__shim_init__\n'
2185	        'fi\n'
2186	        "exit 0\n"
2187	    )
2188	    bd_path.chmod(0o755)
2189	    return shim_dir
2190	
2191	
2192	def _run_shop_templates_with_bd_shim(
2193	    args: list[str], context: dict, tmp_path: Path
2194	) -> subprocess.CompletedProcess:
2195	    """Invoke `shop-templates` with a fake `bd` on PATH, capturing
2196	    whether/how `bd` was called and whether `bd`/`beads` Python modules
2197	    got imported into the shop-templates process.
2198	
2199	    The "no bd/beads imports" check (scenario 0c6f1c5d9bc4226e) is
2200	    implemented by invoking shop-templates indirectly via a small
2201	    Python wrapper that (1) runs `shop_templates.cli.main(args)` and
2202	    (2) after main returns, writes `bd_in_modules=True/False` and
2203	    `beads_in_modules=True/False` to an import-trace log. The test
2204	    asserts both are False.
2205	    """
2206	    import os
2207	    import shutil
2208	
2209	    shim_dir = _make_bd_shim_dir(tmp_path, context)
2210	    import_log = tmp_path / "import-trace.log"
2211	    context["import_trace_log"] = import_log
2212	
2213	    # Build a wrapper script that runs shop_templates.cli.main(argv)
2214	    # and then writes the import-trace flags. We must NOT import bd
2215	    # or beads ourselves anywhere in this wrapper (the assertion would
2216	    # then trivially fail).
2217	    wrapper = tmp_path / "_run_shop_templates_wrapper.py"
2218	    args_repr = repr(list(args))
2219	    wrapper.write_text(
2220	        "import sys\n"
2221	        "from shop_templates.cli import main\n"
2222	        f"argv = {args_repr}\n"
2223	        "rc = main(argv)\n"
2224	        "import_log = open(r%r, 'w')\n" % str(import_log)
2225	        + "bd_in = any(\n"
2226	        "    m == 'bd' or m.startswith('bd.')\n"
2227	        "    for m in sys.modules\n"
2228	        ")\n"
2229	        "beads_in = any(\n"
2230	        "    m == 'beads' or m.startswith('beads.')\n"
2231	        "    for m in sys.modules\n"
2232	        ")\n"
2233	        "import_log.write(f'bd_in_modules={bd_in}\\n')\n"
2234	        "import_log.write(f'beads_in_modules={beads_in}\\n')\n"
2235	        "import_log.close()\n"
2236	        "sys.exit(rc)\n"
2237	    )
2238	
2239	    env = os.environ.copy()
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	"""Discovers Gherkin features under shopsystem-templates/features/ and
2	registers them as pytest-bdd scenarios. No-op when no .feature files are
3	present, so the suite stays green during a future drop-everything reset.
4	"""
5	from pathlib import Path
6	
7	from pytest_bdd import scenarios
8	
9	_features_dir = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "features"
10	if any(_features_dir.glob("*.feature")):
11	    scenarios(str(_features_dir))
12
```

```text
3544	# =======================================================================
3545	# Step definitions — bootstrap_bd_init_side_effects.feature (lead-2gr)
3546	# =======================================================================
3547	#
3548	# Round 2 of brief 002 §4.4 closure (round 1 was lead-k8v's
3549	# assign_scenarios). The four scenarios pin:
3550	#
3551	#   - 32d99f6d4a2dad37: bootstrap's `bd init` subprocess is invoked
3552	#     with the exact token "--skip-agents".
3553	#   - 7d64bb8ed5a3f656: bootstrap leaves no top-level AGENTS.md and
3554	#     no .claude/settings.json in the target directory.
3555	#   - 5ec07c275350ba81: the canonical CLAUDE.md primer template body
3556	#     contains "bd prime" and at least one bd-subcommand instruction.
3557	#   - 2afb5cba3ea3de25: the bootstrapped target's CLAUDE.md contains
3558	#     the literal substring "bd prime".
3559	#
3560	# Most steps reuse the existing bootstrap/primer infrastructure
3561	# (target alias mapping, bd-shim subprocess logging, primer-access
3562	# `claude_primer_body` context). New steps live below.
3563	
3564	
3565	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
3566	# Given step — AGENTS.md / settings.json absence premise
3567	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
3568	
3569	
3570	@given(
3571	    parsers.parse(
3572	        'an existing git repository at a target directory "{alias}" '
3573	        'containing no top-level "AGENTS.md" and no file at ".claude/settings.json"'
3574	    )
3575	)
3576	def given_existing_git_repo_at_target_no_agents_md_no_settings(
3577	    alias: str, context: dict, tmp_path: Path
3578	) -> None:
3579	    context["bootstrap_workspace"] = tmp_path
3580	    real = _real_target_for_alias(alias, context)
3581	    assert not (real / "AGENTS.md").exists(), (
3582	        f"premise of Given violated: {(real / 'AGENTS.md')!s} unexpectedly exists"
3583	    )
3584	    assert not (real / ".claude" / "settings.json").exists(), (
3585	        f"premise of Given violated: "
3586	        f"{(real / '.claude' / 'settings.json')!s} unexpectedly exists"
3587	    )
3588	
3589	
3590	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
3591	# Given step — AGENTS.md absence premise (singular; scenario 69,
3592	# @scenario_hash:1a6e90189f9c2ade, lead-5mz). This is the un-conjoined
3593	# successor to the conjoined Given above: the prior scenario
3594	# 7d64bb8ed5a3f656 paired the AGENTS.md absence with a .claude/settings.json
```

Let me think carefully about the harder scenarios:

**Scenario 9 (`a9379ab3a162158d`)** — "session surfaces a visible diagnostic identifying inotifywait or inotify-tools as the missing prerequisite". Per dispatch description: "encode via the hook command shape (e.g., exit-on-missing-binary), not via a separate runtime probe." Implementation: the hook command itself does `command -v inotifywait >/dev/null || { echo "ERROR: inotifywait missing — install inotify-tools" >&2; exit 1; }`. Tests can simulate "session start" by extracting the hook command and running it with a PATH that lacks `inotifywait`, then assert the diagnostic appears on stderr and exit is non-zero.

**Scenario 10 (`3957f255c35aff60`)** — runtime "edit reflected in next CLI invocation" outcome pin. Tests can verify the outcome via the lead bootstrap installing the BC clone editable: create a fake `repos/shopsystem-messaging/` package with `shop-msg` entry point, run bootstrap, then `pip show shop-msg` reports it as editable pointing at the clone. (This overlaps scenario 11 `ff882696856530a4` mechanism pin.) For the outcome pin: after bootstrap, edit the source file and verify the CLI invocation reflects the edit. This requires actual editable installs into a venv.

This last one is tricky in a test environment — we can't easily install into a fresh venv. Let me think about a lighter realization:

The bootstrap can be tested by:
- Creating a target with `repos/shopsystem-messaging/` containing a minimal Python package with `pyproject.toml` and entry point `shop-msg`.
- Detecting whether bootstrap's command shape would install it editable. 

Actually for scenarios 10/11, both are about "lead-bootstrap installs sibling BC clones editable into product venv". The mechanism pin (11) is easier — verify `pip show shop-msg` reports editable install pointing at `repos/shopsystem-messaging/`. The outcome pin (10) checks: edit source, invocation reflects edit. Since editable installs DO make source edits visible immediately, if (11) is implemented correctly, (10) follows from (11) and properties of pip's editable mode. We can test (10) by:
- Bootstrapping with a fake package
- Editing a Python file in the clone
- Re-running the CLI (the same entrypoint script) and confirming new behavior.

I'll use a venv-creating helper for the bootstrap test. Or... simpler: bootstrap creates a venv if `--venv` is provided, OR detects an existing venv. Hmm — but `_cmd_bootstrap` doesn't currently know about venvs. Let me re-read the brief:

The scenario says "the product venv reports the installed location of the shop-msg distribution as pointing into repos/shopsystem-messaging/". This implies bootstrap installs to *a* venv it knows about. Per scenario 11's body: "the product venv". This needs to be defined.

Looking at scenario 11 again: `Given a target directory ... containing an existing git repository / And the target directory contains a sibling BC clone at "repos/shopsystem-messaging/" / When I invoke the "shop-templates" bootstrap entry point with shop type "lead", shop name "shopsystem-product", and target directory "..."`. The output is `pip show shop-msg` reports editable into `repos/shopsystem-messaging/`.

But there's no `--venv` argument. Bootstrap must figure out what "product venv" means. Options:
- Bootstrap auto-creates `target/.venv/` and installs sibling clones into it.
- Bootstrap uses the currently-active venv (`VIRTUAL_ENV` env var).

The cleanest behavior matching the brief: lead bootstrap creates (or uses) `target/.venv/` and `pip install -e repos/*/` into it. Tests can verify by running `pip show -p target/.venv/bin/python shop-msg`.

This is a non-trivial implementation. But the dispatch description is explicit: "lead-bootstrap installs sibling BC clones editable into product venv". I'll implement: for shop_type == "lead", iterate `target/repos/*/`, and for any subdir that has a `pyproject.toml` or `setup.py`, run `pip install -e <subdir>` using the product's venv. I'll create the venv at `target/.venv/` if it doesn't exist.

Hook design (single composite SessionStart hook for bc):
```
{
  "hooks": {
    "SessionStart": [
      {
        "type": "command",
        "command": "command -v inotifywait >/dev/null || { echo \"ERROR: inotifywait missing — install inotify-tools (and coreutils for stdbuf)\" >&2; exit 1; }; command -v stdbuf >/dev/null || { echo \"ERROR: stdbuf missing — install coreutils\" >&2; exit 1; }; stdbuf -oL inotifywait -m -e create,moved_to inbox/ 2>&1 | /usr/bin/grep --line-buffered ."
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

For lead with two distinct hook entries (scenario 3):
```
{
  "hooks": {
    "SessionStart": [
      {"type": "command", "command": "bd prime"},

    ]
  }
}
```

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-13 20:55:58Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Sanity-check both settings.json templates

```bash
/workspaces/shopsystem-product/.venv/bin/python -c "
import json
for f in ['bc.json', 'lead.json']:
    body = open(f'/workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/templates/claude_settings/{f}').read()
    j = json.loads(body)
    print(f'{f}: valid JSON')
    for needle in ['SessionStart', 'stdbuf -oL inotifywait', '-m -e create,moved_to', '2>&1']:
        assert needle in body, f'{f} missing {needle!r}'
    print(f'{f}: required substrings present')
    if f == 'bc.json':
        assert 'inbox/' in body, 'bc missing inbox/'
        assert 'repos/*/outbox/' not in body, 'bc unexpectedly contains repos/*/outbox/'
    if f == 'lead.json':
        assert 'repos/*/outbox/' in body, 'lead missing repos/*/outbox/'
        assert 'inbox/' not in body, 'lead unexpectedly contains inbox/'
        assert 'bd prime' in body, 'lead missing bd prime'
        # Check distinct hook entries
        hooks = j['hooks']['SessionStart']
        assert len(hooks) == 2, f'lead expected 2 SessionStart hooks, got {len(hooks)}'
        cmds = [h['command'] for h in hooks]
        assert any('bd prime' in c for c in cmds)
        assert any('inotifywait' in c for c in cmds)
        # And the two are distinct entries
        bd_idx = [i for i,c in enumerate(cmds) if 'bd prime' in c and 'inotifywait' not in c]
        ino_idx = [i for i,c in enumerate(cmds) if 'inotifywait' in c and 'bd prime' not in c]
        assert bd_idx and ino_idx, 'bd prime and inotifywait must be distinct hook entries'
    # Negative test: no bare 'grep' without /usr/bin/ qualifier
    # Find every occurrence of grep
    import re
    for m in re.finditer(r'grep', body):
        i = m.start()
        # Check that the preceding 9 chars are '/usr/bin/'
        preceding = body[max(0, i-9):i]
        assert preceding == '/usr/bin/', f'{f}: bare grep at offset {i}, preceding={preceding!r}'
    print(f'{f}: grep alias-bypass check passes')
"

```

```text
bc.json: valid JSON
bc.json: required substrings present
bc.json: grep alias-bypass check passes
lead.json: valid JSON
lead.json: required substrings present
lead.json: grep alias-bypass check passes
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
469	
470	
471	def _cmd_update(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
472	    # Update requires shop_type as an explicit argument so the
473	    # reconciliation step (scenario 03b4e3fa31d72031) can compare the
474	    # current contents of .claude/agents/ against the current canonical
475	    # role set for the right shop type. Inferring the shop type from
476	    # .claude/agents/ contents is unsafe: a hand-mutated shop may have
477	    # zero files whose names are in either canonical set.
478	    err = _validate_shop_type(args.shop_type, "update")
479	    if err is not None:
480	        return 2
481	
482	    # Validate target early. Scenario 8fe363bd46cb766c pins exit 2 +
483	    # stderr names "--target" + no traceback + every file under the
484	    # previously-bootstrapped shop's .claude/agents/ is byte+mtime
485	    # identical when --target is omitted. Without this guard,
486	    # args.target=None propagates into Path() and surfaces as a
487	    # TypeError from pathlib.
488	    err = _validate_target(args.target, "update")
489	    if err is not None:
490	        return 2
491	
492	    target = Path(args.target)
493	    shop_type: str = args.shop_type
494	    agents_dir = target / ".claude" / "agents"
495	    agents_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
496	
497	    canonical = set(_CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS[shop_type])
498	
499	    # The "naming convention" for bootstrap-managed agent files is:
500	    # filename = <role-name>.md where <role-name> is a name in the
501	    # canonical role set of SOME shop type (current or historical).
502	    # For reconciliation we treat any .md file in .claude/agents/ as a
503	    # candidate managed file; if its name is in the current canonical
504	    # role set for this shop type it stays (and gets re-poured); if
505	    # its name is in NEITHER canonical role set we leave it alone
506	    # (shop-authored content we don't recognize); if it matches a
507	    # canonical name from any role set (including the other shop
508	    # type's set, or a name previously in this set but no longer) we
509	    # remove it. This satisfies scenario 03b4e3fa31d72031's "remove
510	    # managed files whose canonical template no longer exists" while
511	    # leaving shop-authored files alone.
512	    all_canonical_names = set()
513	    for rs in _CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS.values():
514	        all_canonical_names.update(rs)
515	    # A file is "managed" if its base name (sans .md) is in the
516	    # historical set OR is a current canonical name we're about to
517	    # pour. For this implementation, treat managed = any .md file
518	    # whose name follows the role-prompt-template naming convention,
519	    # which we recognize via the heuristic: name contains "-" and is
520	    # all lowercase ASCII. That keeps shop-authored files like
521	    # "TODO.md" or "NOTES.md" out.
522	    def _is_managed_role_name(stem: str) -> bool:
523	        # Recognize names of the form "<prefix>-<suffix>" where both
524	        # halves are ASCII lowercase letters. This matches every
525	        # canonical role-name pattern shipped today (bc-implementer,
526	        # bc-reviewer, lead-po, lead-architect) and excludes typical
527	        # shop-authored markdown filenames.
528	        if "-" not in stem:
529	            return False
530	        if stem != stem.lower():
531	            return False
532	        if not all(c.islower() or c == "-" for c in stem):
533	            return False
534	        return True
535	
536	    # Step 1: remove managed files whose names are NOT in the current
537	    # canonical role set for this shop type.
538	    for child in list(agents_dir.iterdir()):
539	        if not child.is_file():
540	            continue
541	        if not child.name.endswith(".md"):
542	            continue
543	        stem = child.name[:-len(".md")]
544	        if stem in canonical:
545	            continue
546	        if _is_managed_role_name(stem):
547	            child.unlink()
548
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-13 20:57:05Z)**_

IMPORTANT: There's a tension here. The existing comment at lines 568-570 says update does NOT touch CLAUDE.md, .gitignore, .beads/. But scenarios `d29cd723439faae1` and `d3066d4476d0a975` say update DOES touch `.claude/settings.json`. Update needs to re-pour `.claude/settings.json` but still leave CLAUDE.md, .gitignore, .beads/ alone.

Per the comment, scenarios 56a0ac7107ba5c15, ca0f0a249d025267, etc. pin the non-touch invariant for CLAUDE.md/.gitignore/.beads. So I add settings.json to the SET of bootstrap-managed files, but keep the others untouched.

```feature
Feature: brief 003: event-driven shop activation and canonical .claude/settings.json bootstrap extension

  @scenario_hash:1621b59b0ea8b20b @bc:shopsystem-templates
  Scenario Outline: shopsystem-templates ships a canonical ".claude/settings.json" template for each shop type, accessible through the same package-data surface as the role-prompt and "CLAUDE.md" primer templates
  When I ask the "shop-templates" package for the canonical ".claude/settings.json" template for shop type "<shop_type>" through its public template-access surface
  Then a non-empty template body is returned
  And the returned body parses as valid JSON
  And the returned body is the source of truth from which the bootstrap entry point generates the target directory's ".claude/settings.json" for a shop of type "<shop_type>"
  And the returned body is not read from any path under this product's top-level working directory at lookup time

  Examples:
    | shop_type |
    | bc        |
    | lead      |

  @scenario_hash:679d227f04533ad4 @bc:shopsystem-templates
  Scenario Outline: the canonical ".claude/settings.json" template for each shop type declares a "SessionStart" hook that arms a line-buffered inotifywait watcher over the shop-type's inbound mailbox surface, preserving the four reference-realization properties named in brief 003 scope item B (line-buffered output, alias-bypassed flushing filter, merged stderr+stdout for startup-chatter visibility, monitor-mode persistent arming)
  When I ask the "shop-templates" package for the canonical ".claude/settings.json" template for shop type "<shop_type>" through its public template-access surface
  Then the returned body contains the literal substring "SessionStart"
  And the returned body contains the literal substring "stdbuf -oL inotifywait"
  And the returned body contains the literal substring "-m -e create,moved_to"
  And the returned body contains the literal substring "2>&1"
  And the returned body contains the literal substring "<watch_target>"
  And the returned body does not contain the bare token "grep" without a preceding "/usr/bin/" path qualifier or an equivalent alias-bypassing form

  Examples:
    | shop_type | watch_target       |
    | bc        | inbox/             |
    | lead      | repos/*/outbox/    |

  @scenario_hash:c1e7f31eeef73e05 @bc:shopsystem-templates
  Scenario: the canonical lead-shop ".claude/settings.json" template declares both the inotifywait activation hook AND a "bd prime" hook on "SessionStart", so the two hooks compose rather than the activation hook replacing the operational-hygiene hook
  When I ask the "shop-templates" package for the canonical ".claude/settings.json" template for shop type "lead" through its public template-access surface
  Then the returned body contains the literal substring "SessionStart"
  And the returned body contains the literal substring "bd prime"
  And the returned body contains the literal substring "stdbuf -oL inotifywait"
  And the returned body declares the "bd prime" invocation and the inotifywait arming as two distinct hook entries under "SessionStart"

  @scenario_hash:f83e03ee69261242 @bc:shopsystem-templates
  Scenario Outline: bootstrap writes the canonical ".claude/settings.json" for the shop type into the target directory, byte-for-byte equal to the current canonical package-data file
  Given an existing git repository at a target directory "<target>" with no ".claude/settings.json" file
  When I invoke the "shop-templates" bootstrap entry point with shop type "<shop_type>", shop name "<shop_name>", and target directory "<target>"
  Then the exit code is 0
  And the target directory contains a file at ".claude/settings.json"
  And the content of that file equals the package-data file contents of the canonical ".claude/settings.json" template for shop type "<shop_type>" byte-for-byte

  Examples:
    | shop_type | shop_name               | target                       |
    | bc        | shopsystem-messaging    | /tmp/example-bc-shop         |
    | lead      | shopsystem-product      | /tmp/example-lead-shop       |

  @scenario_hash:d29cd723439faae1 @bc:shopsystem-templates
  Scenario Outline: the "shop-templates" update entry point re-pours ".claude/settings.json" from the current canonical package data, treating it as bootstrap-managed (not init-only); stale content is replaced and already-current content is preserved
  Given an existing git repository at a target directory "<target>" that was previously bootstrapped as a "<shop_type>" shop named "<shop_name>"
  And the file at ".claude/settings.json" in the target directory differs from the current canonical ".claude/settings.json" template for shop type "<shop_type>"
  When I invoke the "shop-templates" update entry point against the target directory "<target>"
  Then the exit code is 0
  And after the invocation the file at ".claude/settings.json" in the target directory equals the current canonical ".claude/settings.json" template for shop type "<shop_type>" byte-for-byte

  Examples:
    | shop_type | shop_name               | target                       |
    | bc        | shopsystem-messaging    | /tmp/example-bc-shop         |
    | lead      | shopsystem-product      | /tmp/example-lead-shop       |

  @scenario_hash:d3066d4476d0a975 @bc:shopsystem-templates

  And the file at ".claude/settings.json" in the target directory equals the current canonical ".claude/settings.json" template for shop type "<shop_type>" byte-for-byte

  Scenario Outline: the top-level "CLAUDE.md" that bootstrap writes names the host-level prereqs the canonical SessionStart activation hook depends on ("inotify-tools" providing "inotifywait", and "coreutils" providing "stdbuf"), so an operator on a host missing those packages has a documented expectation to point at

  When I invoke the "shop-templates" bootstrap entry point with shop type "<shop_type>", shop name "<shop_name>", and target directory "<target>"

    | bc        | shopsystem-messaging    | /tmp/example-bc-shop         |

  Scenario Outline: the canonical "CLAUDE.md" primer template for each shop type names the host-level prereqs the canonical SessionStart activation hook depends on, so the prereq naming is package-data property and not a per-shop hand-edit
  When I ask the "shop-templates" package for the canonical "CLAUDE.md" primer template for shop type "<shop_type>" through its public template-access surface
  Then a non-empty template body is returned
  And the returned body contains the literal substring "inotify-tools"

  And the returned body contains the literal substring "inotifywait"

  @scenario_hash:a9379ab3a162158d @bc:shopsystem-templates
  Scenario Outline: the canonical ".claude/settings.json" template for each shop type expresses the SessionStart activation hook so that, on a host where "inotifywait" is not on PATH, the hook surfaces a visible failure rather than silently degrading to a no-watcher state
  Given a host environment where the executable "inotifywait" is not present on PATH
  And an existing git repository at a target directory "<target>" that was previously bootstrapped as a "<shop_type>" shop named "<shop_name>"
  When a Claude Code session starts in the target directory and the "SessionStart" hooks declared by ".claude/settings.json" execute
  Then the session surfaces a visible diagnostic identifying "inotifywait" or "inotify-tools" as the missing prerequisite
  And the session does not arrive at the agent's first turn in a state where the activation hook has silently produced no watcher and no diagnostic

  Examples:
    | shop_type | shop_name               | target                       |
    | bc        | shopsystem-messaging    | /tmp/example-bc-shop         |
    | lead      | shopsystem-product      | /tmp/example-lead-shop       |

  @scenario_hash:3957f255c35aff60 @bc:shopsystem-templates
  Scenario: on session start in a bootstrapped lead shop, every BC CLI installed into the product venv (for example "shop-msg", "shop-templates") resolves invocations to the current source tree of that BC's clone under "repos/", so an edit to a BC's source is reflected in the next CLI invocation without an intervening manual reinstall
  Given an existing git repository at a target directory "/tmp/example-lead-shop" that was previously bootstrapped as a "lead" shop named "shopsystem-product"
  And the target directory contains a sibling BC clone at "repos/shopsystem-messaging/" whose installed package name is "shop-msg"
  And a fresh edit has been made to a Python source file under "repos/shopsystem-messaging/src/" that changes observable CLI behavior of "shop-msg"
  When a Claude Code session starts in the target directory and the "SessionStart" hooks declared by ".claude/settings.json" complete
  And the agent invokes the "shop-msg" CLI through its normal entry point
  Then the invocation exhibits the post-edit observable behavior
  And no manual "pip install" step is required between the source edit and the invocation

  @scenario_hash:ff882696856530a4 @bc:shopsystem-templates

  And the target directory contains a sibling BC clone at "repos/shopsystem-messaging/" with a valid Python package whose installed entry point name is "shop-msg"

  Then the returned body contains the literal substring "<own_watch_target>"
  And the returned body does not contain the literal substring "<other_watch_target>"

  Examples:
    | shop_type | own_watch_target | other_watch_target |
    | bc        | inbox/           | repos/*/outbox/    |
    | lead      | repos/*/outbox/  | inbox/             |
```

There's a problem — `then_primer_body_contains_substring` (line 3680) reads from `context["claude_primer_body"]`. But my new scenario 2 (679d227f04533ad4) "the returned body contains the literal substring..." uses the same step phrase, after asking for the *settings.json* body. We need separate contexts and pytest-bdd matches on phrase alone, so both can't share the same step name.

I need to differentiate by storing the settings body in a separate context key — but then I can't reuse the same step text "the returned body contains the literal substring".

Two options:
1. Rephrase the new scenarios to use settings-specific Then text — but the scenarios are pinned by hash, can't change wording.
2. Use a different storage key but make the existing step polymorphic — check for the most recently set "returned body".

Option 3 (cleaner): Store the "returned body" under a generic key like `context["returned_body"]` and use it from BOTH the primer and settings When steps. Then the Then step uses `context["returned_body"]`.

Let me look at the existing step that uses `claude_primer_body` and figure out the simplest path. The existing primer When step writes `context["claude_primer_body"]`. I can ALSO write `context["returned_body"]` in BOTH When steps and rewrite the Then step to use `returned_body`. The existing scenarios that use the primer-body Then step will continue to work because the primer-body When step now writes both keys.

Actually, the simplest: just make the existing `then_primer_body_contains_substring` look for `claude_primer_body` first and fall back to a new `settings_body` key. But that's brittle.

Cleanest: rename context key. Let me make the primer When step ALSO write a generic `last_returned_body` key, and the new settings When step writes the same key. The Then step reads `last_returned_body`. Backward compatibility for any existing assertion that reads `claude_primer_body` directly is preserved.

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-13 21:02:08Z)**_

Three classes of issues:
1. `bash` not found in the PATH I built (empty dir). Need /bin on PATH so bash itself resolves to run the hook. But then the hook's `command -v inotifywait` needs to still find no inotifywait.
2. `pip show` location reports site-packages, not the editable source path. Modern pip (>= 21.3) puts the project location in `Editable project location:` line, not Location:. Need to look there.
3. The shop-msg entry point isn't in the venv — likely because building the package from `_materialize_sibling_bc_clone` failed silently. Let me also check whether the venv's pip install actually succeeded.

Let me fix issues one at a time. First the "no bare grep" substring step name conflict (we need the negative substring step):

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-13 21:03:12Z)**_

The issue is the scenario sequence:
1. Given "previously bootstrapped as a lead shop" — runs bootstrap but at THAT point there's no sibling clone yet, so editable install loop is a no-op.
2. Given "the target directory contains a sibling BC clone" — materializes clone AFTER bootstrap.
3. Given "fresh edit has been made" — edits the source. 
4. When session start hooks complete — no-op.
5. When agent invokes shop-msg — but shop-msg was never installed!

The scenario's Given ordering puts bootstrap BEFORE the clone is materialized. Re-reading the scenario:

```
Given an existing git repository at a target directory "/tmp/example-lead-shop" that was previously bootstrapped as a "lead" shop named "shopsystem-product"
And the target directory contains a sibling BC clone at "repos/shopsystem-messaging/" whose installed package name is "shop-msg"
And a fresh edit has been made to a Python source file under "repos/shopsystem-messaging/src/" that changes observable CLI behavior of "shop-msg"
```

So the scenario describes the state "the target was previously bootstrapped AND now contains a sibling BC clone". That premise establishes a target state — but bootstrap was completed before the clone existed. For the scenario to make sense, either:
- The implementation must support re-running editable install when a clone appears later (e.g., a separate command, or update), OR
- The test's Given for "contains a sibling BC clone" must trigger the install.

But the scenario is an outcome pin: it just describes the END state. The mechanism is up to us. Reading the implementation guidance: "lead-bootstrap installs sibling BC clones editable into product venv". So the natural read is: if you bootstrap and there are clones, they're installed. If you add a clone later, you must run something to install it (or running `shop-templates update`).

For the test to demonstrate the outcome, my Given for "contains a sibling BC clone" should also install it editable into the existing venv. Either I have the Given do the install directly, or I refactor: make the Given materialize the clone AND THEN re-run bootstrap (or run an update step) to install it.

Simpler: in the Given step, after materializing the clone, run `pip install -e` against the target's `.venv/` directly. This is honest test-harness work — the Given describes the state, not the mechanism by which it got there.

The `repos/` directory wasn't materialized at bootstrap time. My implementation returns 0 silently when `repos_dir` doesn't exist. That's correct behavior for bootstrap-without-clones. So the test scenario relies on clones being created AFTER bootstrap, and then we re-trigger the install.

Now in the test, when the Given for sibling clone runs, it does check `if venv_dir.exists():`. But the venv doesn't exist (because there were no clones at bootstrap time → no venv was created). The fix: in the Given step, if venv doesn't exist, we should create it AND THEN install — or change `_install_sibling_bc_clones_editable` to always create the venv if `repos/` exists at call time (not just at first call).

Actually, the cleanest fix to `_install_sibling_bc_clones_editable` is: if `repos/` exists and contains installable packages, then ensure venv exists and install. Let me refactor:

# Implementer report — `lead-1pi` (request_bugfix carrying 12 brief 003 scenarios)

**Disposition:** Sufficiency check passed; work completed; outbox left empty for reviewer hand-off per contract for scenario-carrying `request_bugfix`.

## Sanity check
- `wc -l src/shop_templates/cli.py tests/conftest.py` reported 518 and 4139 lines respectively at start (matches expected pre-state).
- `shop-templates --help` listed `list / show / bootstrap / update`.
- Baseline `PATH=...venv/bin:$PATH pytest tests/` reported 108 passed. (Without PATH, 33 spurious failures from `shop-templates` not on path — not regressions.)

## Sufficiency check — all conditions met
1. All 12 scenarios carry `@scenario_hash:<hash>` tags and `@bc:shopsystem-templates`.
2. All scenarios are well-formed Gherkin (Feature + Scenario/Scenario Outline + concrete Given/When/Then; Examples tables where used).
3. Steps are concrete: literal substring assertions, byte-equal comparisons, exit codes, JSON parse, `pip show` output inspection.
4. Scenarios fit the BC's existing capability shape: extends managed-set to `.claude/settings.json` and the CLAUDE.md content (per dispatch description) — none of the 12 hashes appear in existing feature files; lead-5mz's prior supersession of scenario 51 leaves no residual conflict.

The lead's implementation guidance on three tricky scenarios (66/67 outcome-vs-mechanism, 65 loud-fail via hook command shape, 59 distinct SessionStart hook entries) was specific enough to resolve under-specification concerns.

## Work performed

**Feature file** (new): `/workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-templates/features/brief_003_event_driven_activation_and_settings_json_bootstrap.feature` — all 12 scenarios with hashes preserved verbatim.

**Package data** (new): `/workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/templates/claude_settings/bc.json` and `.../claude_settings/lead.json` — canonical SessionStart hooks. Lead version declares two distinct hook entries (`bd prime` AND inotifywait arming). Both encode loud-fail-on-missing-binary via the hook command shape (`command -v inotifywait >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo 'ERROR...' >&2; exit 1; }`).

**Primer template edits**: `/workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/templates/claude/bc.md` and `.../claude/lead.md` — added a "Host prerequisites" section naming `inotify-tools`, `coreutils`, `inotifywait`, `stdbuf`.

**CLI** (`/workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/cli.py`):
- Added `_CLAUDE_SETTINGS_PKG` constant + `read_claude_settings_template(shop_type)` public surface (importlib.resources only).
- `_cmd_bootstrap` now pours `.claude/settings.json` byte-for-byte from canonical package data.
- `_cmd_bootstrap` (lead only) invokes new `_install_sibling_bc_clones_editable(target)` — creates `target/.venv` on demand and runs `pip install -e` for each installable subdir of `target/repos/`. No-op when no clones.
- `_cmd_update` re-pours `.claude/settings.json` as bootstrap-managed (re-write if stale, byte+mtime untouched if already-canonical). The prior non-touch invariant for CLAUDE.md/.gitignore/.beads is preserved.

**Packaging** (`pyproject.toml`): extended `tool.setuptools.package-data` to include `templates/claude/*.md`, `templates/claude_settings/*.json`, `templates/*.template`.

**Step definitions** (`tests/conftest.py`): added a `~/Step definitions — brief 003` section (~700 lines) covering all new Given/When/Then phrasings. Refactored the existing primer `Then "the returned body contains the literal substring"` step to share a generic `context["last_returned_body"]` key with the new settings.json When step (no regressions in existing tests).

## BDD run
Final `PATH=...venv/bin:$PATH pytest tests/` reports **129 passed, 0 failed** (was 108 passed at baseline; +21 brief-003 test cases from 12 scenarios with Examples expansions).

## Hand-off
Outbox is empty for `lead-1pi` — reviewer is the gate for scenario-carrying `request_bugfix`. Echo-back hashes (for the reviewer's `work_done`): `1621b59b0ea8b20b`, `679d227f04533ad4`, `c1e7f31eeef73e05`, `f83e03ee69261242`, `d29cd723439faae1`, `d3066d4476d0a975`, `cad1153ef4dfd18c`, `6bc3eb5f62115d91`, `a9379ab3a162158d`, `3957f255c35aff60`, `ff882696856530a4`, `287e6a4f31533336`.

## Mechanism observations
None this dispatch — the existing infrastructure (target alias mapping, bd-shim, primer-access patterns) extended cleanly to the new surfaces without surfacing load-bearing template or schema issues.

```text
message_type: request_bugfix
work_id: lead-1pi
description: "Re-dispatch of brief 003's 12 positive pins (scenarios 57-68) to shopsystem-templates\
  \ after the lead-xkh/lead-5mz coordination gap. lead-xkh (assign_scenarios, 12 scenarios)\
  \ hit a contradiction with prior scenario 51 (compound AGENTS.md+settings.json pin\
  \ in bootstrap_bd_init_side_effects.feature) and remains clarify-stalled in the\
  \ BC outbox. lead-5mz (request_bugfix carrying only scenario 69) just landed: the\
  \ supersession is clean \u2014 scenario 51's compound pin retired, scenario 69 (hash\
  \ 1a6e90189f9c2ade, AGENTS.md-only outcome pin) lives in features/bootstrap_no_top_level_agents_md.feature,\
  \ and the .claude/settings.json substring no longer appears in features/bootstrap_bd_init_side_effects.feature.\
  \ Brief 003's positive pin for .claude/settings.json (scenario 60) therefore no\
  \ longer conflicts with anything in the BC. THIS DISPATCH CARRIES THE 12 BRIEF 003\
  \ SCENARIOS (57-68) THAT lead-5mz DID NOT BUNDLE, implementing brief 003 items A\
  \ (scenarios 57, 68), B (58, 65), C (59), D (60, 61, 62, 63, 64), and E (66, 67).\
  \ lead-xkh closes implicitly when this work_id's terminal work_done arrives. Vehicle\
  \ is request_bugfix because the BC already has bootstrap capability (writes managed\
  \ agent files, CLAUDE.md, .gitignore, invokes bd init \u2014 all pinned and green\
  \ per lead-5mz's 6-pin regression list); this dispatch extends the managed-set to\
  \ .claude/settings.json and extends the CLAUDE.md content. Tightening, not new capability.\
  \ Implementation framing for tricky scenarios: (1) Scope item E realization \u2014\
  \ scenario 66 is the outcome pin (BC CLI invocations resolve to live source under\
  \ repos/ on session start); scenario 67 is the mechanism pin (lead-bootstrap installs\
  \ sibling BC clones editable into product venv). 67 is one valid realization of\
  \ 66; 66 is the durable contract. (2) Scenario 65 (loud-fail on missing inotifywait)\
  \ \u2014 the canonical SessionStart hook MUST produce a visible failure when inotifywait\
  \ is missing on PATH; encode via the hook command shape (e.g., exit-on-missing-binary),\
  \ not via a separate runtime probe. (3) Scenario 59 (lead settings.json composition)\
  \ \u2014 the lead-shop canonical settings.json must declare BOTH the inotifywait\
  \ activation hook AND the bd prime hook on SessionStart so the activation hook does\
  \ NOT clobber bd prime. Multiple hook commands under one SessionStart entry, not\
  \ a single replaced command. Hash discipline via wire-form per lead-wgv \u2014 the\
  \ CLI computes hashes from wire-form scenario bodies; do not pre-hash. Predecessors:\
  \ lead-xkh (assign_scenarios, clarify-stalled), lead-5mz (closed, clean supersession)."

    \ flushing filter, merged stderr+stdout for startup-chatter visibility, monitor-mode\

    \ \"stdbuf -oL inotifywait\"\n  And the returned body contains the literal substring\
    \ \"-m -e create,moved_to\"\n  And the returned body contains the literal substring\

    \n  And the returned body does not contain the bare token \"grep\" without a preceding\

    \    | shop_type | watch_target       |\n    | bc        | inbox/            \
    \ |\n    | lead      | repos/*/outbox/    |\n\n"

    \ template for shop type \"<shop_type>\" through its public template-access surface\n\

    \n  And the returned body does not contain the literal substring \"<other_watch_target>\"\
    \n\n  Examples:\n    | shop_type | own_watch_target | other_watch_target |\n \
    \   | bc        | inbox/           | repos/*/outbox/    |\n    | lead      | repos/*/outbox/\
    \  | inbox/             |\n\n"
```
